0x1e 0x4b

Packet Command 0x1e 0x4b

Reset Receiver Command

Clears all battery-backed data and performs a software reset to initiate a cold start of the receiver.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 ResetReceiverCmdId ID 1e
0 1 ResetReceiverBatteryClearFlag SubID 4b

0x1f

Packet Request 0x1f

Receiver Firmware Information Req

Requests the firmware information from the receiver. Responds with the 0x45 packet.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 FirmwareInfoReqId ID 1f

0x1f

Packet Request 0x1f

Extended Receiver Firmware Information Req

Requests the firmware information from the receiver. Responds with the 0x45 packet.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1FirmwareInfoReqIdID1f
01MachineIdBYTEnReceiver Machine ID. See product-specific appendix for machine IDs.
11RequestTypeBYTEEnumRequests the receiver configuration or sets the Reset Acknowledgment bit in Report Packet 0x4B

RequestType: Requests the receiver configuration or sets the Reset Acknowledgment bit in Report Packet 0x4B
ValueDescription
0Request Receiver configuration
1Set Reset Acknowledge bit in Report Packet 0x4B

0x20

Packet Request 0x20

Almanac Request

Requests almanac data for one satellite from the receiver
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AlmanacReqId ID 20 Almanac Request
0 1 SatNumber BYTE n 0 = All Satellites. 1-32 = specific PRN number

0x21

Packet Request 0x21

GPS Time Request

Requests current GPS time from the receiver. Report packet 0x41 is sent in response.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 GPSTimeReqId ID 21

0x22

Packet Command 0x22

Position Fix Mode Command

Configures the receiver to operate in a specific position fix mode and stores the new mode setting in battery-backed memory.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1PosFixModeIdID22
01PositionFixModeBYTEEnumPosition Fix Mode

PositionFixMode: Position Fix Mode
ValueDescription
0Automatic (2D/3D) (default)
1Time only (0D)
22D clock hold (not all receivers support this mode)
3Horizontal (2D)
4Full position (3D)
5Auto 2D to 2D clock hold (not all receivers support this mode)
6Auto 3D to 2D to 2D clock hold (not all receivers support this mode)
10Overdetermined clock mode(not all receivers support this mode)

0x25

Packet Command 0x25

Soft Reset/ Self Test Command

Initiates a software reset for the receiver, causing the receiver to perform the equivalent of powering off and then on. The receiver performs a self-test during the reset routine. Command Packet 0x25 contains no data bytes.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 SoftResetId ID 25

0x26

Packet Request 0x26

Health Request

Requests health and status information from the receiver. Report packets 0x46 and 0x4B are sent in response.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 HealthId ID 26

0x27

Packet Request 0x27

Signal Levels Request

Requests signal levels for all satellites currently being tracked. Report packet 0x47 is sent in response.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 SignalLevelsReqId ID 27

0x2c

Packet Request 0x2c

Operating Parameters Request

Request the current operating parameter values of the receiver, which responds with Report Packet 0x4C. The receiver stores the operating parameters in battery-backed memory.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 OperatingParametersId ID 2c

0x2c

Packet Command 0x2c

Operating Parameters Command

Sets the operating parameter values of a receiver or requests the current parameter values, and the receiver responds by sending the parameter values in Report Packet 0x4C. The receiver stores the operating parameters in battery-backed memory.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1OperatingParametersIdID2c
01DynamicsCodeBYTEEnumSets expected vehicle dynamics (velocity), used when computing the initial position
14ElevationMaskSINGLEradiansSets lowest acceptable elevation angle for satellites included in position solutions
54SignalLevelMaskSINGLE-Sets minimum signal level for satellites included in position solutions.
94PDOPMaskSINGLE-Sets maximum PDOP for satellites included in position solutions
134PDOPSwitchSINGLE-Sets transition between 2D and 3D mode when the receiver is operating in Auto 2D/3D position fix mode. A 3D position fix is computed when 4 or more satellites are available and the resulting PDOP is less than the PDOP Switch value

DynamicsCode: Sets expected vehicle dynamics (velocity), used when computing the initial position
ValueDescription
0Current value left unchanged
1Land Mode (< 2G)
2Sea Mode (< 1G)
3Air Mode (< 4G)
4Stationary

0x33

Packet Request 0x33

Request A-to-D Readings

Requests analog to digital readings from receiver
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AtoDReadings ID 33

0x35

Packet Request 0x35

I/O Option Flags Request

Requests the current I/O option flags. The receiver responds by sending Report Packet 0x55.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 OptionFlagsId ID 35 I/O Option Flags

0x35

Packet Command 0x35

I/O Option Flags Command

Sets the current I/O option flags. The receiver records the I/O option flags settings in battery-backed memory and sends Report Packet 0x55 in response.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1OptionFlagsIdID35I/O Option Flags
01PosFlagsBYTEBitsetSets options for position and altitude-related data
11VelocityFlagsBYTEBitsetSets options for velocity-related data
21TimingFlagsBYTEBitsetSets options for time-related data
31AuxFlagsBYTEBitsetSets miscellaneous options

PosFlags: Sets options for position and altitude-related data
BitDescription

VelocityFlags: Sets options for velocity-related data
BitDescription

TimingFlags: Sets options for time-related data
BitDescription

AuxFlags: Sets miscellaneous options
BitDescription

0x38

Packet Command 0x38

Download and Upload Satellite System Data

Command Packet 0x38 downloads satellite data from one receiver, and uploads the data to another receiver. The receiver acknowledges a download operation by sending the requested data in Report Packet 0x58. The process of downloading satellite data from one receiver and uploading it to another decreases the amount of time required for the receiver to initialize from a cold start (battery-backed memory cleared). Note that the receiver can initialize itself without uploading data, it merely takes longer. To download data from one receiver, use only bytes 0–2. To upload the data to another receiver, use all bytes.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1DownloadSatelliteSystemDataIdID38
01OperationBYTEEnumDetermines whether the data is uploaded to the receiver or downloaded from the receiver
11DataTypeBYTEEnumRequested data type
21SVPRNBYTEnSelects an individual satellite (or all satellites, if applicable)

Operation: Determines whether the data is uploaded to the receiver or downloaded from the receiver
ValueDescription
1Download data from the receiver
2Uploads data into receiver, if the receiver supports satellite data uploads
3Clears the current receiver's data, if the receiver supports the clearing function

DataType: Requested data type
ValueDescription
1Unused data type
2Almnanac Data
3Health page, T_oa and W_oa
4Ionosphere data
5UTC data
6Ephemeris Data

0x3b

Packet Request 0x3b

Satellite Ephemeris Status Request

Command Packet 0x3B requests the current status of satellite ephemeris data. The receiver acknowledges with Report Packet 0x5B when data is available.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 SatTrackingReqId ID 3b Satellite ephemeris status request
0 1 SatNumber BYTE n 0 = All Satellites. 1-32 = specific PRN number

0x3c

Packet Request 0x3c

Satellite Tracking Status Request

Requests the current satellite tracking status. The receiver acknowledges with Report Packet 0x5C when data is available.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 SatTrackingReqId ID 3c Satellite tracking status request
0 1 SatNumber BYTE n 0 = All Satellites. 1-32 = specific PRN number

0 1 TSIP Diagnostics SubID 02
1 1 DebugEnabled BYTE bool Enable/disable output

2 4 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use

-1ReceiverDiagsID3f
01DiagIdSubID22
11MaskIdSubID00
24MaskLONGBitsetDiagnostic type mask
64ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
102ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Mask: Diagnostic type mask
BitDescription
0Error log diagnostics allowed
1Run-time log diagnostics allowed
2Debug diagnostics allowed

0x62

Packet Request 0x62

DGPS Position Fix Mode Request

Requests the differential GPS position fix mode. Report packet 0x82 is sent in response
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 DGPSPosFixId ID 62 DGPS Position Fix Mode

0x62

Packet Command 0x62

DGPS Position Fix Mode Command

Sets the differential GPS position fix mode. Report packet 0x82 is sent in response

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1DGPSPosFixIdID62DGPS Position Fix Mode
01FixModeBYTEEnumGPS Position Fix Mode

FixMode: GPS Position Fix Mode
ValueDescription
0Differential correction processing is disabled and position fix solutions are determined without DGPS corrections.
1The receiver only computes solutions if corrections are available for the selected satellites.
2The receiver automatically switches between GPS and DGPS mode, depending on the availability of the differential corrections.

0x65

Packet Request 0x65

DGPS corrections Req

Requests DGPS corrections for given SVID
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 DGPGcorr ID 65
0 1 SVID BYTE n SVID to get corrections for (Max 32)

0 1 SecureRTKID SubID 00
1 1 SecureRTK System Status SubID 00

3 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

Packet Request 0x69 0x00 0x01

SecureRTK Key Data Req

Rover Key Data
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 RTKID ID 69
0 1 SecureRTKID SubID 00
1 1 SecureRTK Get Key SubID 01
2 1 Version BYTE 0 Requested SecureRTK Version
3 1 KeySlot BYTE n Requested Rover Key (0-4)
4 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x69 0x00 0x02

Packet Command 0x69 0x00 0x02

SecureRTK Set Key Data Cmd

Set Rover Key Data
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 RTKID ID 69
0 1 SecureRTKID SubID 00
1 1 SecureRTK Set Key SubID 02
2 1 Version BYTE 0 Current SecureRTK Version
3 1 KeySlot BYTE n Rover Key Index (0-4)
4 17 KeyString STRING - SecureRTK Rover Key (NUL terminated)
21 16 DescriptionString STRING - Key Description/Notes Field
37 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

SecureRTK Delete Key Data Cmd

Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 RTKID ID 69
0 1 SecureRTKID SubID 00
1 1 SecureRTK Delete Key SubID 03
2 1 Version BYTE 0 Requested SecureRTK Version
3 1 KeySlot BYTE n Requested Rover Key (0-4; 255=All Slots)
4 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x69 0x32

Packet Request 0x69 0x32

RTK Stats Req

Requests one of several RTK stats reports. A variant of report packet 0x89 0x33 is sent in response, with the second report packet byte indicating the type of stats.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1RTKIDID69
01RTKStatsIDSubID32
11StatsTypeBYTEEnumThe type of status report being requested
21ControlBYTEBitsetControl bits for various stats
32ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

StatsType: The type of status report being requested
ValueDescription
0RTK1
1RTK Control
2RTK Receiver Info
3RTK Rover State
4Extended RTK Rover State
5RTK Constants
7RTK Error Report
8CMR Reception Statistics
9INS Status Info
10INS ZUPT Info

Control: Control bits for various stats
BitDescription
Reset the RTK Engine
Toggle OTF initialization (search) capability
Resets the CMR statistics for all radio links

0x69 0x40

Packet Request 0x69 0x40

RTK Configuration Req

Requests the RTK configuration
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 RTKID ID 69
0 1 RTKConfigurationID SubID 40
1 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x69 0x40

Packet Command 0x69 0x40

RTK Configuration Cmd

Sets the RTK configuration

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1RTKIDID69
01RTKConfigurationIDSubID40
11PropagationModeBYTEEnumType of CMR propagation used to compute RTK solutions
21VelocityTypeBYTEEnumType of velocity to be output in the RTK position solution
330ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
332ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

PropagationMode: Type of CMR propagation used to compute RTK solutions
ValueDescription
0Solutions generated at the time of the incoming CMR's
1Solutions generated using propagated CMR's

VelocityType: Type of velocity to be output in the RTK position solution
ValueDescription
0Use RTK Library velocity
1Use Doppler velocity
2Use RTK Library velocity when fixed, Doppler velocity otherwise
3Use RTK velocity static, or moving slowly and Doppler velocity otherwise (deprecated, replaced with RTKVelocity)
4Use RTK velocity when fixed and static and Doppler velocity otherwise (deprecated, replaced with RTKVelocity)
5Use Delta (position difference) velocity

Packet Request 0x69 0x41

Requests RTK solution info. Report packet 0x89 0x41 is sent in response.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 RTKID ID 69
0 1 RTKSolutionInfoID SubID 41
1 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x69 0x50

Packet Request 0x69 0x50

RTK Aux Settings Req

Requests the RTK auxilliary settings. Report packet 0x89 0x50 is sent in response.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 RTKID ID 69
0 1 RTKAuxSettingsID SubID 50
1 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x69 0x50

Packet Command 0x69 0x50

RTK Aux Settings Cmd

Updates RTK auxilliary settings. Report packet 0x89 0x50 is sent in response.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1RTKIDID69
01RTKAuxSettingsIDSubID50
11ScintillationModeBYTEEnumRTK engine mode that mitigates the effects of ionospheric scintillation. On ProPoint capable receivers, this enables IonoGuard(tm). IonoGuard mitigates all ionospheric disturbances, not only equatorial scintillation. This setting can be enabled, and will report that IonoGuard is enabled, but will not work without the IonoGuard feature allowed. This can be checked independently by the Picus Feature Manager Feature Response packet, or the Ionospheric Disturbance Response packet.
21BackupSourceBYTEEnumThe RTK Backup Source
31RTKBaseDatumBYTEEnumDatum in which RTK base station was surveyed
48XFillSatFrequencyDOUBLEHzxFill L-band satellite frequency. Value 0 = unchanged
124XFillSatBitRateSINGLEbaudxFill L-band satellite bit rate (600, 1200, 2400)
162RTKBaseIDINTEGERnRTK Base Station ID Number. Valid values are 0-40, kRTKBaseIDUseAnyBase and kRTKBaseIDNoChange
181SatConfigSourceBYTEEnumChoose your source for configuring RTX Satellite tracking
193ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
222ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

ScintillationMode: RTK engine mode that mitigates the effects of ionospheric scintillation. On ProPoint capable receivers, this enables IonoGuard(tm). IonoGuard mitigates all ionospheric disturbances, not only equatorial scintillation. This setting can be enabled, and will report that IonoGuard is enabled, but will not work without the IonoGuard feature allowed. This can be checked independently by the Picus Feature Manager Feature Response packet, or the Ionospheric Disturbance Response packet.
ValueDescription
0Disable scintillation mode
1Enable scintillation mode
255Do not change this setting

BackupSource: The RTK Backup Source
ValueDescription
0Do not use any RTK backup source
1Enable xFill
2Enable Fallback RTX (deprecated, no longer supported)
3Do not use this value
255Do not change this setting

RTKBaseDatum: Datum in which RTK base station was surveyed
ValueDescription
0ITRF2008 Datum
1NAD83 Datum
2ETRS89 Datum
3GDA94 Datum
4SIRGAS-CON 2016.0 Datum
5ITRF1988 Datum
6ITRF1989 Datum
7ITRF1990 Datum
8ITRF1991 Datum
9ITRF1992 Datum
10ITRF1993 Datum
11ITRF1994 Datum
12ITRF1996 Datum
13ITRF1997 Datum
14ITRF2000 Datum
15ITRF2005 Datum
16NAD83CSRS Datum
17NAD83-CORS96 Datum
18ETRF2000-R05 Datum
19SIRGAS2000 Datum
20SIRGAS95 Datum
21NAD83 2011 Datum
22NAD83 MA11 Datum
23NAD83 PA11 Datum
24RGF93v2 Datum
25CGCS2000 Datum
26NZGD2000 Datum
27JGD2000 Datum
28POSGAR07 Datum
29RGAF09 Datum
30RGFG95 Datum
31RGNC9193 Datum
32RGR92 Datum
33RGTAAF07 Datum
34MAGNASIRGAS Datum
35EST97 Datum
36ITRF2014 Datum
37GDA2020 Datum
38NAD83-CSRS 1997.0 Datum
39NAD83-CSRS 2002.0 Datum
40NAD83-CSRS 2010.0 Datum
246ITRF2014_2021 Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
247Legacy_SIRGASCON Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
248ITRF2014_CurrentEpoch Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
249Legacy_GDA94 Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
250Legacy_ITRF2000 Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
251Legacy_ETRF2000_R05 Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
252Legacy_ETRS89 Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
253Legacy_ETRS89 Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
254Automatic Datum (The picus receiver automatically determines the right datum to use depending on the receiver location.
255Do not change this setting

SatConfigSource: Choose your source for configuring RTX Satellite tracking
ValueDescription
0Manual: Receiver uses the Frequency and Bit Rate value from this packet to track the RTX satellite.
1Auto: Receiver uses its position to automatically configure frequency and bit rate to track the RTX Satellites
255Do not change this setting

7 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x69 0x70 0x0a

1TT450s 9600bps

4Trimmark3 19200bps
5Trimmark3 9600bps
18TT450s 16000bps
19TT450s 8000bps
32Trimcomm900
35Old TT450s 4800bps
36Old TT450s 9600bps

48PCC EOC 4800bps

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1RTKIDID69
01FallbackRTXSubID71
11FallbackRTXOffsetSourceValueSubID00
21RTXOffsetSourceBYTEEnumFallback RTX Offset Source being requested.
38ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
112ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

RTXOffsetSource: Fallback RTX Offset Source being requested.
ValueDescription
0Retrieve offsets set by the user
1Retrieve offsets set by the base-station

0x69 0x71 0x00

Fallback RTX User Offsets Cmd

Set the X, Y, and Z user offsets. ACK packet is 0x89 0x71 0x01. No longer supported, do not use.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 RTKID ID 69
0 1 FallbackRTX SubID 71
1 1 FallbackRTXOffsetSourceValue SubID 00
2 8 XOffsetValue DOUBLE meters X-Offset value (Range: +/- 100 Meters)
10 8 YOffsetValue DOUBLE meters Y-Offset value (Range: +/- 100 Meters)
18 8 ZOffsetValue DOUBLE meters Z-Offset value (Range: +/- 100 Meters)
26 8 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
34 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

Packet Command 0x69 0x71 0x01

Fallback RTX Mode Configuration Cmd

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1RTKIDID69
01FallbackRTXSubID71
11FallbackRTXModeSubID01
21FallbackModeBYTEEnumFallback RTX Mode Setting
31RTXOffsetSourceBYTEEnumFallback RTX Offset Source being set
48ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
122ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

FallbackMode: Fallback RTX Mode Setting
ValueDescription
0Set Fallback RTX Mode to RTK
1Set Fallback RTK Mode to RTX
255Do not change this setting

RTXOffsetSource: Fallback RTX Offset Source being set
ValueDescription
0Apply user-set offsets
1Apply base-set offsets
255Do not change this setting

0x69 0x71 0x03

Packet Request 0x69 0x71 0x03

xFill Premium Configuration Request

Requests xFill Premium configuration. Response packet is 0x89 0x71 0x03.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 RTKID ID 69
0 1 xFillPremiumID SubID 71
1 1 xFillPremiumSource SubID 03
2 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x69 0x71 0x03

Packet Command 0x69 0x71 0x03

xFill Premium Configuration Cmd

Sets xFill Premium Configuration. ACK packet is 0x89 0x71 0x03.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1RTKIDID69
01xFillPremiumIDSubID71
11xFillPremiumSourceSubID03
21ScintillationSwitchRuleBYTEEnumConfigures the rule for when to activate xFill Premium due to scintillation. Note: xFill Premium will automatically activate on radio outages for all of these rules.
31ActivateXFillPremiumBYTEEnumFor Manual scintillation switch rule, force receiver to uses xFill Premium positions. Note: Receiver uses xFill Premium Positions only if xFill Premium Status reports Available. Otheriwse, force receiver to use xFill Premium Positions when Status reports Ready.
41ActivateHourBYTEnFor Time-Based switch rule, xFill Premium is force-activated at this hour. A value of 0xFF will not modify existing setting
51ActivateMinuteBYTEnFor Time-Based switch rule, xFill Premium is force-activated at this minute. A value of 0xFF will not modify existing setting
61DeactivateHourBYTEnFor Time-Based switch rule, the receiver is no longer forced to use xFill Premium at this hour. A value of 0xFF will not modify existing setting
71DeactivateMinuteBYTEnFor Time-Based switch rule, the receiver is no longer forced to use xFill Premium at this minute. A value of 0xFF will not modify existing setting
81RecalXFillBYTEnRe-calibrate RTK Base for xFill Premium: Set this to 1 if you need to recalibrate. All other values leave it unchanged. Note:Clearing the current calibration can stop xFill Premium operation for upto an hour.
98ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
172ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

ScintillationSwitchRule: Configures the rule for when to activate xFill Premium due to scintillation. Note: xFill Premium will automatically activate on radio outages for all of these rules.
ValueDescription
0Default rule. xFill Premium activates on radio outages only.
1User controls xFill Premium activation.
2xFill Premium will be force-activated at this user-configured time. Note that the receiver will use xFill Premium generated positions only if the xFill Premium Status reports Available. For Not Available or Not Ready Status, the receiver waits for xFill Premium to report Available status before force-activating and/or using xFill Premium positions. Note: The non-availability of RTK correction stream may lead the receiver to use xFill Premium generated positions beyond the deactivation time.
255Do not change this setting

ActivateXFillPremium: For Manual scintillation switch rule, force receiver to uses xFill Premium positions. Note: Receiver uses xFill Premium Positions only if xFill Premium Status reports Available. Otheriwse, force receiver to use xFill Premium Positions when Status reports Ready.
ValueDescription
0Use RTK positions
1Force receiver to use xFill Premium generated positions.
255Do not change this setting

0 2 MaxPRCAge INTEGER seconds Maximum pseudorange correction age

NMEA Extended Port Configuration Options Request

Requests the data reporting options for NMEA on the specified receiver port. The receiver responds with Report Packet 0x7B 0x08.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1NMEAOptionsCfgIdID7a
01NMEAOptionsIdSubID08
11PortBYTEEnumSerial port for which NMEA options are required for

Port: Serial port for which NMEA options are required for
ValueDescription
0Port0
1Port1
2Port2 (if supported)
3Port3 (if supported)

0x7a 0x08

Packet Command 0x7a 0x08

NMEA Extended Port Configuration Options Command

Each Ag Receivers supports a varying number of serial ports that can transmit NMEA messages. This command configures various parameters of the NMEA for the specified serial port (if supported) on the receiver. This command supersedes other NMEA configuration commands and is designed to be used stand-alone (and not in conjunction with other NMEA Configuration command packets). The receiver acknowledges with 0x7B 0x08.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1NMEAOptionsCfgIdID7a
01NMEAOptionsIdSubID08
11PortBYTEEnumSerial port number
21MessageProtocolBYTEEnumProtocol to which the message applies
31MessageIntervalBYTEEnumMessage interval
44OutputMaskLONGBitsetNMEA bit-mask for outputting messages.
81GGAOptionsBYTEBitsetGGA Options
91PrecisionBYTE0x00-0x07GGA precision (0-7 decimal places). 0xFF specifies to retain existing setting and not modify
1040ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use

Port: Serial port number
ValueDescription
0Port0
1Port1
2Port2
3Port3

MessageProtocol: Protocol to which the message applies
ValueDescription
0Output raw NMEA messages on the specified port
1Output NMEA messages wrapped in TSIP, if TSIP is enabled on that port
255Retain existing message protocol

MessageInterval: Message interval
ValueDescription
0Report at Position Rate
15 seconds
21 second
3200 milliseconds
4100 milliseconds
255Retain existing message interval

OutputMask: NMEA bit-mask for outputting messages.
BitDescription
Retain the mask currently configured for this serial port and ignore other bits
GGA
GLL
VTG
GSV
GSA
ZDA
ALM - deprecated
RMC
GRS
GBS - deprecated
GST

GGAOptions: GGA Options
BitDescription
Retain the existing configuration and ignore value set in other bits
Invalidate GGA for old positions
Invalidate GGA for non-differential positions when in Auto DGPS mode
Show invalid GGA as '0' in the status field
Do not show decimal places for time in GGA

-1PosFixRateOptionsIdID7c
01PosFixRateOptionsSubIdSubID01
11OptionFlags1BYTEBitsetVarious Options
21OptionFlags2BYTEBitsetVarious Options

OptionFlags1: Various Options
BitDescription
Double precision time for report packets 0x42, 0x43, 0x4A, 0x54, 0x57, 0x83, and 0x84
True timestamp of velocity

OptionFlags2: Various Options
BitDescription
No Port A automatic message output
No Port B automatic message output
No time only (0D) output in Report Packet 0x54
Output full Satellite Data for ephemeris and UTC when received in Report Packet 0x58
Do not echo RTCM Port A input in Report Packet 0x1A 0x00

0x8e 0x7c

Packet Command 0x8e 0x7c

DGPS Receiver Configuration Block v3

Used to set the receiver configuration parameters stored in battery-backed RAM (Random Access Memory). Report Packet 0x8F 0x7C is sent in response.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 DGPSConfigId SubID 7c
1 1 PortNumber BYTE n The receiver port number
2 20 Name STRING - Software name
22 1 FirmwareMajVersion BYTE n Major firmware version
23 1 FirmwareMinVersion BYTE n Minor firmware version
24 1 FirmwareMonth BYTE n Firmware release month
25 1 FirmwareDay BYTE n Firmware release day
26 1 FirmwareYear BYTE n Firmware release year (from 1900)
27 187 ConfigBlockV3 ConfigBlockV3 -
214 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

ConfigBlockV3: 
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
0 1 Head BYTE 0x55
1 1 CfgBlkVersion BYTE 0x03
2 1 StartupCount BYTE n
3 120 FixedDef FixedDef -
123 60 UserDef UserDef -
183 2 Tail INTEGER 0xAA55
185 2 RxCfgChecksum INTEGER n This is an internal checksum and can be ignored by tsip users

FixedDef: 
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
0 61 BoilerPlate BoilerPlate -
61 32 RxDef RxDef -
93 27 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use

BoilerPlate: 
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
0 22 SerialNumber STRING - Serial Number
22 10 PartNumber STRING - Part Number
32 17 OverrideName STRING - Product name override
49 6 BriefOverrideName STRING - Brief product name override
55 1 ManufactureDay BYTE n Day of receiver manufacture
56 1 ManufactureMonth BYTE n Month of receiver manufacture
57 2 ManufactureYear INTEGER n Year of receiver manufacture
59 2 SuperPacketPP INTEGER n Super-packet post processing option

222TNLSubscriptionWeekINTEGERn
244AntennaGainSINGLE-
281RTKOptionBYTEn
291BrandingDisabledOptionBYTEn
301PrototypeHardwareBYTEn
311DefDGPSSourceBYTEn

0x8e 0x84

Packet Command 0x8e 0x84

Satellite FFT Control Command

Starts or stops the satellite FFT (Fast Fourier Transform) diagnostics and sets the FFT diagnostic options. Acknowledged with Report Packet 0x8F 0x84.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01SpotFFTAppIdSubID84
11ModeBYTEEnumStarts or stops FFT diagnostics
21OscillatorOffsetBYTEEnumInclude or exclude oscillator offset in narrowband FFT
31FFTPlotTypeBYTEEnumSelects narrowband or wideband FFT plot
41InputSquaredBYTEnIndicates whether to square the input
58CenterFreqDOUBLE-FFT center frequency (Hz)
131NumIntegrationsBYTEnNumber of post-FFT power sums (Recommended: 5-narrowband, 33-wideband)
143ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
172ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Mode: Starts or stops FFT diagnostics
ValueDescription
0Stop the FFT
1Start the FFT

OscillatorOffset: Include or exclude oscillator offset in narrowband FFT
ValueDescription
0Exclude the oscillator offset
1Include the oscillator offset

FFTPlotType: Selects narrowband or wideband FFT plot
ValueDescription
0Wideband FFT spectral plot
1Narrowband FFT spectral plot

0x8e 0x85

Packet Request 0x8e 0x85

DGPS Source Tracking Status Request

requests the tracking status for the source of DGPS corrections (either beacon or satellite). Report packet 0x8f 0x85 is sent in response.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 DGPSSourceTrackingId SubID 85
1 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

Spot Options Request

Requests current

Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppID ID 8e
0 1 SpotIoOptions SubID 88
1 1 StreamId BYTE n
2 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0x88

Packet Command 0x8e 0x88

DGPS Source Control Request

Requests current DGPS source and related settings. Report packet 0x8F 0x89 is sent in response.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppID ID 8e
0 1 SpotIoOptions SubID 88
1 1 StreamId BYTE n
2 1 OptionsFlags BYTE n
3 4 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
7 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0x89

Packet Request 0x8e 0x89

DGPS Source Control Request

Requests current DGPS source and related settings. Report packet 0x8F 0x89 is sent in response.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppID ID 8e
0 1 DGPSSourceControl SubID 89
1 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0x89

Packet Command 0x8e 0x89

DGPS Source Control Command

Controls DGPS source and related settings. Acknowledgement packet 0x8F 0x89 is sent in response.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8e
01DGPSSourceControlSubID89
11DGPSSrcModeBYTEEnumDGPS Source mode
21BeaconAcqModeBYTEEnumBeacon mode used to acquire DGPS signals:
32BeaconFreq0INTEGER2835-3250Channel 0 manual beacon frequency for manual beacon mode and DGPS source autoswitching mode (units of 0.1 kHz). Value 0 = unchanged
52BeaconFreq1INTEGER2835-3250Channel 1 manual beacon frequency for manual beacon mode and DGPS source autoswitching mode (units of 0.1 kHz). Value 0 = unchanged
72BeaconRTCMTimeoutINTEGERsecondsTime to wait for RTCM corrections before switching to an alternate source of DGPS in DGPS source auto-switching mode (default: XX sec). Value 0 = unchanged
98SatelliteFrequencyDOUBLEHzSatellite frequency for manual Satellite mode and DGPS source auto-switching mode. Value 0 = unchanged; max = 1559 MHz
174SatelliteBitRateSINGLEbaudSatellite bit rate (600, 1200, 2400)
212SatelliteRTCMTimeoutINTEGERsecondsTime to wait for RTCM corrections before switching to an alternate source of DGPS in DGPS source auto-switching mode (default: XX sec). Value 0 = unchanged
232WAASTimeoutINTEGERsecondsTime to wait for WAAS corrections before switching to an alternate source of DGPS in DGPS source auto-switching mode (default: XX sec). Value 0 = unchanged
251CorrectionOptionsBYTEBitsetCorrection options
261SatConfigSourceBYTEEnumChoose your source for configuring RTX Satellite tracking
272ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

DGPSSrcMode: DGPS Source mode
ValueDescription
0Beacon differential only
1Satellite differential only
2Automatic source selection amongst all available DGPS sources (i.e. in an NH134, switching amongst beacon, satellite, external RTCM, and WAAS). This functionality is only available in the NH134 receiver (dual DSPs)
3WAAS differential only
4External RTCM/DGPS only
5Satellite high accuracy solution only (i.e. Omnistar HP)
6RTK only
7External Receiver Only
8CenterPoint RTX standard mode over network only
9CenterPoint RTX standard mode over satellite only
10CenterPoint RTX US fast mode over satellite only (Deprecated)
11CenterPoint RTX US fast mode over network only (Deprecated)
12RangePoint RTX over satellite
13Reserved
14CenterPoint RTX EU standard mode over network only
15CenterPoint RTX EU standard mode over satellite only
16RangePoint RTX over network
17CenterPoint RTX best subscription over network only
18CenterPoint RTX best subscription over satellite only
19Reserved for future use.
20Reserved for future use.
21ViewPoint RTX over network only
22ViewPoint RTX over satellite only
23CLAS (QZSS, Japan) over satellite only
130CenterPoint RTX Fast automatic delivery method selection
131CenterPoint RTX automatic delivery method selection
132RangePoint RTX automatic delivery method selection
133ViewPoint RTX automatic delivery method selection
134RTX Best automatic delivery method selection
167Galileo HAS automatic delivery method selection

BeaconAcqMode: Beacon mode used to acquire DGPS signals:
ValueDescription
0Manual mode
1Auto Distance mode
2Auto Power mode
3Reserved
4Channel disabled

CorrectionOptions: Correction options
BitDescription
Use WAAS corrections as a backup
Treat the test mode Type 2 WAAS corrections as a valid corrections source.
Use NCS decoder instead of the standard Racal/Omnistar decoders
Use DGPS solution before high accuracy solution has converged.

SatConfigSource: Choose your source for configuring RTX Satellite tracking
ValueDescription
0Manual: Receiver uses the Frequency and Bit Rate value from this packet to track the RTX satellite.
1Auto: Receiver uses its position to automatically configure frequency and bit rate to track the RTX Satellites
255Do not change this setting

0x8e 0x8b

Packet Request 0x8e 0x8b

Service Provider Activation Information Request

Requests service provider activation information. Report Packet 0x8F 0x8B is sent in response.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8e
01ServiceProviderActivationInfoSubID8b
11ServiceProviderBYTEEnumService Provider
21InfoTypeBYTEEnumInformation Type
31ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
42ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

ServiceProvider: Service Provider
ValueDescription
3Racal
4Omnistar

InfoType: Information Type
ValueDescription
0Brief description
1Extended description

0x8e 0x8c 0x00

Packet Command 0x8e 0x8c 0x00

RDI Key Press Command

The key press command packet simulates sending a key press to the display. Whenever the end user application wants to move around the remote display (i.e. in response to a key press on its own terminal, touch screen, etc.), this packet is sent to the receiver to indicate that the receiver should process the specified key press action. The receiver will acknowledge the key press command by sending Report Packet 0x8F 0x8C.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01RDIIdSubID8c
11RDIKeyPressIdSubID00
21KeyPressBYTEEnumA keypress
34ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
72ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

KeyPress: A keypress
ValueDescription
0Enter key press
1Up arrow key press
2Down arrow key press
3Right arrow key press
4Escape key press
6Enter key held down for a long time
7Enter key released after held down for a long time
8Left arrow key press
9Function 1 key press
10Function 2 key press
11Function 3 key press
12Function 4 key press
13Function 5 key press
14Function 6 key press

0x8e 0x8c 0x01

Packet Request 0x8e 0x8c 0x01

RDI Screen Contents Request

Requests the remote display screen contents once. The receiver will respond by sending Report Packet 0x8F 0x8C 0x01. If automatic reporting of the screen contents is configured, this packet must be sent once per minute to maintain that configuration (version 1.40 and later). This provides a timeout mechanism if the receiver is disconnected from the user terminal so the automatic reporting does not continue indefinitely if not needed.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 RDIId SubID 8c
1 1 RDIScreenContentsId SubID 01
2 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0x8c 0x03

Packet Request 0x8e 0x8c 0x03

RDI Configuration Request

Requests the remote display configuration. The receiver will respond by sending Report Packet 0x8F 0x8C 0x03
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 RDIId SubID 8c
1 1 RDIConfigId SubID 03
2 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0x8c 0x03

Packet Command 0x8e 0x8c 0x03

RDI Configuration Command

Sets the remote display configuration

01RDIIdSubID8c
11RDIConfigIdSubID03

34ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
72ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

DataLocation: Remote display options
BitDescription
Display information to receiver front panel
Display information to remote display via serial port. screen content and cursor information packet

0x8e 0x8c 0x05

Remote Event Line State Change Command

Indicates that one of the event lines into the remote display has changed state.

0 1 RDIId SubID 8c
1 1 RDIEventLineId SubID 05

0x8e 0x93 0x15 0x00

Packet Request 0x8e 0x93 0x15 0x00

File Transfer Listing Request

Requests a list of files found in the specified directory. A File Transfer Listing Reponse packet is sent for each file in the directory (empty directory will produce no response).

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8e
01DebugIdSubID93
11FileTransferIdSubID15
21FileListingIdSubID00
3END-3PathARRAY of CHAR-Directory path for which listing is requested
3 + END-32ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

0x8e 0x93 0x15 0x01 0x01

Packet Request 0x8e 0x93 0x15 0x01 0x01

File Transfer Get - Open Request

Requests that file be opened for reading. File Transfer Get - Open Response is sent with info needed for the transfer.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8e
01DebugIdSubID93
11FileTransferIdSubID15
21FileGetIdSubID01
31OpenReqIdSubID01
4END-4FilenameARRAY of CHAR-Name of file to open for reading
4 + END-42ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

0x8e 0x93 0x15 0x01 0x02

Packet Request 0x8e 0x93 0x15 0x01 0x02

File Transfer Get - Data Block Request

Requests to read block of data from open file. If successful File Transfer Get - Data Block Response is sent with the data, otherwise File Transfer Get - Close Response or File Transfer Get - Error Response is sent.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppID ID 8e
0 1 DebugId SubID 93
1 1 FileTransferId SubID 15
2 1 FileGetId SubID 01
3 1 DataBlockReqId SubID 02
4 4 FileId LONG n Internal identifier of file from which to read
8 4 Offset LONG n Offset from start of file at which to read
12 1 Size BYTE n Size of block to read in bytes
13 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0x93 0x15 0x01 0x03

Packet Request 0x8e 0x93 0x15 0x01 0x03

File Transfer Get - Close Request

Requests to close previously opened file. If successful File Transfer Get - Close Response is sent, otherwise File Transfer Get - Error Response is sent.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppID ID 8e
0 1 DebugId SubID 93
1 1 FileTransferId SubID 15
2 1 FileGetId SubID 01
3 1 CloseReqId SubID 03
4 4 FileId LONG n Internal identifier of file to close
8 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0x93 0x15 0x01 0x04

Packet Request 0x8e 0x93 0x15 0x01 0x04

File Transfer Request Hash of File

Requests an Hash of the specified file in the fusion file system. The receiver will respond with an Hash response packet (8f930104...)

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8e
01DebugIdSubID93
11FileTransferIdSubID15
21FileGetIdSubID01
31HashIdSubID04
41AlgorithmBYTEEnumAlgorithm to use for Hash generation
54ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
9END-9FilenameARRAY of CHAR-File to generate the Hash
9 + END-92ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Algorithm: Algorithm to use for Hash generation
ValueDescription
0MD5 Message Digest Algorithm (128-bit)
1SHA256 Secure Hash Algorithm (256-bit)

- 1 AppID ID 8e
0 1 DGPSPrioritiesId SubID 9e
1 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0x9e

Packet Command 0x8e 0x9e

DGPS Priority Command

Sets the priorities of the DGPS sources that are in use by the receiver. Up to four different DGPS sources can be set. The resulting receiver configuration is returned in the response packet 0x8F 0x9E.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01DGPSPrioritiesIdSubID9e
11NumSourcesBYTEnNumber of DGPS sources used (1-4)
23ReservedBYTE0Reserved for future use
53 * NumSourcesSourceInfoArrayARRAY of 8ef9eDGPSSourceInfo-DGPS Source info array
5 + 3 * NumSources2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

8ef9eDGPSSourceInfo: DGPS Source info
Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
01DGPSSourceModeBYTEEnumDGPS Source mode
12ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use

DGPSSourceMode: DGPS Source mode
ValueDescription
0Beacon
1Omnistar VBS
3WAAS
4External RTCM/DGPS
5Omnistar HP
6RTK
7External Receiver Only
8CenterPoint RTX standard mode over network only
9CenterPoint RTX standard mode over satellite only
10CenterPoint RTX US fast mode over satellite only (Deprecated)
11CenterPoint RTX US fast mode over network only (Deprecated)
12RangePoint RTX
13
14CenterPoint RTX EU standard mode over network only
15CenterPoint RTX EU standard mode over satellite only
16RangePoint RTX over network only
17CenterPoint RTX best subscription over network only
18CenterPoint RTX best subscription over satellite only
19Stinger with RTX corrections over network only
20Stinger with RTX corrections over satellite only
21ViewPoint RTX over network only
22ViewPoint RTX over satellite only
23CLAS (QZSS, Japan) over satellite only
24Galileo HAS automatic delivery method selection

01CANBusIdSubID9f
11CANBusCfgIdSubID00
22ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
41NumChannelsBYTEn
510 * NumChannelsChannelCfgARRAY of CANChannelCfg-CAN channel configuration
5 + 10 * NumChannels2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

CANChannelCfg: CAN Channel Config
Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
01ChannelBYTEnCAN Channel Id (Note: First channel = 0)
11BusSpeedBYTEEnumSpeed of the CAN bus
21J1939AddressBYTEnJ1939 Address for ECU on the bus
31J1939ECUInstanceBYTEnJ1939 ECU Instance used in the NAME field
41J1939FunctionInstanceBYTEnJ1939 Function Instance used in the NAME field
51FlagsBYTEBitsetFlags indicating special CAN properties
64ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use

BusSpeed: Speed of the CAN bus
ValueDescription
0250 kbps
1125 kbps
2500 kbps
3850 kbps

Flags: Flags indicating special CAN properties
BitDescription
Inserts a delay between multi-packet writes
CAN channel is self configurable
The CAN bus is internally terminated (Note: Only relevant on hardware that supports internal termination)

Packet Request 0x8e 0xa2

Position Solution Status Request

Requests a Position Solution Status 0x8F 0xA2 report.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppID ID 8e
0 1 PosSolnStatusId SubID a2
1 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa3

Packet Request 0x8e 0xa3

Omnistar XP/HP Info Request

Requests specific information about the Omnistar XP/HP process

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8e
01OmnistarXPHPSubIdSubIDa3
11TypeBYTEEnumType of Omnistar XP/HP information to be retrieved
22ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Type: Type of Omnistar XP/HP information to be retrieved
ValueDescription
0Version Information
1Subscription Information
2Status Information
3Station Information
4Auto-Seed Information
5Control Information
6Debug Information

0x8e 0xa3 0x04

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa3 0x04

Omnistar XP/HP Auto-Seed Command

Sets Auto-Seed information held by the receiver. If the Auto-Seed functionality is turned on in the receiver, these values will be used at startup when the receiver is rebooted.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppID ID 8e
0 1 OmnistarXPHPSubId SubID a3
1 1 PositionVarianceId SubID 04
2 4 Confidence SINGLE - The confidence in the position-variance pair
6 8 Latitude DOUBLE d The latitude of the position
14 8 Longitude DOUBLE d The longitude of the position
22 8 Height DOUBLE m The height of the position
30 4 LatitudeVariance SINGLE d2 The latitude variance of the position
34 4 LongitudeVariance SINGLE d2 The longitude variance of the position
38 4 HeightVariance SINGLE m2 The height variance of the position
42 16 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
58 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa3 0x05

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa3 0x05

Omnistar XP/HP Controls Command

Sets various control parameters of the Omnistar XP/HP processor

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8e
01OmnistarXPHPSubIdSubIDa3
11ControlsIdSubID05
22ValidFieldsINTEGERBitsetA bitset of which fields are being changed by the command
41SeedOnStartupBYTEEnumUse the AutoSeed functionality on startup
54ConfidenceThresholdSINGLE-The minimum confidence for AutoSeed to operate
94VelocityThresholdSINGLEm/sThe minimum velocity for AutoSeed to fail
134ConvergenceThresholdSINGLEmThe 3D sigma accuracy at which point the solution is regarded as converged
171StaticConvergenceBYTEEnumEnables or disables the static convergence functionality
181SourceSelectorBYTEEnumSelects source that will be controlled by this packet
1913ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
322ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

ValidFields: A bitset of which fields are being changed by the command
BitDescription
Autoseed enable field valid
Autoseed confidence threshold field valid
Autoseed velocity threshold field valid
Convergence threshold field valid
Static convergence field valid

SeedOnStartup: Use the AutoSeed functionality on startup
ValueDescription
0Disables the AutoSeed functionality on startup
1Enables the Autoseed functionality on startup

StaticConvergence: Enables or disables the static convergence functionality
ValueDescription
0Disables static initialization
1Enables static initialization

SourceSelector: Selects source that will be controlled by this packet
ValueDescription
0Omnistar HP/XP configuration
1GE configuration

0x8e 0xa3 0x06

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa3 0x06

Omnistar XP/HP Debug Command

Sets up the debugging output of the XP/HP processor

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8e
01OmnistarXPHPSubIdSubIDa3
11DebugControlIdSubID06
21EnabledBYTEEnumDebug output is enabled
31PortBYTEEnumThe port to output the data on
418ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
222ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Enabled: Debug output is enabled
ValueDescription
0Disables the AutoSeed functionality on startup
1Enables the Autoseed functionality on startup

Port: The port to output the data on
ValueDescription
0Output debug info on Port A
1Output debug info on Port B
2Output debug info on Port C

0x8e 0xa3 0x07

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa3 0x07

Omnistar XP/HP Reset Command

Resets the XP/HP engine
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppID ID 8e
0 1 OmnistarXPHPSubId SubID a3
1 1 ResetXPHP SubID 07
2 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa4

Packet Request 0x8e 0xa4

Filter Config Request

Requests Filter configuration. Report Packet 0x8F 0xA4 is sent in response.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8e
01FilterSubIdSubIDa4
11TypeBYTEEnumType of a filter config to be retrieved
22ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Type: Type of a filter config to be retrieved
ValueDescription
3Position-Velocity Filter
4Linear Bias Filter
5Quadratic Bias Filter
6Kalman Filter
7Field Level Smoothing

0x8e 0xa4 0x05

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa4 0x05

QB Filter Config Command

Sets Quadratic Bias Filter configuration. Report Packet 0x8F 0xA4 0x05 is sent in response.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8e
01FilterSubIdSubIDa4
11QBFilterSubID05
24TimeSINGLEsecondsTime constant that controls the decay of biases
64DepthSINGLE0-4 secDepth of data to use for estimation of vehicle position, velocity and acceleration
104MaxPropagationTimeSINGLEsecondsMaximum propagation time
141StateBYTEEnumFilter state
151ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
162ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

State: Filter state
ValueDescription
0Filter off
1Filter on
2Filter reset

0x8e 0xa4 0x06

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa4 0x06

Kalman Filter Config Command

Sets Kalman Filter configuration. Report Packet 0x8F 0xA4 0x06 is sent in response.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8e
01FilterSubIdSubIDa4
11KalmanFilterSubID06
24MaxPropagationTimeSINGLEsecondsMaximum propagation time
61SpeedSettingBYTEnSpeed setting (0-99; 253 = slow setting; 254 = medium setting; 255 = fast setting)
71StateBYTEEnumFilter state
84ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
122ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

State: Filter state
ValueDescription
0Filter off
1Filter on
2Filter reset

0x8e 0xa4 0x07

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa4 0x07

Field Level Smoothing Command

Command to enable or disable Field Level Smoothing. Response (ACK) Packet is 0x8f 0xa4 0x07
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppID ID 8e
0 1 FLSmoothingSubID SubID a4
1 1 SetSmoothingSubID SubID 07
2 1 SmoothingEnable BYTE 0 Enable or Disable Field Level Smoothing
3 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa5 0x00

Packet Request 0x8e 0xa5 0x00

SBAS Satellite Control Request

Requests and sets the current SBAS settings. The command and report packets can each contain a variable number of SBAS SV entries.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 SBASAppId SubID a5
1 1 SBASInfoId SubID 00
2 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa5 0x00

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa5 0x00

SBAS Satellite Control Command

Sets the current SBAS settings. This packet can contain a variable number of SBAS SV entries. SV entries not listed in the command packet will not be updated.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01SBASAppIdSubIDa5
11SBASInfoIdSubID00
21NumSvsBYTEnNumber of SBAS satellite settings
32 * NumSvsSBASInfoArrayARRAY of SBASInfo-Information for all the SBAS satellites to be updated
3 + 2 * NumSvs2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

SBASInfo: Information for a particular SBAS satellite
Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
01PRNBYTEnPRN of the SBAS satellite
11FlagsBYTEEnumSBAS tracking states

Flags: SBAS tracking states
ValueDescription
0SBAS satellite is disabled
1SBAS satellite is enabled depending on the health
2SBAS satellite is enabled regardless of the health
3SBAS satellite is enabled if in the correct broadcast region and health is good
4SBAS satellite is enabled if in the correct broadcast region. Deprecated, please use EnabledOverrideHealth If commanded, will respond with EnabledOverrideHealth in future versions.

0x8e 0xa5 0x01

Packet Request 0x8e 0xa5 0x01

Use SBAS+ Control Request

Requests the current 'Use SBAS+' settings. Response packet is 0x8F 0xA5 0x01
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 SBASAppId SubID a5
1 1 SBASPlusSubId SubID 01
2 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa5 0x01

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa5 0x01

Use SBAS+ Control Command

Sets the 'Use SBAS+' configuration settings. SBAS+ mode uses as much SBAS correction information as possible and as many satellites as possible to improve yield and accuracy when positioning in SBAS mode. Response packet is 0x8F 0xA5 0x01
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 SBASAppId SubID a5
1 1 SBASPlusSubId SubID 01
2 1 UseSBASPlus BYTE bool Set to 1 to use SBAS+, 0 otherwise
3 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa5 0x02

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa5 0x02

SBAS Default Constellation Reset Command

Resets the receiver's SBAS constellation tracking scheme to the defaults.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 SBASAppId SubID a5
1 1 SBASPlusSubId SubID 02
2 1 ResetSBASTrackingDefaults BYTE bool Set to 1 to reset the SBAS tracking defaults. 0 makes no changes.
3 39 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
42 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa6 0x01

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa6 0x01

Manufacturing Set External Output Pin State Cmd

Sets the state of a Output pin.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01MfgAppIdSubIDa6
11MfgOutputPinSetIdSubID01
21ProductIdBYTEEnumProduct Id of the expected device.
31OutputPin0BYTEnSets the state of Output pin 0 (0=lo, 1=hi). This is platform specific, but typically event out.
41OutputPin1BYTEnSets the state of Output pin 1 (0=lo, 1=hi). This is platform specific, but typically digital out.
51OutputPin2BYTEnSets the state of Output pin 2 (0=lo, 1=hi). This is platform specific, but typically data out.
61OutputPin3BYTEnSets the state of Output pin 3 (0=lo, 1=hi). This is platform specific, but typically data out 2.
712ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
192ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

ProductId: Product Id of the expected device.
ValueDescription
0EZG-500 (Orca)
1Ag252/332 (Buffalo)
2EZG-250 (Echo)
3Pollux
4Orff
5Ahi
6Saturn
7AG-372, AG-382 (Wahoo, Senna)
8TM-200 (Manatee)
9NAV-900, AG-392, AG-482 (Shifter, Rubble, Mariposa)
10NAV-500, AG-200 (Nickel)
11NAV-960 (Whippet)
255Unknown Product

0x8e 0xa6 0x02

Packet Request 0x8e 0xa6 0x02

1 1 ExInfoId SubID 25

Product Information Command

0 1 MfgAppId SubID a6
1 1 ProdInfoId SubID 27
2 20 PartNumber STRING - Product part number
22 18 Name STRING - Product name
40 6 AbbrevName STRING - Abbreviated product name (exactly 5 characters)
46 60 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
106 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

1 1 MFGUnlockId SubID 28

Firmware signature command

- 1 AppId ID 8e

1 1 MfgFwSignature SubID 29
2 4 Offset LONG n offset into the firmware

14 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa6 0x30

Packet Request 0x8e 0xa6 0x30

[Manufacturing Test] GNSS Band RF Spectrum Analyzer Peak FFT Frequency

Requests the peak FFT frequency from the RF spectrum analyzer for GNSS bands. Intended for factory test use only.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01MfgAppIdSubIDa6
11FFTTestIdSubID30
21ProductIdBYTEEnumProduct Id of the expected device.
31RFBandBYTEEnumRF Band to return peak FFT frequency. Not all bands are supported on all hardware.
48ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
122ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

ProductId: Product Id of the expected device.
ValueDescription
0EZG-500 (Orca)
1Ag252/332 (Buffalo)
2EZG-250 (Echo)
3Pollux
4Orff
5Ahi
6Saturn
7AG-372, AG-382 (Wahoo, Senna)
8TM-200 (Manatee)
9NAV-900, AG-392, AG-482 (Shifter, Rubble, Mariposa)
10NAV-500, AG-200 (Nickel)
11NAV-960 (Whippet)
255Unknown Product

RFBand: RF Band to return peak FFT frequency. Not all bands are supported on all hardware.
ValueDescription
0L1 Band (1575.42MHz / GLN = 1602 + 0.5625 * Chan)
1L2 Band (1227.6MHz / GLN = 1246 + 0.4375 * Chan)
4L5 Band (1176.45MHz / GAL 1191.795MHz)
5E6 Band (1278.75MHz / BDS 1268.52)
6B1 Band 1561.098MHz
11S1 Band 2492.028MHz

-1AppIdID8e
01MfgAppIdSubIDa6
11SetTXPowerIdSubID43
21ProductIdBYTEEnumProduct Id of the expected device
32OutputPowerINTEGERnDesired output power in dBmX1000 at 125 resolution. For GainCalculationMode 0 or 1, OutputPower is (0-20000). For GainCalculationMode = 2, OutputPower is (-15000-(-4000))
51LevelBYTEnSystem support for different preconfigurable power levels in operational mode. For PLT usage power level should be configured to 0. Max = 3
61BandBYTEEnumOperation band
72ChannelINTEGERnChannel within the WiFi band
91BandwidthBYTEEnumBandwidth
101AntennaBYTEEnum
111NonServingChannelBYTEEnumThe power settings will be set only for nonserving rate groups
121ChannelLimitationBYTEEnumDisable/Enable whether to use the channel power limits from the ini file
131GainCalculationModeBYTEEnum
141AnalogGainControlBYTEnAnalog settings index - For debug purpose only: This indicator is used to indicate the user's chosen analog gain. Max = 4
152ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

ProductId: Product Id of the expected device
ValueDescription
0EZG-500 (Orca)
1Ag252/332 (Buffalo)
2EZG-250 (Echo)
3Pollux
4Orff
5Ahi
6Saturn
7AG-372, AG-382 (Wahoo, Senna)
8TM-200 (Manatee)
9NAV-900, AG-392, AG-482 (Shifter, Rubble, Mariposa)
10NAV-500, AG-200 (Nickel)
11NAV-960 (Whippet)
255Unknown Product

Band: Operation band
ValueDescription
02.4GHz band
15GHz band
24.9GHz band

Bandwidth: Bandwidth
ValueDescription
-140MHz Lower Primary
020MHz
140MHz Upper Primary

Antenna: 
ValueDescription
0The FW will automatically select which TX paths to calculate the power for (based on how many antennas are assembled, band, BW and etc.)
1Force TX power calculation for TX1 path
2Force TX power calculation for TX2 path
3Force TX power calculation for both TX paths

NonServingChannel: The power settings will be set only for nonserving rate groups
ValueDescription
0Serving
1NonServing

ChannelLimitation: Disable/Enable whether to use the channel power limits from the ini file
ValueDescription
0Disabled
1Enabled

GainCalculationMode: 
ValueDescription
0The BO (digital gain) and the analog gain (VGA/iPA/mixer) are being calculated and set using FW defines
1No BO limits at max analog gain settings - at max gain combination there will be no limits for the available BO (digital gain)
2The analog gain and the digital gain are overridden, i.e. the use can choose which of the analog gain combinations he wants to use (choose 1 of the 4 available combinations) and the user can set the digital gain (digital BO as required)

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa6 0x44

-1AppIdID8e
01MfgAppIdSubIDa6
11ContTXTestEnableIdSubID44
21ProductIdBYTEEnumProduct Id of the expected device

71RateBYTEnTransmission rate (0-27)
82SizeINTEGERnSize of data field
104ModeLONGnNumber of packets
141GuardIntervalBYTEEnum
151Options1BYTEnUnused. Default=0
161Options2BYTEnSupported options according to bitmap. Bit 1: Override CCA. Bit 2: Fixed/Incremental Sequence Number
176SourceMACARRAY of BYTE-Array containing source MAC address in hex
236DestMACARRAY of BYTE-Array containing destination MAC address in hex
291ChannelWidthBYTEEnum
302ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

4Orff
5Ahi
6Saturn

9NAV-900, AG-392, AG-482 (Shifter, Rubble, Mariposa)
10NAV-500, AG-200 (Nickel)
11NAV-960 (Whippet)
255Unknown Product

GuardInterval: 
ValueDescription
0Long
1Short

ChannelWidth: 
ValueDescription
020MHz
140MHz

0x8e 0xa7 0x00 0x00

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa7 0x00 0x00

GPS Position Auto-Report by Antenna Configuration Packet

Sets up default automatic reports for a selected antenna. This will output the position used internally by the receiver.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 PositioningId SubID a7
1 1 PositionAutoReportId SubID 00
2 1 FilterByAntennaId SubID 00
3 1 Enable BYTE n Indicates whether to enable (1) or disable (0) the report
4 1 AntennaId BYTE n The id of the antenna to report on (255 means all antennas)
5 4 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
9 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa7 0x00 0x01

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa7 0x00 0x01

GPS Position Auto-Report by Position Engine Configuration Packet

Sets up automatic reports for a selected antenna based on the position engine used to generate the solution. Only positions of from the specified engines will be output

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01PositioningIdSubIDa7
11PositionAutoReportIdSubID00
21FilterByPositionEngIdSubID01
31EnableBYTEnIndicates whether to enable (1) or disable (0) the report
41AntennaIdBYTEnThe id of the antenna to report on (255 means all antennas)
54ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
91NumEngsBYTEnThe number of position engines to enable/disable
10NumEngsPositionEngArrayARRAY of BYTE-An array of Position engines to report on (not report on)
10 + NumEngs2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa7 0x00 0x02

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa7 0x00 0x02

GPS Position Auto-Report by Position Type Configuration Packet

Sets up automatic reports for a selected antenna based on the position type produced. Only positions of those types will be output.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01PositioningIdSubIDa7
11PositionAutoReportIdSubID00
21FilterByPositionTypeIdSubID02
31EnableBYTEnIndicates whether to enable (1) or disable (0) the report
41AntennaIdBYTEnThe id of the antenna to report on (255 means all antennas)
54ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
91NumTypesBYTEnThe number of position types to enable/disable
10NumTypesPositionTypeArrayARRAY of BYTE-An array of Position types to report on (not report on)
10 + NumTypes2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa7 0x00 0x03

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa7 0x00 0x03

GPS Position Auto-Report by Flag Type Configuration Packet

Sets up automatic reports for a selected antenna based on the position flags assigned. Only positions with the given flags will be output.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01PositioningIdSubIDa7
11PositionAutoReportIdSubID00
21FilterByFlagTypeIdSubID03
31EnableBYTEnIndicates whether to enable (1) or disable (0) the report
41AntennaIdBYTEnThe id of the antenna to report on (255 means all antennas)
54SetFlagsLONGBitsetEnables/Disables the report of positions with the given flags set.
94ClearedFlagsLONGBitsetEnables/Disables the report to positions with the given flags cleared.
134ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
172ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

SetFlags: Enables/Disables the report of positions with the given flags set.
BitDescription
Indicates this position was the one used internally for the antenna
Indicates that the position has gone through some form of filtering or adjustiment

ClearedFlags: Enables/Disables the report to positions with the given flags cleared.
BitDescription
Indicates this position was the one used internally for the antenna
Indicates that the position has gone through some form of filtering or adjustiment

0x8e 0xa7 0x01 0x00

Packet Request 0x8e 0xa7 0x01 0x00

GPS Position Packet Request

Requests the last position from for a specified antenna. If the last position wasn't valid, a no position is output instead
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 PositioningId SubID a7
1 1 GPSPositionId SubID 01
2 1 PositionReqTypeId SubID 00
3 1 AntennaId BYTE n The id of the antenna to request from
4 4 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
8 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa7 0x01 0x01

Packet Request 0x8e 0xa7 0x01 0x01

GPS Position Packet by Engine Request

Requests the last position from the given engine on the specified antenna. If the last position wasn't valid, a no position is output instead
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 PositioningId SubID a7
1 1 GPSPositionId SubID 01
2 1 PositionReqTypeId SubID 01
3 1 AntennaId BYTE n The id of the antenna to request from
4 1 PositionEng BYTE n The type of position to output
5 4 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
9 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa7 0x01 0x02

Packet Request 0x8e 0xa7 0x01 0x02

GPS Position Packet by Type Request

Requests the last position of the given type from the specified antenna. If the last position wasn't valid, a no position is output instead
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 PositioningId SubID a7
1 1 GPSPositionId SubID 01
2 1 PositionReqTypeId SubID 02
3 1 AntennaId BYTE n The id of the antenna to request from
4 1 PositionType BYTE n The type of position to output
5 4 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
9 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa7 0x01 0x03

Packet Request 0x8e 0xa7 0x01 0x03

GPS Position Packet by Flag Request

Requests the last position matching the provided flags from the specified antenna. If the last position wasn't valid, a no position is output instead

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01PositioningIdSubIDa7
11GPSPositionIdSubID01
21PositionReqTypeIdSubID03
31AntennaIdBYTEnThe id of the antenna to request from
44SetFlagsLONGBitsetReports positions (or no positions) with the given flags set.
84ClearedFlagsLONGBitsetReports positions (or no positions) with the given flags cleared.
124ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
162ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

SetFlags: Reports positions (or no positions) with the given flags set.
BitDescription
Indicates this position was the one used internally for the antenna
Indicates that the position has gone through some form of filtering or adjustiment

ClearedFlags: Reports positions (or no positions) with the given flags cleared.
BitDescription
Indicates this position was the one used internally for the antenna
Indicates that the position has gone through some form of filtering or adjustiment

0x8e 0xa8 0x01

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa8 0x01

VRS Radio: Set NTRIP Params

Set the NTRIP parameters for the VRS Radio. All strings are not null terminated, and the stringLength value is the number of actual characters

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01VRSRadioSubIDa8
11NTRIPParamsSubID01
22IPPortINTEGERnIP Port (Unsigned 16 bit number)
41IPAddrLengthBYTEnString length of the IP Address
5IPAddrLengthIPAddressARRAY of CHAR-NTRIP IP Address (dotted quad, or canonical address)
5 + IPAddrLength1MountPointLengthBYTEnString length of the mount point
6 + IPAddrLengthMountPointLengthMountPointARRAY of CHAR-NTRIP mount point
6 + IPAddrLength + MountPointLength1UserNameLengthBYTEnString length of the userName
7 + IPAddrLength + MountPointLengthUserNameLengthUserNameARRAY of CHAR-NTRIP user name
7 + IPAddrLength + MountPointLength + UserNameLength1PasswordLengthBYTEnString length of the password
8 + IPAddrLength + MountPointLength + UserNameLengthPasswordLengthPasswordARRAY of CHAR-NTRIP password
8 + IPAddrLength + MountPointLength + UserNameLength + PasswordLength1UseForRTXBYTEnIf set to 1, NTRIP settings will be used for RTX modes, otherwise, hard-coded receiver defaults are used.
9 + IPAddrLength + MountPointLength + UserNameLength + PasswordLength7ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
16 + IPAddrLength + MountPointLength + UserNameLength + PasswordLength2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

VRS Radio Config

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01VRS_RadioSubIDa8
11VRS Radio ConfigSubID06
21EnableVRSBYTEn0xFF: no change, 1: enable VRS radio, 0: disable VRS radio
31EnableAutoReportBYTEnIf set to 1, enable automatic VRS status change reports (for port on which this packet was received)

56ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
112ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

StreamType: Indicates stream type for VRS radio
ValueDescription
0Search for serial stream with DCOL protocol configured
1Use dedicated NTRIP client stream
2Use dedicated CLAAS RTK NET modem

Requests the various firmware and hardware version information
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 PicusVersionId SubID 00
2 1 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
3 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa9 0x01 0x00

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa9 0x01 0x00

Picus Upgrade Command

Requests that the receiver begin upgrading with the provided file. The upgrading performed by the receiver will depend on the hardware, but it is expected to begin upgrading if possible. The receiver will respond with information regarding whether the request was carried out.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PicusSLMPIdSubID01
21PicusUpgradeIdSubID00
31ImageTypeBYTEEnumIndicates the type of upgrade image
41FilenameLenBYTEnString length of the filename
5FilenameLenFilenameARRAY of CHAR-The filename of the firmware to upgrade with.
5 + FilenameLen2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

ImageType: Indicates the type of upgrade image
ValueDescription
0The file is a main application.
1The file is a monitor.
2The file is for a peripheral device.

0x8e 0xa9 0x01 0x01

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa9 0x01 0x01

Picus Logging Control Command

Allows the user to start and stop logging, and provide the location to log to.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PicusSLMPIdSubID01
21PicusLoggingControlIdSubID01
31ActionBYTEEnumThe logging action to be performed.
41prefixLenBYTEnString length of the prefix to log to
5prefixLenprefixARRAY of CHAR-The prefix of the file to log to.
5 + prefixLen2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Action: The logging action to be performed.
ValueDescription
0Starts the receiver logging.
1Stops the receiver logging.

0x8e 0xa9 0x01 0x01

Packet Request 0x8e 0xa9 0x01 0x01

0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 PicusSLMPId SubID 01
2 1 PicusLoggingControlId SubID 01
3 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PicusSLMPIdSubID01
21PicusLogDumpIdSubID02
31LogTypeBYTEEnumThe type of log to be dumped to the path.
41FilenameLenBYTEnString length of the filename
5FilenameLenFilenameARRAY of CHAR-The filename of the logs to be dumped.
5 + FilenameLen2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

LogType: The type of log to be dumped to the path.
ValueDescription
0Dump the error log.
1Dump the error log.
2Dump the run-time log.

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa9 0x01 0x03

Sends a variable length data packet (ping), that will be returned by the receiver (pong). This allows a type of serial communication test to occur.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription

01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PicusSLMPIdSubID01
21PicusPingPongIdSubID03
31PingSizeBYTEnThe number of bytes being sent

Frequency: The frequency at which the receiver will automatically reboot
ValueDescription
0Do not automatically reboot on any interval
1Reboot every 5 minutes (5/10/15/20/etc)
2Reboot every 15 minutes (15/30/45/on hour)
3Reboot every 30 minutes (30/on hour)
4Reboot every hour (XX:00)

0x8e 0xa9 0x01 0x07

Packet Request 0x8e 0xa9 0x01 0x07

0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 PicusSLMPId SubID 01
2 1 PicusClaasRtkNetId SubID 07
3 5 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
8 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

Is Up/Downgrade Version Allowed Request

Checks if an upgrade/downgrade to the given version is allowed
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 PicusSLMPId SubID 01
2 1 PicusDowngradeVersionCheckId SubID 31
3 1 MajorVersion BYTE n Major version of the firmware
4 1 MinorVersion BYTE n Minor version of the firmware
5 2 BuildNum INTEGER n Build number of the firmware
7 1 BuildType BYTE n The type of the firmware
8 1 FeatureSpecific BYTE n Whether this is a feature specific build
9 8 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
17 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PolluxHardwareIdSubID02
21PolluxDigOutMuxIdSubID00
31PortIdBYTEEnumThe physical port the Digital output pin is on
42ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

PortId: The physical port the Digital output pin is on
ValueDescription
0PortA (Castor)
1PortB (Castor)
2PortC (Pollux)
3PortD (Pollux)

0x8e 0xa9 0x02 0x00

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa9 0x02 0x00

Pollux Set Digital Out Mux Control Cmd

Sets the mux behaviour of the digital output for a particular port.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PolluxHardwareIdSubID02
21PolluxDigOutMuxIdSubID00
31PortIdBYTEEnumThe physical port the Digital output pin is on
41MuxTypeBYTEEnumHow the digital output of the port should behave
51OutputValueBYTEnSets the Output value when mux is set to Output (0=lo,1=hi,0xff=no change)
63ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
92ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

PortId: The physical port the Digital output pin is on
ValueDescription
0PortA (Castor)
1PortB (Castor)
2PortC (Pollux)
3PortD (Pollux)

MuxType: How the digital output of the port should behave
ValueDescription
0Pin acts as an input (Note: only PortC/D)
1Pin acts as an general output
2Pin acts as a PPS source
4Pin acts as a PWM output for radar and other applications
5Pin acts as an audio output (Note: only PortA/B)

0x8e 0xa9 0x02 0x01

Packet Request 0x8e 0xa9 0x02 0x01

Pollux Digital In Control Req

Request the settings of the digital input of a particular port.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PolluxHardwareIdSubID02
21PolluxDigInIdSubID01
31PortIdBYTEEnumThe physical port the Digital output pin is on (Note: Currently only PortC/D are supported)
42ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

PortId: The physical port the Digital output pin is on (Note: Currently only PortC/D are supported)
ValueDescription
0PortA (Castor)
1PortB (Castor)
2PortC (Pollux)
3PortD (Pollux)

0x8e 0xa9 0x02 0x01

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa9 0x02 0x01

Pollux Set Digital In Control Cmd

Sets the behaviour of the digital input for a particular port.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PolluxHardwareIdSubID02
21PolluxDigInIdSubID01
31PortIdBYTEEnumThe physical port the Digital output pin is on (Note: Currently only PortC/D are supported)
41ThresholdBYTEEnumThe input voltage threshold of the pin
51PullBYTEEnumThe pulling behaviour of the input pin
64ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
102ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

PortId: The physical port the Digital output pin is on (Note: Currently only PortC/D are supported)
ValueDescription
0PortA (Castor)
1PortB (Castor)
2PortC (Pollux)
3PortD (Pollux)

Threshold: The input voltage threshold of the pin
ValueDescription
0A 3.3V threshold is required for the input pin
1A 1.65 threshold is required for the input pin

Pull: The pulling behaviour of the input pin
ValueDescription
0The input does not pull the line
1The input pulls down the line
2The input pulls up the line

0x8e 0xa9 0x02 0x02

Packet Request 0x8e 0xa9 0x02 0x02

Request the settings of the Pollux FPGA GPO.

0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 PolluxHardwareId SubID 02
2 1 PolluxGPOId SubID 02
3 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa9 0x02 0x02

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa9 0x02 0x02

Pollux Set GPO Control Cmd

Sets the behaviour of the GPO for the Pollux FPGA.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PolluxHardwareIdSubID02
21PolluxGPOIdSubID02
31GPOSettingBYTEBitsetThe new value for the Pollux FPGA GPO.
44ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
82ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

GPOSetting: The new value for the Pollux FPGA GPO.
BitDescription
Controls whether Antenna2 has 5V (0) or 7V (1) output to it.
Controls whether power is supplied to the antennas
Controls whether the radio gets 3.4V (0) or 3.6V (1)
Turns LED1 on/off
Turns LED2 on/off

Antenna Power Cmd

Commands the power state of an antenna. The command is acknowledged with the Antenna info packet.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 AntennaId SubID 03
2 1 AntennaPowerId SubID 01
3 1 AntennaId BYTE n Id of the Antenna to be controlled.
4 1 PowerOn BYTE n Any non-zero value sends power to the antenna
5 4 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
9 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

Requests information on the Radar setup
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 RadarId SubID 04
2 1 RadarInfoId SubID 00
3 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa9 0x04 0x00

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa9 0x04 0x00

Radar Settings Cmd

Configures the Radar setup
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 RadarId SubID 04
2 1 RadarInfoId SubID 00
3 1 Enable BYTE n Enable the Radar out functionality (0=disable, 1=enable)
4 4 FreqSpeedRate SINGLE Hz/kph Defines the rate at which the radar frequency (HZ) changes with respect to speed (kph). Note: If set less than zero, the rate will not be changed
8 8 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
16 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa9 0x05

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa9 0x05

Event Log Command

Place a string command into the receiver program log. Responds with a simple ack.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11EventLogIdSubID05
24TimeStampLONGnUser timestamp to print directly in the log. The receiver does not do anything with this data.
61EventTypeBYTEnUser supplied event type. The receiver does not try to understand what this value means.
71LengthBYTEnLength of the message that is being sent.
8LengthLogMessageARRAY of CHAR-The message to place in the program log. This message can not be longer than 240 characters.
8 + Length2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa9 0x06 0x00

Packet Request 0x8e 0xa9 0x06 0x00

ADC Channel Reading Req

Requests the reading of the given ADC Channel
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 ADCId SubID 06
2 1 ADCInfoId SubID 00
3 1 Normalized BYTE n Whether to retrieve converted (0) values, or values normalized (1) between 0.0 and 1.0
4 1 Channel BYTE n Channel to request (0xff for all channels)
5 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

Module-A Port-D Socket Uart Device Cmd

Sets the socket uart device to be used for Port-D on the Module-A. The device must be a TBIP device that supports the socket-uart protocol.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 ModuleAHardwareId SubID 07
2 1 ModuleAPortDDevice SubID 07
3 4 TBIPDeviceType LONG n TBIP device type to be used for Port-D, 0=Unknown, 1=PC, 2=G12, 3=Module-A 4=Freon
7 32 SerialNumber STRING - Serial number of the device to use for Port-D
39 1 UartId BYTE n The uart on the device to use
40 8 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
48 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

LED Control Req

Request the status of the Receiver LEDs (if supported). This request invokes a response TSIP packet: 0x8F 0xA9 0x08 0x00.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11RcvrHardwareIdSubID09
21RcvrIdBYTEEnumSpecifies the receiver for which the LED state must be retrieved
31LEDIdBYTEEnumRetrieve the state of the LED Id
42ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

RcvrId: Specifies the receiver for which the LED state must be retrieved
ValueDescription
17Receiver ID for Ag-372

LEDId: Retrieve the state of the LED Id
ValueDescription
0Retrieve LED 0 state
1Retrieve LED 1 state

0x8e 0xa9 0x09

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa9 0x09

LED Control Cmd

Sets the behaviour of Receiver LEDs. This command is acknowledged with TSIP Packet 0x8F 0xA9 0xA8 0x00.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11RcvrHardwareIdSubID09
21RcvrIdBYTEEnumSpecifies the receiver to which the LED control will apply
31EnableTSIPControlBYTEnWhen set to 1, TSIP controls LED state. Set this to 0 to have another process in the receiver to control the LED state.
41LEDIdBYTEEnumLed ID
51LEDStateBYTEEnumSet the Led State
68ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
142ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

RcvrId: Specifies the receiver to which the LED control will apply
ValueDescription
17Receiver ID for Ag-372

LEDId: Led ID
ValueDescription
0Operate LED 0
1Operate LED 1

LEDState: Set the Led State
ValueDescription
0Turn OFF the LED
1Turn ON the LED

Picus System File Transfer Initiate Command

Initiates transfer operation (get or put) for the given file type.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PicusSysFileOpIdSubID15
21PicusSysFileXferInitiateSubID00
31xferTypeBYTEEnumType of transfer (get or put)
41fileTypeBYTEEnumType of file to transfer
54fileSizeLONGnSize of the file to be transferred (put only)
91clientFileIdSizeBYTEnSize of clientFileId
10clientFileIdSizeclientFileIdARRAY of BYTE-array of bytes that uniquely identifies client file (e.g., its pathname)
10 + clientFileIdSize2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

xferType: Type of transfer (get or put)
ValueDescription
0Get (export) a system file from the server
1Put (import) a system file to the server

fileType: Type of file to transfer
ValueDescription
