Fusion IMU Settings Request

Request the IMU settings. Expects response of 0x8f 0xa9 0x17 0x00.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 FusionOpId SubID 17
2 1 FusionIMUSettings SubID 00
3 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa9 0x17 0x00

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa9 0x17 0x00

Fusion IMU Settings Command

Send IMU settings to configure pitch and roll corrected positions. Expects response of 0x8f 0xa9 0x17 0x00.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 FusionOpId SubID 17
2 1 FusionIMUSettings SubID 00
3 4 AntLevX SINGLE m Antenna lever arm X offset from vehicle position reference point (PRP). Ahead of PRP is positive.
7 4 AntLevY SINGLE m Antenna lever arm Y offset from vehicle PRP. Vehicle right of PRP is positive.
11 4 AntLevZ SINGLE m Antenna lever arm Z offset from vehicle PRP.
15 4 ImuAngleRoll SINGLE radians IMU roll angle offset for the unit's installation orientation.
19 4 ImuAnglePitch SINGLE radians IMU pitch angle offset for the unit's installation orientation.
23 4 ImuAngleYaw SINGLE radians IMU roll yaw offset for the unit's installation orientation.
27 4 ImuLevX SINGLE m IMU lever arm X offset from vehicle PRP.
31 4 ImuLevY SINGLE m IMU lever arm Y offset from vehicle PRP.
35 4 ImuLevZ SINGLE m IMU lever arm Z offset from vehicle PRP.
39 4 ImuRollOffset SINGLE radians Calibration offset for IMU roll.
43 4 ImuPitchOffset SINGLE radians Calibration offset for IMU pitch.
47 12 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
59 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa9 0x17 0x01

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa9 0x17 0x01

Enable or disable IMU-Corrected Positions

Enable Fusion position correction based on IMU values.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8e
0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 FusionOpId SubID 17
2 1 FusionIMUCorrPosEnableOp SubID 01
3 1 EnableIMUCorrection BYTE BOOL 0:Disable position correction. 1: Enable position correction based on IMU yaw, pitch, roll.
4 1 EnableIMUDetailStream BYTE BOOL 0:Disable IMU detail message stream. 1: Enable IMU detail message stream (0x8f 0xa9 0x17 0x02).
5 4 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
9 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xa9 0x17 0x01

0x8e 0xa9 0x17 0x03

Packet Command 0x8e 0xa9 0x17 0x03

Fusion IMU Settings Command, Block 2

Send IMU settings to configure extra settings related to the Fusion IMU supported features. Expects response of 0x8f 0xa9 0x17 0x03.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8e
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11FusionOpIdSubID17
21FusionIMUSettings2SubID03
31ProPointEngineModeBYTEEnumConfigure the ProPoint engine's positioning mode. If the IMU-Positions Correction feature is enabled, this field is ignored. If the mode is GNSS-only, the contents of the packet related to static bench mode is ignored and static bench mode is disabled.
41StaticBenchModeEnableBYTEEnumEnable or disable static bench mode.
54StaticBenchHeadingSINGLEdegreesDesired static bench mode heading.
91VehicleTypeBYTEEnumVehicle type for the ProPoint engine.
1080ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
902ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

ProPointEngineMode: Configure the ProPoint engine's positioning mode. If the IMU-Positions Correction feature is enabled, this field is ignored. If the mode is GNSS-only, the contents of the packet related to static bench mode is ignored and static bench mode is disabled.
ValueDescription
0GNSS-Only Mode
1GNSS+INS (AINS) Mode
255Do not change the existing setting.

StaticBenchModeEnable: Enable or disable static bench mode.
ValueDescription
0Disable static bench mode. The value in StaticBenchHeading is ignored.
1Enable static bench mode
255Do not change the existing setting. When this is used, the value in StaticBenchHeading is ignored.

VehicleType: Vehicle type for the ProPoint engine.
ValueDescription
0Unspecified vehicle type
1Land mapping vehicle
2Automotive vehicle
3Non-tracking vehicle
4Offroad vehicle
5Slow motion vehicle, such as most tractors.
255Do not change the existing setting.

1 1 CryptoChipId SubID 18

58ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
132ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

AntennaType: Type of antenna connected to receiver

0x8e 0xab 0x04

Packet Command 0x8e 0xab 0x04

Centerpoint RTX FastRestart Confirm Vehicle Movement

Allows TSIP to confirm whether vehicle has moved post reboot on FastRestart enabled Ag receivers. The command is acknowledged with packet 0x8f 0xab 0x04.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppID ID 8e
0 1 CenterpointRTXID SubID ab
1 1 CenterPointRTXFastRestartID SubID 04
2 1 HasVehicleMoved BYTE 0 User confirms whether the vehicle has moved or not. (0 = Vehicle has not moved. 1 = Yes, Vehicle has moved, Fast Restart will be cancelled)
3 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xab 0x05

Packet Command 0x8e 0xab 0x05

Centerpoint RTX Output Settings

Allows the RTX output settings to be selected using TSIP. The command is acknowledged with packet 0x8f 0xab 0x05.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8e
01CenterpointRTXIDSubIDab
11CenterPointRTXOutDatumIDSubID05
21RTXOutputDatumBYTEEnumUser-selected output datum for RTX position
313RTXOffsetRTXOffset-Custom RTX offset
1651ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
672ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

RTXOutputDatum: User-selected output datum for RTX position
ValueDescription
0ITRF2008 Datum
1NAD83 Datum
2ETRS89 Datum
3GDA94 Datum
4SIRGAS-CON 2016.0 Datum
5ITRF1988 Datum
6ITRF1989 Datum
7ITRF1990 Datum
8ITRF1991 Datum
9ITRF1992 Datum
10ITRF1993 Datum
11ITRF1994 Datum
12ITRF1996 Datum
13ITRF1997 Datum
14ITRF2000 Datum
15ITRF2005 Datum
16NAD83CSRS Datum
17NAD83-CORS96 Datum
18ETRF2000-R05 Datum
19SIRGAS2000 Datum
20SIRGAS95 Datum
21NAD83 2011 Datum
22NAD83 MA11 Datum
23NAD83 PA11 Datum
24RGF93v2 Datum
25CGCS2000 Datum
26NZGD2000 Datum
27JGD2000 Datum
28POSGAR07 Datum
29RGAF09 Datum
30RGFG95 Datum
31RGNC9193 Datum
32RGR92 Datum
33RGTAAF07 Datum
34MAGNASIRGAS Datum
35EST97 Datum
36ITRF2014 Datum
37GDA2020 Datum
38NAD83-CSRS 1997.0 Datum
39NAD83-CSRS 2002.0 Datum
40NAD83-CSRS 2010.0 Datum
246ITRF2014_2021 Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
247Legacy_SIRGASCON Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
248ITRF2014_CurrentEpoch Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
249Legacy_GDA94 Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
250Legacy_ITRF2000 Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
251Legacy_ETRF2000_R05 Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
252Legacy_ETRS89 Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
253Legacy_ETRS89 Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
254Automatic Datum (The picus receiver automatically determines the right datum to use depending on the receiver location.
255Do not change this setting

RTXOffset: Custom RTX offset
Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
01enabledBYTEboolUser-selected RTX offset is enabled
14eastSINGLEmetersUser-selected RTX offset in ENU (East)
54northSINGLEmetersUser-selected RTX offset in ENU (North)
94upSINGLEmetersUser-selected RTX offset in ENU (Up)

0x8e 0xab 0x05

Packet Request 0x8e 0xab 0x05

Centerpoint RTX Output Settings

Requests the current RTX output settings using TSIP. The request is acknowledged with packet 0x8f 0xab 0x05.

0 1 CenterpointRTXID SubID ab
1 1 CenterPointRTXOutDatumID SubID 05
2 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xab 0x06

Packet Command 0x8e 0xab 0x06

RTK/RTX Select Best Mode

If RTK is unlocked, function automatically determines what Centerpoint RTX (FAST/STD) to be used based on what Centerpoint license is available. If RTK and/or RTX unlock(s) are not found, convergence is autoamtically set to SBAS. The request is acknowledged with packet 0x8f 0xab 0x06.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppID ID 8e
0 1 CenterpointRTXID SubID ab
1 1 RTK/RTXBestTypeSelection SubID 06
2 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xab 0x07

Packet Request 0x8e 0xab 0x07

MSS Mode Request

Request the current configuration and mode of the MSS Mode Switch

0 1 CenterpointRTXID SubID ab
1 1 MSSModeID SubID 07
2 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8e 0xab 0x07

Packet Command 0x8e 0xab 0x07

MSS Mode Command

Control the MSS Mode Switch

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8e
01CenterpointRTXIDSubIDab
11MSSModeIDSubID07
21MSSControlModeBYTEEnumMode selection
32ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

MSSControlMode: Mode selection
ValueDescription
0Automatically configure the MSS Mode switch to narrowband or wideband based on the requested RTX frequency
1Set the filter bank to pass 1525MHz to 1559MHz
2Set the filter bank to pass 1555MHz to 1559MHz

11RTK/RTX Custom OffsetsSubID08

1Return with Test Pattern 1
2Return with Test Pattern 2
3Return with Test Pattern 3
4Disable Requested

-1AppIDID8e
01Boom HeightSubIDac

31APIversionBYTE0API version

58targetHeightDOUBLEmeters
131sprayerSuspensionTypeBYTEEnumEAeroSprayerSuspensionType
141sensingModeBYTEEnumEAeroSensingModeType
151groundSensitivityBYTEEnumsensor sensitivity setting
161canopySensitivityBYTEEnumsensor sensitivity setting

181canopyFilterBYTEEnumEAeroSensorCanopyFilterType
198chevronThreshDOUBLE
278maximumHeightDOUBLEmeters
358minimumHeightDOUBLEmeters
438minSafeHeightBelowTargetDOUBLEmeters
518heightStepDOUBLEmeters
592aggressivenessINTEGER%
612downSlewLimINTEGER
632downGainStabilizerINTEGER
658wingPhasingHeightThresholdDOUBLELow side height error threshold for additional dampening. 0 is disabled.
731useKatoBYTEboolSwitch Input enable
744autoDisableTimeoutLONGsecondsTimeout when enable boom on runscreen revert to disabled
7820ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
982ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

sprayerSuspensionType: EAeroSprayerSuspensionType
ValueDescription
-1Undefined
0Normal
1Loose
2Limp
3Tight

sensingMode: EAeroSensingModeType
ValueDescription
-1Undefined
0Canopy
1Ground

groundSensitivity: sensor sensitivity setting

0Normal
1Low
2High

canopySensitivity: sensor sensitivity setting
ValueDescription
0Normal
1Low
2High

88 4 txHiPriorityByte LONG n txHiPriorityByte
92 4 txLateCollision LONG n txLateCollision
96 4 txPausePkts LONG n txPausePkts
100 4 txBroadcastPkts LONG n txBroadcastPkts
104 4 txMulticastPackets LONG n txMulticastPackets
108 4 txUnicastPackets LONG n txUnicastPackets
112 4 txDeferred LONG n txDeferred
116 4 txTotalCollision LONG n txTotalCollision
120 4 txExcessiveCollision LONG n txExcessiveCollision

0xb0 0x00

Packet Request 0xb0 0x00

PPS Signal Configuration Req

Requests the PPS configuration settings. The response is sent in 0xB0 0x80.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 PPSId ID b0
0 1 PPSConfigId SubID 00
1 1 PPSNumber BYTE n Currently supports the output of one PPS signal (PPSNumber=1)
2 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0xb0 0x00

Packet Command 0xb0 0x00

PPS Signal Configuration Cmd

Sets the PPS configuration settings. The command is acknowleged with a 0xB0 0x80 response.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1PPSIdIDb0
01PPSConfigIdSubID00
11PPSNumberBYTEnCurrently supports the output of one PPS signal (PPSNumber=1)
21EnableFlagBYTEnEnabls or disables the specified PPS signal (0=Disabled, 1=Enable Internal/External, 2=Enable Internal Only )
31PPSTimebaseBYTEEnumSets the PPS timebase to use the GPS or UTC time of the week, or the receivers internal clock (user-defined)
41PPSPolarityBYTEEnumSets the polarity of the specified PPS signal.
51AutoReportBYTEnIdentifies whether or not the Report Packet 0xB0 0x82 is automatically generated for the PP signal and set to the remote device.
64ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
108FrequencyDOUBLEHzSupports 1Hz or the 10Hz PPS (Pulse Per Second) output rate
188OffsetDOUBLEsPPS output signal offset in seconds. The Polarity of the PPS signal is offset by the specified number of seconds. Negative polarity causes the signal to output sooner, and positive polarity causes the signal to output later.
264MaxUncThresholdSINGLEsMaximum allowable clock uncertainty threshold in seconds. If the Maximum Uncertainty Threshold is set to a large value (e.g. 1 second), the GPS receiver continues to produce a PPS signal even when a position fix is not available (satellites are not tracked). If the Maximum Uncertainty Threshold is set to a small value (e.g. 1ms), the PPS signal is disabled a few seconds after a position is no longer available (satellites are no longer tracked). The PPS signal drifts when a position fix is not available and snaps back to the correct position on ce a position fix becomes available again.
302ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

PPSTimebase: Sets the PPS timebase to use the GPS or UTC time of the week, or the receivers internal clock (user-defined)
ValueDescription
0GPS Time
1UTC (Universal Time Coordinated)
2Receiver's internal clock (user-defined)

PPSPolarity: Sets the polarity of the specified PPS signal.
ValueDescription
0Negative polarity
1Positive polarity

0xb0 0x01

Packet Command 0xb0 0x01

PPS Signal Enable/Disable Cmd

Enables or disables the specified PPS signal. The command is acknowledged with a 0xB0 0x81 response.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 PPSId ID b0
0 1 PPSEnabledId SubID 01
1 1 PPSNumber BYTE n Currently supports the output of one PPS signal (PPSNumber=1)
2 1 EnableFlag BYTE n Enabls or disables the specified PPS signal (0=Disabled, 1=Enable Internal/External, 2=Enable Internal Only)
3 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1RcvrCfgIdIDbbPrimary Receiver Configuration Block
01RcvrCfgBlockIdSubID00
11OperatingDimensionBYTEEnumOperating Dimension
21DGPSModeBYTEEnumDifferential GPS mode
31DynamicsCodeBYTEEnumVehicle dynamics code
41SolutionModeBYTEEnumSolution mode
54ElevetionMaskSINGLE0-PI/2Lowest satellite elevation for fixes in radians
94AMUMaskSINGLEAMUsMinimum signal level for fixes
134PDOPSINGLE-Maximum PDOP for fixes
174PDOPSwitchSINGLE-Selects 2D/3D transition mode when the receiver is operating in auto 2D/3D mode
211DGPSAgeLimitBYTEnMaximum time to use a DGPS correction, in seconds
221FoliageModeBYTEEnumFoliage mode usage
231LowPowerModeBYTEEnumLow power mode
241ClockHoldModeBYTEEnumClock hold mode
251MeasurementRateBYTEEnumMeasurement rate
261PosFixRateBYTEEnumPosition fix rate
2713ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use

OperatingDimension: Operating Dimension
ValueDescription
0Automatic
1Time Only (1 SV)
3Horizontal (2D)
4Full Position (3D)
5DGPS Reference
62D Clock Hold
7Overdetermined Clock

DGPSMode: Differential GPS mode
ValueDescription
0DGPS off
1DGPS only
3DGPS auto

DynamicsCode: Vehicle dynamics code
ValueDescription
0Land
2Sea
3Air
4Stationary

SolutionMode: Solution mode
ValueDescription
1Overdetermined fix
2Weighted overdetermined fix

FoliageMode: Foliage mode usage
ValueDescription
0Never use foliage mode
1Sometimes use foliage mode
2Always use foliage mode

LowPowerMode: Low power mode
ValueDescription
0Disable (recommended)
1Auto

ClockHoldMode: Clock hold mode
ValueDescription
0Disable
1Auto

MeasurementRate: Measurement rate
ValueDescription
01 Hz
15 Hz
210 Hz

PosFixRate: Position fix rate
ValueDescription
01 Hz
15 Hz
210 Hz
3Position at measurement rate

0xbc

Packet Request 0xbc

Serial Port Configuration Parameters Request

Requests the configuration of a particular serial port

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1PortConfigIdIDbc
01PortBYTEEnumThe serial port for the request

Port: The serial port for the request
ValueDescription
0Serial Port A
1Serial Port B (not available on Echo)
2Serial Port C (not available on Orca/Echo)
3Serial Port D
253Second alternate port (not available on Orca/Echo)
254First alternate port (not available on Echo)
255The currently connected port

0xbc

Packet Command 0xbc

Serial Port Configuration Parameters Command

Sets the configuration of a particular serial port. This includes the port settings and input and output protocols.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1PortConfigIdIDbc
01PortBYTEEnumThe serial port for the request
11InputBaudRateBYTEEnumInput baud rate to be configured
21OutputBaudRateBYTEEnumOutput baud rate to be configured
31DataBitsBYTEEnumNumber of Data Bits to be configured
41ParityBYTEEnumParity to be configured
51StopBitsBYTEEnumNumber of Stop Bits to be configured
61FlowControlBYTEEnumFlow control to be configured
71InputProtocolBYTEEnumInput protocol to be configured
81OutputProtocolBYTEEnumOutput protocol to be configured
91ProtocolOperationBYTEnSpecial protocol operation modes

Port: The serial port for the request
ValueDescription
0Serial Port A
1Serial Port B (not available on Echo)
2Serial Port C (not available on Orca/Echo)
3Serial Port D
253Second alternate port (not available on Orca/Echo)
254First alternate port (not available on Echo)
255The currently connected port

InputBaudRate: Input baud rate to be configured
ValueDescription
0Don't configure baud rate
1110 bps
2300 bps
3600 bps
41200 bps
52400 bps
64800 bps
79600 bps
819200 bps
938400 bps
1057600 bps
11115200 bps

OutputBaudRate: Output baud rate to be configured
ValueDescription
0Don't configure baud rate
1110 bps
2300 bps
3600 bps
41200 bps
52400 bps
64800 bps
79600 bps
819200 bps
938400 bps
1057600 bps
11115200 bps

DataBits: Number of Data Bits to be configured
ValueDescription
05 data bits
16 data bits
27 data bits
38 data bits

Parity: Parity to be configured
ValueDescription
0No Parity
1Odd Parity
2Even Parity

StopBits: Number of Stop Bits to be configured
ValueDescription
01 bit for 6–8 data bits or 1.5 bits for 5 data bits
12 bits

FlowControl: Flow control to be configured
ValueDescription
0RTS/CTS enabled
1Transmit XON/XOFF
2Transmit XANY
3Receive XON/XOFF

InputProtocol: Input protocol to be configured
ValueDescription
0No Input Protocol
1TAIP Input Protocol
2TSIP Input Protocol
4NMEA Input Protocol
8RTCM Input Protocol
16DCOL (RTK Link) Input Protocol
32LBAR Input Protocol
64CMR Input Protocol
65NCS Input Protocol
80Omnistar Demodulated Data Input Protocol
82AutomaticRTK Input Protocol - RTCM/CMR(+)/CMRx/CMRxe

OutputProtocol: Output protocol to be configured
ValueDescription
0No Output Protocol
1TAIP Output Protocol
2TSIP Output Protocol
4NMEA Output Protocol
8RTCM Output Protocol
8RTCM Bent Pipe Output Protocol
16DCOL(RTK Link) Output Protocol
32LBAR Output Protocol
64CMR Output Protocol
70Output ASCII from Port A Protocol
71Output ASCII from Port B Protocol
81Omnistar HP Debug Output Protocol

1

Autopilot Boot Monitor packet

Used to reset the steering controller on the receiver. The AGL currently first sends the SwitchToApp command, followed by ReturnMode after a delay.

01BootMonSubIdSubID46
11SwitchModePageIdSubID00
21CommandBYTEEnum
3END-3--
3 + END-32ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Command: 
ValueDescription
0Performs the 2nd part of the controller reset
1
2Resets the system after a delay to make sure flash changes are saved/reloaded

0xbe 0x4c 0x01 0x19

Packet Command 0xbe 0x4c 0x01 0x19

Field Computer Information Command

This packet should be sent from the Field Computer to the Controller when communication is established. It allows the controller to identify what hardware it is connected to and allows the Field Computer (from the Controller's response) to know what sort of Controller the Field Computer is connected to
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AutosteerCmdId ID be
0 1 ExtDevSubId SubID 4c
1 1 FieldComputerPageId SubID 01
2 1 FieldComputerInformationCmdId SubID 19
3 1 ManufacturerId BYTE n
4 1 DisplayId BYTE n
5 1 ProductId BYTE n
6 4 FirmwareVersion SINGLE -
10 8 Reserved BYTE 0 Reserved for future use
18 END-18 - -
18 + END-18 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

Packet Command 0xbe 0x4c 0x01 0x1a

ValueDescription

AutosteerAllowed: Autosteer allowed status

1Autosteering is allowed

Clothoid New Path Command

New Path command starts a new guidance path. Set points to be interpreted as Polyline or Clothoid points

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AutopilotRespIdIDbe
01external deviceSubID4c
11Clothoid APISubID23
21New pathSubID00
31typeBYTEEnumDefine how to use points in clothoid AppendPath packet
4END-4--
4 + END-42ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

type: Define how to use points in clothoid AppendPath packet
ValueDescription
0Clothoid points have XYZ and distance, heading, curvature

0xbe 0x4c 0x23 0x01

Packet Command 0xbe 0x4c 0x23 0x01

Clothoid Append Path Command

Extend the guidance path by 1 point. Each point has a position XYZ and optional parameters for Clothoids
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AutosteerCmdId ID be
0 1 external device SubID 4c
1 1 Clothoid API SubID 23
2 1 Append path SubID 01
3 4 Sequence_Number LONG n starts at 0. Incremented for every point on path
7 8 X DOUBLE - Point position X in ECEF (meters)
15 8 Y DOUBLE - Point position Y in ECEF (meters)
23 8 Z DOUBLE - Point position Z in ECEF (meters)
31 4 Heading SINGLE - Path tangent angle in clockwise degrees from north
35 4 Speed SINGLE - Target speed (m/s)
39 END-39 - -
39 + END-39 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0xbe 0x4c 0x23 0x02

Packet Request 0xbe 0x4c 0x23 0x02

0 1 external device SubID 4c
1 1 Clothoid API SubID 23
2 1 Activate Clothoid Guidance SubID 02

0 1 external device SubID 4c
1 1 Clothoid API SubID 23
2 1 Activate Clothoid Guidance SubID 02

0xbe 0x4c 0x80 0x00

Packet Command 0xbe 0x4c 0x80 0x00

Autopilot External Device FFA Button Press command

Informs the NAV that a Nextswath related button was pressed on the display

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AutopilotCmdIdIDbe
01ExtDevSubIdSubID4c
11FfaIdSubID80
21FfaButtonPressIdSubID00
31ReservedBYTEn
41FfaButtonBYTEEnumThe Ffa button that was pressed
5END-5--
5 + END-52ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

FfaButton: The Ffa button that was pressed
ValueDescription
0Enables/Disables Nextswath
1Deprecated
2Enables/Disables Trueswath
3Move the target swath left
4Move the target swath right
5Enables/Disables end of row turn actions
6Enables/Disables start of row turn actions
7Declines an end of row turn
8Accepts an end of row turn
9Also accepts an end of row turn
10Accepts the nextswath liability warning
11Declines the nextswath liability warning
12Switches to automatic turns mode
13Switches to manual turns mode
14Sets the starting swath of a block pattern
15Switches to standard pattern mode
16Switches to block pattern mode
17Switches to alternating block pattern mode
18Switches to continuous block pattern mode

0xbe 0x4c 0x80 0x01

Packet Command 0xbe 0x4c 0x80 0x01

0 1 ExtDevSubId SubID 4c
1 1 FfaId SubID 80
2 1 FfaButtonPressId SubID 01
3 1 InnerHeadlandExists BYTE BOOL
4 1 OuterHeadlandExists BYTE BOOL
5 1 BoundaryCount BYTE n
6 1 NumberOfExclusionZones BYTE n
7 END-7 - -

Autopilot Publish Parameter Block OPS Config Information Packet

Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description

0 1 TsipParamBlkId SubID 71 Sub Id
1 1 TsipParamPageId SubID 02 Page Id - OPS Config Info
2 1 TsipParamSet SubID 01 Store/Set Command
3 4 LookAheadTime SINGLE - Look Ahead Time.
7 1 SwathChangeoverPoint BYTE n Swath Changeover Point.
8 4 SteeringAggressiveness SINGLE - Steering Aggressiveness.
12 4 NudgeIncrement SINGLE - Nudge Increment.
16 4 ImplementDraftIncrement SINGLE - Implement Draft Increment (step size in meters).
20 4 ImplementDraftOffset SINGLE - Implement Draft Offset (current total offset in meters).
24 8 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
32 END-32 - -

3 + END-3 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0xbe 0x80 0x00

Packet Command 0xbe 0x80 0x00

Autopilot Full Field Automation Command to Autopilot

FFA command to Autopilot Navigation Controller.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AutopilotCmdIdIDbe
01FFASubIdSubID80
11FFAHeartBeatIdSubID00
21VersionBYTEnVersion.
31ButtonBYTEEnumWhich button was pressed.

Button: Which button was pressed.
ValueDescription
0NextSwath On/Off
1Turn On/Off
2TrueSwath On/Off
3Left
4Right
5End of Row Sequence
6Start of Row Sequence
7Dismiss
8Auto Turn at End of Row
9FFA Engage
10Accept Liability
11Decline Liability

0xc5 0x00

Packet Request 0xc5 0x00

Satellite Constellation Request

Requests satellite constellation report from receiver. One or more 0xd5 0x00 packets are sent in response.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 MultiSystemGNSSId ID c5
0 1 SatConstellationId SubID 00
1 1 AntennaId BYTE n ID of antenna for which contellation report is requested (0 = primary, 1 = secondary, 255 = antenna associated with current port)
2 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0xc5 0x01

Packet Command 0xc5 0x01

Satellite System Selection

Set which satellite systems are enabled/disabled

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1MultiSystemGNSSIdIDc5
01SetSatSystemSelectIDSubID01
12SatelliteSystemControlFlagINTEGERBitsetBitfield that indicates which satellite system is enabled/disabled, if bits are set then it means the satellite system is enabled, which bit to set corresponds to value in GNSSSystemType
32ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

SatelliteSystemControlFlag: Bitfield that indicates which satellite system is enabled/disabled, if bits are set then it means the satellite system is enabled, which bit to set corresponds to value in GNSSSystemType
BitDescription
0GPS - Global Positioning System
1SBAS - Space Based Augmentation System (WAAS, EGNOS, etc.)
2GLONASS - Global Orbiting Navigational Satellite System
3Galileo Navigation Satellite System
4QZSS - Quasi-Zenith Satellite System
5BeiDou Navigation Satellite System
6Indian Regional Navigation Satellite System
7Mobile Satellite Services - RTX delivered via geo-stationary L-band satellites

0xc5 0x02

Packet Request 0xc5 0x02

Satellite System Selection Request

Request report which satellite system is enabled/disabled
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 MultiSystemGNSSId ID c5
0 1 SatSystemSelectID SubID 02
1 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0xc6 0x00 0x00

Packet Command 0xc6 0x00 0x00

TBIP Authenticated Region Protection Command

TBIP authenticated command to set the allowed GeoFencing Regions.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1TBIPAuthenticatedMessageIdIDc6
01DomainRegionProtectionIdSubID00
11CmdRegionProtectSubIdSubID00
22KeyIndexINTEGERnIndex of the TBIP key to use
42DeviceTypeINTEGERnTBIP Device Type of the sender
632SerialNumberSTRING-Serial number of the sending device
3816ChallengeResponseARRAY of BYTE-The TBIP challenge response for the sending device
544RegionProtectMaskLONGBitsetBit Field specifying regions in which positioning is disabled.
5816ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
7416DigestARRAY of BYTE-Digest used to authenticate the message
902ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

RegionProtectMask: Bit Field specifying regions in which positioning is disabled.
BitDescription

0xc6 0x01 0x00

Packet Command 0xc6 0x01 0x00

TBIP Authenticated SecureRTK Base Authorization Command

TBIP authenticated command to set list of authorized SecureRTK bases.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1TBIPAuthenticatedMessageIdIDc6
01DomainSecureRTKIdSubID01
11CmdSecureRTKBaseAuthSubIdSubID00
22KeyIndexINTEGERnIndex of the TBIP key to use
42DeviceTypeINTEGERnTBIP Device Type of the sender
632SerialNumberSTRING-Serial number of the sending device
3816ChallengeResponseARRAY of BYTE-The TBIP challenge response for the sending device
5435AuthorizedBaseArrayARRAY of AuthorizedBaseEntry-Authorized base array
8916ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
10516DigestARRAY of BYTE-Digest used to authenticate the message
1212ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

AuthorizedBaseEntry: Authorized base array entry
Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
01IsValidBYTEnIndicates whether or not this entry is valid (1 = valid, 0 = invalid)
14AccessControlIdLONGnAccess control ID for base
52ExpiryGPSDayINTEGERnAccess expires immediately following this GPS day (0xFFFF = never expires)

0xc6 0x02 0x00

Packet Command 0xc6 0x02 0x00

TBIP Authenticated GPS Fix Engine Output Authorization Command

TBIP authenticated command to authorize the output of GPS fix engines (per antenna) by accuracy level.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1TBIPAuthenticatedMessageIdIDc6
01FixEngineOutputAuthorizationMessageIdSubID02
11CmdSetFixEngineOutputAuthSubIdSubID00
22KeyIndexINTEGERnIndex of the TBIP key to use
42DeviceTypeINTEGERnTBIP Device Type of the sender
632SerialNumberSTRING-Serial number of the sending device
3816ChallengeResponseARRAY of BYTE-The TBIP challenge response for the sending device
544AuthorizedFixEngineArrayARRAY of BYTE Enum Authorized fix engine for each GPS antenna by accuracy level
5816ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
7416DigestARRAY of BYTE-Digest used to authenticate the message
902ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

AuthorizedFixEngineAccuracyLevel: Indicates the maximum fix engine accuracy level authorized for output
ValueDescription
0No fix engines are authorized for output
1Basic accuracy fix engines are authorized for output
2Intermediate or lower accuracy fix engines are authorized for output
3High accuracy or lower fix engines are authorized for output

0xc6 0x04 0x00

Packet Command 0xc6 0x04 0x00

632SerialNumberSTRING-Serial number of the sending device
3816ChallengeResponseARRAY of BYTE-The TBIP challenge response for the sending device
541AuthorizedRTXAdvDatumBYTEBitsetBit Field specifying if RTX Advanced Datum is disabled.
5516ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
7116DigestARRAY of BYTE-Digest used to authenticate the message
872ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

AuthorizedRTXAdvDatum: Bit Field specifying if RTX Advanced Datum is disabled.
BitDescription

0xc7 0x00

Packet Command 0xc7 0x00

Lock or Unlock Stinger Channels

Lock or Unlock all stinger channels. The action is persistent unless a command forces the existing state to change. This packet is for diagnostic purposes only and may change without notice. Acknowledge Packet: 0xD7 0x00
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 StingerAppID ID c7
0 1 StingerSubId SubID 00
1 2 ChannelInformation INTEGER n 0xFFFF to modify behavior of all stinger channels.
3 1 LockChannel BYTE n A value of 1 locks the channel, value of 0 unlocks the channel
4 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0xc7 0x01

Packet Request 0xc7 0x01

Request RF Band and Filter Switches

Request setting of Band and Filter switches for a signal.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1StingerIDc7
01StingerSubIdSubID01
11Band_SelectBYTEEnumSelect band for status.
22ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Band_Select: Select band for status.
ValueDescription
0Select MSS Band filters
1Select L2 Band filters

0xc7 0x01

Packet Command 0xc7 0x01

Set RF Band and Filter Switches

Set Band and Filter switches for a signal. 0xd7 0x01 is the acknowledge packet.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1StingerIDc7
01StingerSubIdSubID01
11Band_SelectBYTEEnumSelect band filter to modify.
21Filter_SelectBYTEEnumSelect filter option (wide / narrow).
310ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
132ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Band_Select: Select band filter to modify.
ValueDescription
0Select MSS Band filters
1Select L2 Band filters

Filter_Select: Select filter option (wide / narrow).
ValueDescription
0Select automatic mode (not yet supported).
1Select full MSS / LG2 band.
2Select lower MSS or L2 (GPS only) band.

Response Packets

Index


0x00

Packet Response 0x00

TSIP Generic Response

This is used so we can get to the raw data

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1TSIPDummyID00
0END-0PacketDataARRAY of BYTE-Data bytes of the TSIP packet

0x13

Packet Response 0x13

TSIP Parsing Error Notification

Packet 0x13 is sent to notify the calling software when the receiver cannot parse the data sent in a command packet. The contents of the problem packet are included in the report.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1TSIPParsingErrorIdID13
01PacketIdBYTEnId of the TSIP packet that failed to be parsed
1END-1PacketDataARRAY of BYTE-Data bytes of the TSIP packet

0x1a 0x01

Packet Response 0x1a 0x01

TSIP Wrapped NMEA

Wraps NMEA messages for output in a TSIP stream. Command Packet 0x7A 0x07 configures the output of the NMEA wrapped data on a particular port.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1TSIPWrappedDataIdID1a
01TSIPWrappedNMEAIdSubID01
11NumBytesBYTEnNumber of characters in the NMEA message, including $ and <CR><LF>
2NumBytesNMEADataARRAY of CHAR-The NMEA message

0x1a 0x02 0x00

Packet Response 0x1a 0x02 0x00

TSIP DCOL Pipe Control Response

Indicates current settings for forwarding DCOL packets to connected port

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1TSIPWrappedDataIdID1a
01TSIPWrappedDCOLIdSubID02
11DCOLPipeControlIdSubID00
21NumTypesBYTEnNumber of DCOL message types that are being forwarded
3NumTypesDCOLTypesARRAY of BYTE-Array of DCOL message types that are being forwarded

0x1a 0x02 0x01

Packet Response 0x1a 0x02 0x01

TSIP DCOL Pipe Data (Response)

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1TSIPWrappedDataIdID1a
01TSIPWrappedDCOLIdSubID02
11DCOLPipeDataIdSubID01
21NumBytesBYTEnNumber of bytes in wrapped DCOL message
3NumBytesDCOLMessageARRAY of BYTE-DCOL message

0x1a 0x03 0x00

Packet Response 0x1a 0x03 0x00

TSIP CAN pipe message Control (Response)

TSIP wrapped CAN message

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1TSIPWrappedDataIdID1a
01TSIPWrappedCANIdSubID03
11CANPipeControlIdSubID00
21CanPortBYTEEnumCAN port the CAN IDs are registered on
33ReservedBYTE0Reserved for future use
61NumIDsBYTEnNumber of CAN IDs that are being registered for forward
74 * NumIDsCanIdsARRAY of LONG-Array of CAN IDs that are being registered for forward (0 - 50 IDs)

CanPort: CAN port the CAN IDs are registered on
ValueDescription
0default
1CAN A
2CAN B

0x1a 0x03 0x01

Packet Response 0x1a 0x03 0x01

TSIP CAN pipe message (from CAN device)

TSIP wrapped CAN message forwarded from device

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1TSIPWrappedDataIdID1a
01TSIPWrappedCANIdSubID03
11CANPipeDataIdSubID01
23ReservedBYTE0Reserved for future use
54CanIdLONGnCAN Id of the message
91CanMsgLengthBYTEnLength of the CAN message
10CanMsgLengthCanMsgARRAY of BYTE-The CAN message (max length 8 bytes)

0x40

Packet Response 0x40

Almanac Data for Single Satellite Report

reports the almanac data for a single satellite
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AlmanacDataId ID 40 Almanac data for single satellite report
0 1 SatNumber BYTE n 0 = All Satellites. 1-32 = specific PRN number
1 4 T_zc SINGLE seconds The Z count time when the almanac was received, normally positive. If no almanac data is available, this is negative.
5 2 Week INTEGER weeks GPS week number at the time the almanac was received
7 4 Eccentricity SINGLE - Refer to ICD-GPS-200 specification
11 4 T_oa SINGLE seconds Refer to ICD-GPS-200 specification
15 4 i_o SINGLE radians Refer to ICD-GPS-200 specification
19 4 Omega_dot SINGLE radians/sec Refer to ICD-GPS-200 specification
23 4 sqrt_A SINGLE sqrt(meters) Refer to ICD-GPS-200 specification
27 4 Omega_o SINGLE radians Refer to ICD-GPS-200 specification
31 4 Omega SINGLE radians Refer to ICD-GPS-200 specification
35 4 m_o SINGLE radians Refer to ICD-GPS-200 specification

0x41

Packet Response 0x41

GPS Time Report

Reports the current GPS time of week and the week number.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 GPSTimeRptId ID 41
0 4 Time SINGLE seconds GPS time of week.
4 2 Week INTEGER weeks GPS week number.
6 4 Offset SINGLE seconds UTC/GPS time offset.

0x42

Packet Response 0x42

Float-Precision XYZ Position Fix

Reports the current GPS position fix in XYZ ECEF coordinate components. Note also that this packet assumes double-precision time of fix, since 5Hz and faster fix rates are now the norm.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 FltPrecXYZPosFixId ID 42
0 4 X SINGLE meters X coordinate component along the x-axis of ECEF coordinate system.
4 4 Y SINGLE meters Y coordinate component along the y-axis of ECEF coordinate system.
8 4 Z SINGLE meters Z coordinate component along the z-axis of ECEF coordinate system.
12 8 TimeOfFix DOUBLE seconds Double-precision GPS time or UTC, as selected by the I/O timing option

0x43

Packet Response 0x43

Velocity Fix (XYZ Cartesian ECEF) Report

Reports the current GPS velocity fix in XYZ ECEF coordinate. The I/O velocity option must be set to XYZ ECEF for this packet to be generated. Note also that this packet assumes double-precision time of fix, since 5Hz and faster fix rates are now the norm.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 XYZVelocityFixId ID 43
0 4 X SINGLE meters/sec Velocity of vehicle along x-axis of XYZ Cartesian ECEF coordinate system.
4 4 Y SINGLE meters/sec Velocity of vehicle along y-axis of XYZ Cartesian ECEF coordinate system.
8 4 Z SINGLE meters/sec Velocity of vehicle along z-axis of XYZ Cartesian ECEF coordinate system.
12 4 BiasRate SINGLE meters/sec Bias rate
16 8 TimeOfFix DOUBLE seconds Double-precision GPS time or UTC, as selected by the I/O timing option

0x45

Packet Response 0x45

Receiver Firmware Information Report

Provides information about the version of firmware in the Navigation and Signal Processors, and can provide information about the receiver configuration. The receiver sends this packet containing the software versions only after a power-on or reset and in response to Command Packet 0x1F.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 FirmwareInfoRspId ID 45
0 1 NavProcMajVersion BYTE n Major version of the NAV Processor
1 1 NavProcMinVersion BYTE n Minor version of the NAV Processor
2 1 NavProcMonth BYTE n Month of year (1–12) when NAV Processor firmware released
3 1 NavProcDay BYTE n Day of month (1–31) when NAV Processor firmware released
4 1 NavProcYear BYTE n Year when NAV Processor firmware released, minus 1900
5 1 SigProcMajVersion BYTE n Major version of the SIG Processor
6 1 SigProcMinVersion BYTE n Minor version of the SIG Processor
7 1 SigProcMonth BYTE n Month of year (1–12) when SIG Processor firmware released
8 1 SigProcDay BYTE n Day of month (1–31) when SIG Processor firmware released
9 1 SigProcYear BYTE n Year when SIG Processor firmware released, minus 1900

0x45

Packet Response 0x45

Extended Receiver Firmware Information Report

Provides information about the version of firmware in the Navigation and Signal Processors, and can provide information about the receiver configuration. The receiver sends this packet containing the software versions only after a power-on or reset and in response to Command Packet 0x1F.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1FirmwareInfoRspIdID45
01NavProcMajVersionBYTEnMajor version of the NAV Processor
11NavProcMinVersionBYTEnMinor version of the NAV Processor
21NavProcMonthBYTEnMonth of year (1–12) when NAV Processor firmware released
31NavProcDayBYTEnDay of month (1–31) when NAV Processor firmware released
41NavProcYearBYTEnYear when NAV Processor firmware released, minus 1900
51SigProcMajVersionBYTEnMajor version of the SIG Processor
61SigProcMinVersionBYTEnMinor version of the SIG Processor
71SigProcMonthBYTEnMonth of year (1–12) when SIG Processor firmware released
81SigProcDayBYTEnDay of month (1–31) when SIG Processor firmware released
91SigProcYearBYTEnYear when SIG Processor firmware released, minus 1900
104BCDSerialNumberARRAY of BYTE-Serial number of BCD
141BCDChecksumBYTEnChecksum of serial number. Least significant byte of sum of bytes 10 to 15 = 0xFF
152RevisionINTEGERnConfiguration revision number.
171ConfigLengthBYTEnLength of data in the configuration block
181NumChannelsBYTEnNumber of satellite tracking channels
191RTCMInputBYTEEnumRTCM input status
201RTCMOutputBYTEEnumRTCM output status
211FixRateBYTEEnumPosition fix rate
221SynchMeasBYTEEnumSynchronized measurements
231MiscellaneousBYTEnDefault value
241NMEAOutputBYTEEnumNMEA Output
251PPSOutputBYTEEnum1 PPS Output
261ProductIdBYTEnProduct ID
271ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
2864ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
922ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

RTCMInput: RTCM input status
ValueDescription
0RTCM input functionality not installed
1RTCM input is available
2Default value on cleared settings

RTCMOutput: RTCM output status
ValueDescription
0RTCM input functionality not installed
1RTCM Version 2 output is available
3RTCM Version 2 with PRC 9 is available

FixRate: Position fix rate
ValueDescription
01Hz positioning
12Hz positioning
45Hz positioning
910Hz positioning

SynchMeas: Synchronized measurements
ValueDescription
0Synchronized measurements not installed
1Synchronized measurements are available
3Carrier phase measurements are available

NMEAOutput: NMEA Output
ValueDescription
0NMEA output functionality not installed
1NMEA output is available
2Default value on cleared settings

PPSOutput: 1 PPS Output
ValueDescription
01 PPS output not installed
11 PPS output is available

0x46

Packet Response 0x46

Health of Receiver Report

Reports info about the satellite tracking status and operational health of the receiver.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1HealthOfReceiverIdID46
01StatusCodeBYTEEnumCurrent status of receiver.
11ErrorFlagsBYTEBitsetBits of byte are flags for displaying error codes

StatusCode: Current status of receiver.
ValueDescription
0Doing position fixes
1Do not have GPS time yet
2Reserved
3PDOP is too high
8No usable satellites
9Only 1 usable satellite
10Only 2 usable satellites
11Only 3 usable satellites
12The chosen satellite is unusable. This message is included when the one-satellite mode is in effect and a specific satellite is chosen with Command Packet 0x34, but the selected satellite is not usable.
16No integrity
170Generating differential corrections

ErrorFlags: Bits of byte are flags for displaying error codes
BitDescription
Battery-backed memory condition (0=Good condition; 1=BBRAM cleared)
Reserved (set to zero)
Reserved (set to zero)
Reserved (set to zero)
Antenna feed line status(0=No fault; 1=Fault)
Reference frequency error condition (0=No errors or acceptable rate of errors; 1=Excessive error rate)
Reserved (set to zero)
Reserved (set to zero)

0x47

Packet Response 0x47

Signal Levels Report

Reports signal levels for all satellites currently being tracked in response to packet 0x27.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1SignalLevelsIdID47Signal levels for all satellites
01NumSatsBYTEnNumber of satellite records in packet
15 * NumSatsSignalLevelsInfoArrayARRAY of SignalLevelInfo-Signal levels info array

SignalLevelInfo: Signal level info
Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
01PRNBYTEnPRN number
14SignalLevelSINGLE-Signal level

0x48

Packet Response 0x48

GPS System Message Report

GPS system message
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 GPSSVSystemMessage ID 48 GPS system message
0 22 LineContents STRING -

Provides the single precision lls fix values stored in the receiver

0 4 Altitude SINGLE -
4 4 InverseVariance SINGLE -
8 1 Flags BYTE n

0x4a

Packet Response 0x4a

Single Presicion LLA Fix Report

Provides the single precision lls fix values stored in the receiver
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 SinglePrecisionLlaFixId ID 4a
0 4 LLH_0 SINGLE -
4 4 LLH_1 SINGLE -
8 4 LLH_2 SINGLE -
12 4 ClockBias SINGLE -
16 4 Time SINGLE -

0x4a

Packet Response 0x4a

Single Presicion LLA Fix Report

Provides the single precision lls fix values stored in the receiver
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 SinglePrecisionLlaFixId ID 4a
0 4 LLH_0 SINGLE -
4 4 LLH_1 SINGLE -
8 4 LLH_2 SINGLE -
12 4 ClockBias SINGLE -
16 8 Time DOUBLE -

0x4b

Packet Response 0x4b

Machine/Code ID Report

Reports receiver's machine ID and additional status info in response to packet 0x26.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1MachineIdID4bMachine ID for receiver
01MachineIdBYTEnMachine ID for receiver
11StatusFlags1BYTEBitsetStatus information (part 1)
21StatusFlags2BYTEBitsetStatus information (part 2)

StatusFlags1: Status information (part 1)
BitDescription
Reserved
Battery powered time clock fault
A-to-D converter fault
Almanac stored in receiver memory is not complete or old
Receiver status acknowledged with Command Packet 0x1F
Secondary antenna in use

StatusFlags2: Status information (part 2)
BitDescription
Output of TSIP superpackets supported

0x4c

Packet Response 0x4c

Operating Parameters Response

Provides the operating parameter values of a receiver or requests the current parameter values stored in the receiver

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1OperatingParametersIdID4c
01DynamicsCodeBYTEEnumSets expected vehicle dynamics (velocity), used when computing the initial position
14ElevationMaskSINGLEradiansSets lowest acceptable elevation angle for satellites included in position solutions
54SignalLevelMaskSINGLE-Sets minimum signal level for satellites included in position solutions.
94PDOPMaskSINGLE-Sets maximum PDOP for satellites included in position solutions
134PDOPSwitchSINGLE-Sets transition between 2D and 3D mode when the receiver is operating in Auto 2D/3D position fix mode. A 3D position fix is computed when 4 or more satellites are available and the resulting PDOP is less than the PDOP Switch value

DynamicsCode: Sets expected vehicle dynamics (velocity), used when computing the initial position
ValueDescription
0Current value left unchanged
1Land Mode (< 2G)
2Sea Mode (< 1G)
3Air Mode (< 4G)
4Stationary

0x4f

Packet Response 0x4f

UTC Parameters Report

UTC Parameters
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 UTCTimeRptId ID 4f Universal Coordinated Time (UTC) info. Refer to ICD-200 for the description of each element for GPS.
0 8 A_0 DOUBLE - System clock model vs UTC - Seconds
8 4 A_1 SINGLE - System clock model vs UTC - Seconds/Second
12 2 delta_t_LS INTEGER n UTC leap seconds before DN/WN_LSF
14 4 t_ot SINGLE - Validity time of system clock model
18 2 WN_t INTEGER n Validity GPS week of system clock model
20 2 WN_LSF INTEGER n Week number of future UTC leap second update
22 2 DN INTEGER n Day of week of future UTC leap second update
24 2 delta_t_LSF INTEGER n UTC leap seconds after DN/WN_LSF

0x53

Packet Response 0x53

A-to-D Readings Response

Analog to digital readings from receiver
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AtoDReadings ID 53
0 4 Temp SINGLE degrees Temperature
4 4 Unused1 SINGLE -
8 4 Unused2 SINGLE -
12 4 AgcVoltage SINGLE volts Current AGC DAC setting
16 4 BatteryVotage SINGLE volts Current Battery
20 4 Unused3 SINGLE -
24 4 Unused4 SINGLE -
28 4 Unused5 SINGLE -

0x55

Packet Response 0x55

I/O Option Report

Reports current I/O options in response to Command Packet 0x35.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1OptionFlagsIdID55I/O Option Flags
01PosFlagsBYTEBitsetPosition flags show current status of automatic position reporting options, position precision, etc.
11VelocityFlagsBYTEBitsetShow current status of automatic velocity reports.
21TimingFlagsBYTEBitsetShow current status of time and position fix timing parameters
31AuxFlagsBYTEBitsetShow current status of raw measurement output parameter, codephase output parameter, and automatic output of additional fix reports

PosFlags: Position flags show current status of automatic position reporting options, position precision, etc.
BitDescription

VelocityFlags: Show current status of automatic velocity reports.
BitDescription

TimingFlags: Show current status of time and position fix timing parameters
BitDescription

AuxFlags: Show current status of raw measurement output parameter, codephase output parameter, and automatic output of additional fix reports
BitDescription

0x58

Packet Response 0x58

Satellite System Data Report

Report Packet 0x58 provides GPS data (almanac, ephemeris, etc.). The receiver sends this packet on request or in response to Command Packet 0x38 (acknowledging the loading of data). To get ionosphere or ephemeris, this report packet must be used

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1SatelliteSystemDataReportsIdID58
01OperationBYTEEnumType of satellite information operation
11DataTypeBYTEEnumType of satellite information included in this report
21PRNBYTEn0 = Data is not satellite specific. 1-32 = specific PRN number
31NumBytesBYTEnNumber of bytes of data
4NumBytesSVDataARRAY of BYTE-Requested data according to data type field

Operation: Type of satellite information operation
ValueDescription
0Request acknowledged/Cannot grant request
1Request acknowledged
2Requested data included in this report
3Requested data not available for this SV

DataType: Type of satellite information included in this report
ValueDescription
1
2
3Health page, T_oa, WN_oa
4
5
6

0x58 0x2 0x2

Packet Response 0x58 0x2 0x2

Satellite System Almanac Data Report

Report data specific to the almanc response. This data will arrive in response to command packet 0x38 For all following fields, please see their corresponding reference in ICD-GPS-2000
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 SatelliteSystemDataReportsId ID 58
0 1 DataIncludedSubId SubID 2
1 1 AlmanacDataSubId SubID 2
2 1 PRN BYTE n 0 = Data is not satellite specific. 1-32 = specific PRN number
3 1 NumBytes BYTE n Number of bytes of data
4 1 t_oa_raw BYTE n
5 1 SV_HEALTH BYTE n
6 4 e SINGLE -
10 4 t_oa SINGLE -
14 4 i_io SINGLE -
18 4 omega_dot SINGLE -
22 4 sqrt_A SINGLE -
26 4 omega_0 SINGLE -
30 4 omega SINGLE -
34 4 m0 SINGLE -
38 4 a_f0 SINGLE -
42 4 a_f1 SINGLE -
46 4 axis SINGLE -
50 4 n SINGLE -
54 4 omega_n SINGLE -
58 4 o_dot_n SINGLE -
62 4 t_zc SINGLE -
66 2 weeknum INTEGER n
68 2 wn_oa INTEGER n

0x58 0x2 0x3

Packet Response 0x58 0x2 0x3

Satellite System Almanac Health Report

Report data specific to the almanc health response. This data will arrive in response to command packet 0x38 For all following fields, please see their corresponding reference in ICD-GPS-2000
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 SatelliteSystemDataReportsId ID 58
0 1 DataIncludedSubId SubID 2
1 1 AlmanacHealthDataSubId SubID 3
2 1 PRN BYTE n 0 = Data is not satellite specific. 1-32 = specific PRN number
3 1 NumBytes BYTE n Number of bytes of data
4 1 weekno_health BYTE n See ICD-GPS-2000 section 20.3.3.5.1.3
5 4 SVHealth LONG n See ICD-GPS-2000 section 20.3.3.5.1
9 1 t_oa_health BYTE n See ICD-GPS-2000 section 20.3.3.5.1
10 1 t_oa_current BYTE n Units = seconds/4096
11 2 weekno_current INTEGER n Current GPS week number

0x58 0x2 0x4

Packet Response 0x58 0x2 0x4

Satellite System Ionosphere Report

Report data specific to the ionosphere data response. This data will arrive in response to command packet 0x38 For all following fields, please see their corresponding reference in ICD-GPS-2000
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 SatelliteSystemDataReportsId ID 58
0 1 DataIncludedSubId SubID 2
1 1 IonosphereDataSubId SubID 4
2 1 PRN BYTE n 0 = Data is not satellite specific. 1-32 = specific PRN number

4 8 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
12 4 Alpha_0 SINGLE - Refer to ICD-GPS-200, Sec 20.3.3.5.1.9
16 4 Alpha_1 SINGLE - Refer to ICD-GPS-200, Sec 20.3.3.5.1.9
20 4 Alpha_2 SINGLE - Refer to ICD-GPS-200, Sec 20.3.3.5.1.9
24 4 Alpha_3 SINGLE - Refer to ICD-GPS-200, Sec 20.3.3.5.1.9
28 4 Beta_0 SINGLE - Refer to ICD-GPS-200, Sec 20.3.3.5.1.9
32 4 Beta_1 SINGLE - Refer to ICD-GPS-200, Sec 20.3.3.5.1.9
36 4 Beta_2 SINGLE - Refer to ICD-GPS-200, Sec 20.3.3.5.1.9
40 4 Beta_3 SINGLE - Refer to ICD-GPS-200, Sec 20.3.3.5.1.9

0x5b

Packet Response 0x5b

Satellite Ephemeris Status Report

Reports ephemeris status for a specified satellite in response to Command Packet 0x3B.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 SatEphemerisId ID 5b Satellite ephemeris status report
0 1 PRN BYTE 1-32 Pseudorandom number of satellite
1 4 CollectionTime SINGLE seconds GPS time when Ephemeris data is collected from the satellite
5 1 Health BYTE ICD-GPS-200 The 6-bit ephemeris health
6 1 IODE BYTE ICD-GPS-200 Issue of Data Ephemeris. See the U.S. Government document ICD-GPS-200
7 4 t_oe SINGLE seconds See the U.S. Government document ICD-GPS-200
11 1 FitIntervalFlag BYTE ICD-GPS-200 See the U.S. Government document ICD-GPS-200
12 4 URA SINGLE meters User Range Accuracy of satellite, converted to meters from the 4-bit code described in ICD-GPS-200

0x5c

Packet Response 0x5c

Satellite Tracking Status Report

Reports tracking status for a specified satellite in response to Command Packet 0x3C.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1SatTrackingIdID5cSatellite tracking status report
01PRNBYTE1-32Pseudorandom number of satellite
11ChanAndSlotBYTEEnumChannel and Slot Code (internal code assigned to the hardware channel and slot used to track the specified satellite)
21AcquisitionFlagBYTEEnumAcquisition Flag
31EphemerisFlagBYTEnEphemeris Flag (0=no ephemeris; other=good ephemeris)
44SignalLevelSINGLE-Signal level
84GPSTimeSINGLE-GPS time of last measurement (< 0=no measurements)
124ElevationSINGLEradiansElevation
164AzimuthSINGLEradiansAzimuth
201OldMeasFlagBYTEnOld measurement flag (0=new measurement)
211IntegerMsecFlagBYTEEnumStatus of integer millisecond range to the specified satellite
221BadDataFlagBYTEEnumCurent health status of the data
231DataCollectFlagBYTEnReceiver is collecting data from satellite (0=no data)

ChanAndSlot: Channel and Slot Code (internal code assigned to the hardware channel and slot used to track the specified satellite)
ValueDescription
0Channel 1
8Channel 2
16Channel 3
24Channel 4
32Channel 5
40Channel 6
48Channel 7
56Channel 8
64Channel 9
72Channel 10
80Channel 11
88Channel 12

AcquisitionFlag: Acquisition Flag
ValueDescription
0Never acquired
1Acquired
2Reopened search

IntegerMsecFlag: Status of integer millisecond range to the specified satellite
ValueDescription
0Unknown
1Acquired from sub-frame data collection
2Verified by a bit crossing time
3Verified by a successful position fix
4Suspected msec error

BadDataFlag: Curent health status of the data
ValueDescription
0Good data
1Bad parity
2Bad ephemeris

01DiagDataIdSubID22
11MsgIdSubID01
21TypeBYTEEnumDiagnostic type
32ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
58TimeDOUBLE-Time in seconds since unix epoch
13END-13MsgARRAY of CHAR-The diagnostic message

Type: Diagnostic type
ValueDescription
0Error log diagnostics allowed
1Run-time log diagnostics allowed
2Debug diagnostics allowed

0x6a 0x01

Packet Response 0x6a 0x01

Differential Corrections Output Control Report

Indicates whether or not the receiver will output differential correction information.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1DGPSOutputIdID6a
01DGPSOutputControlIdSubID01
11CorrectionsUsedInFixBYTEEnumControls the automatic output of the 0x6A 0x00, corrections used in fix report.
21CorrectionInfoBYTEBitsetControls configuration of auto-reporting for correction/fix adjustment related information.
31ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use

CorrectionsUsedInFix: Controls the automatic output of the 0x6A 0x00, corrections used in fix report.
ValueDescription
0Disable automatic output of correction info
1Automatically output correction info used in DGPS fixes

CorrectionInfo: Controls configuration of auto-reporting for correction/fix adjustment related information.
BitDescription

-1SatSelectionIdID6d
01FixModeBYTEnBits 0-2: 2D or 3D mode; Bit 3: Auto or Manual mode; Bits 4-7: Number of satellites
14PDOPSINGLE-Precision Dilution of Precision.
54HDOPSINGLE-Horizontal Dilution of Precision.
94VDOPSINGLE-Vertical Dilution of Precision.
134TDOPSINGLE-Time Dilution of Precision.
17FixModeSatsInViewARRAY of BYTE-Array of PRN's for satellites in view

1Enable

StaticFilter: Static Filter Switch

0Disable
1Enable

AltitudeFilter: Altitude Filter Switch
ValueDescription
0Disable
1Enable

ValueDescription

1High
2Maximum

0 2 MaxPRCAge INTEGER seconds Maximum pseudorange correction age

-1NMEAOutputCfgIdID7b
01NMEAOutputOptIdSubID07
11PortBYTEEnumSerial port number
21Options1BYTEBitsetOptions 1
32ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use

Port: Serial port number
ValueDescription
0Port0
1Port1
254Opposite Port
255Current Port

Options1: Options 1
BitDescription
Output NMEA messages wrapped in TSIP, if TSIP is enabled on that port (0=Disabled; 1=Enabled)

0x7b 0x08

Packet Response 0x7b 0x08

NMEA Extended Port Configuration Options Report

Reports the data reporting options for the NMEA extended output for a specified port.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1NMEAOptionsCfgIdID7b
01NMEAOptionsIdSubID08
11PortBYTEEnumSerial port number
21MessageProtocolBYTEBitsetMessage Protocol to which the message applies
31MessageIntervalBYTEEnumMessage interval
44OutputMaskLONGBitsetNMEA bit-mask for outputting messages.
81GGAOptionsBYTEBitsetGGA Options
91PrecisionBYTE0x00-0x07GGA precision (0-7 decimal places).
1040ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use

Port: Serial port number
ValueDescription
0Port0
1Port1
2Port2
3Port3

MessageProtocol: Message Protocol to which the message applies
BitDescription
Output NMEA messages wrapped in TSIP, if TSIP is enabled on that port
Output raw NMEA messages on the specified port

MessageInterval: Message interval
ValueDescription
0Report at Position Rate
15 seconds
21 second
3200 milliseconds
4100 milliseconds
255Invalid or unsupported value stored

OutputMask: NMEA bit-mask for outputting messages.
BitDescription
Reserved (Set to 0)
GGA
GLL
VTG
GSV
GSA
ZDA
ALM
RMC
GRS
GBS
GST

GGAOptions: GGA Options
BitDescription
Retain the existing configuration and ignore value set in other bits
Invalidate GGA for old positions
Invalidate GGA for non-differential positions when in Auto DGPS mode
Show invalid GGA as '0' in the status field
Do not show decimal places for time in GGA

0x7d 0x00

ASAP Fix Rate Report

Reports the number of position fixes per second.

- 1 ASAPFixRateRepId ID 7d
0 1 ASAPFixRateSubId SubID 00
1 1 ASAPRate BYTE n Number of position fixes per second (range: 5-10)

0x7d 0x01

Packet Response 0x7d 0x01

Position Fix Rate I/O Options Report

Reports the position fix rate I/O options bytes.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1PosFixRateOptionsRepIdID7d
01PosFixRateOptionsSubIdSubID01
11OptionFlags1BYTEBitsetVarious Options
21OptionFlags2BYTEBitsetVarious Options

OptionFlags1: Various Options
BitDescription
Precision of time for report packets 0x42, 0x43, 0x4A, 0x54, 0x57, 0x83, and 0x84 (0=Single; 1=Double)
Timestamp of velocity (0=Same as position timestamp; 1=True time of velocity)

OptionFlags2: Various Options
BitDescription
Port A automatic message control for all automatic packets that cannot be controlled in another way (0=Output; 1=No output)
Port B automatic message control for all automatic packets that cannot be controlled in another way (0=Output; 1=No output)
Time only (0D) output in Report Packet 0x54 (0=On; 1=Off)
Output full Satellite Data for ephemeris and UTC when received in Report Packet 0x58 (0=Off; 1=On)
Echo RTCM Port A input in Report Packet 0x1A 0x00 (0=On; 1=Off)

0x7d 0x02

Packet Response 0x7d 0x02

Position Fix Output Interval and Offset Report

Reports the position fix output interval and offset that are used to deterimne output rate in relation to fix rate.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 OutputIntervalOffsetRepId ID 7d
0 1 OutputIntervalOffsetSubId SubID 02
1 2 Interval INTEGER n Output one every interval position fixes. A value of 0 restores the receiver defaults.
3 2 Offset INTEGER n Determines which position is output within an interval.

Packet Response 0x7d 0x09

01MessageIntervalSubIdSubID09
11PortBYTEEnumSerial port number to which the interval applies
21MessageProtocolBYTEEnumProtocol to which the interval applies

48ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
122ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

0Port0

0TSIP
1NMEA

15 seconds
21 second
3200 milliseconds
4100 milliseconds

Packet Response 0x82

Differential Position Fix Mode

Reports the differential position fix mode of the receiver.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1ShortDiffPosFixModeIdID82
01ModeBYTEEnumDifferential position fix mode

Mode: Differential position fix mode
ValueDescription
0Differential forced off
1Differential forced on
2Differential currently off
3Differential currently on

0x82

Packet Response 0x82

Differential Position Fix Mode

Reports the differential position fix mode of the receiver.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1DiffPosFixModeIdID82
01ModeBYTEEnumDifferential position fix mode
11RTCMVersionBYTEEnumRTCM version used to compute positions
22ReferenceStationIDINTEGERnStation number assigned to the selected reference station for receviers supporting input from reference stations. (-1 for any reference station, other numbers for the given reference station ID)

Mode: Differential position fix mode
ValueDescription
0Differential forced off
1Differential forced on
2Differential currently off
3Differential currently on

RTCMVersion: RTCM version used to compute positions
ValueDescription
0Version 1,2, or PRC Type 9
1Version 1 only
2Version 2 or PRC Type 9 only

0x83

Packet Response 0x83

Double-Precision XYZ Position Fix and Clock Bias Report

Reports the current GPS position fix in XYZ ECEF coordinate components. The I/O position option must be set to XYZ ECEF and double-precision must be selected for this packet to be generated. Note also that this packet assumes double-precision time of fix, since 5Hz and faster fix rates are now the norm.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 DblPrecXYZPosFixId ID 83
0 8 X DOUBLE meters X coordinate component along the x-axis of ECEF coordinate system.
8 8 Y DOUBLE meters Y coordinate component along the y-axis of ECEF coordinate system.
16 8 Z DOUBLE meters Z coordinate component along the z-axis of ECEF coordinate system.
24 8 ClockBias DOUBLE meters Clock bias
32 8 TimeOfFix DOUBLE seconds Double-precision GPS time or UTC, as selected by the I/O timing option

0x89 0x00 0x00

Packet Response 0x89 0x00 0x00

SecureRTK System Status Rsp

Status of SecureRTK and the 5 rover keys.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1RTKIDID89
01SecureRTKIDSubID00
11SecureRTK System StatusSubID00
21VersionBYTE0Current SecureRTK Version
31NumberOfKeysBYTE5Number of Rover Keys
43OverallSystemStatusOverallSystemStatus-Overall Secure RTK Status
720IndividualKeysStatusARRAY of KeyStatus-Status of Keys 0-4
272ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

OverallSystemStatus: Overall Secure RTK Status
Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
01SystemStatusBYTEEnumSecureRTK System Status
12DaysToExpiryINTEGERnUTC Days before key in use expires, 0xFFFF == Never

SystemStatus: SecureRTK System Status
ValueDescription
0Not receiving CMR+ with SecureRTK enabled
1SecureRTK key needed
2SecureRTK key expired
3SecureRTK key in use

KeyStatus: Key Slot Information
Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
01KeyStatusBYTEEnumKey Status
11KeyExpiryStatusBYTEEnumKey Expiry Status
22KeyDayOfExpiryINTEGERnGPS day (UTC) of key expiry, 0xFFFF == never

KeyStatus: Key Status
ValueDescription
0no key in slot
1bad serial number etc, typo?
2good key, may or may not be expired
3good key, currently receiving, but may or may not be expired

KeyExpiryStatus: Key Expiry Status
ValueDescription
0waiting for GPS time/unknown
1not yet expired
2already expired

RTK CMR Stats Report

Provides CMR reception statistics for all the available base stations

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1RTKIDID89
01RTKStatsIDSubID33
11CMRStatsIDSubID08
21ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
31NumStationsBYTEnNumber of stations for which CMR stats are provided
41SelStationIdBYTEnID of currently selected station
5NumStationsStationIdsARRAY of BYTE-Available RTK base station ID's
5 + NumStationsNumStationsLinkQualityARRAY of BYTE%Quality of radio link to the corresponding base station
5 + NumStations + NumStations4 * NumStationsLastCMRTimeARRAY of LONGmsecGPS time of week that last CMR was received
5 + NumStations + NumStations + 4 * NumStations2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

0x89 0x40

Packet Response 0x89 0x40

RTK Configuration Rsp

Sets the RTK configuration

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1RTKIDID89
01RTKConfigurationIDSubID40
11PropagationModeBYTEEnumType of CMR propagation used to compute RTK solutions
21VelocityTypeBYTEEnumType of velocity to be output in the RTK position solution
330ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
332ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

PropagationMode: Type of CMR propagation used to compute RTK solutions
ValueDescription
0Solutions generated at the time of the incoming CMR's
1Solutions generated using propagated CMR's

VelocityType: Type of velocity to be output in the RTK position solution
ValueDescription
0Use RTK Library velocity
1Use Doppler velocity
2Use RTK Library velocity when fixed and Doppler velocity otherwise
3Use RTK velocity static, or moving slowly and Doppler velocity otherwise (deprecated, replaced with RTKVelocity)
4Use RTK velocity when fixed and static and Doppler velocity otherwise (deprecated, replaced with RTKVelocity)
5Use Delta (position difference) velocity

0x89 0x41

Packet Response 0x89 0x41

RTK Solution Info Rsp

Provides RTK Solution Info

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1RTKIDID89
01RTKSolutionInfoIDSubID41
11SolutionModeBYTEEnumMode in which the RTK engine is currently operating
21SolutionTypeBYTEEnumType of solution being generated by RTK engine
34SolutionFlagsLONGnBitfield that indicates various attributes of solution [future]
78CorrAgeDOUBLEsecAge of corrections used to generate solution
158HorzErrorEstDOUBLEmetersEstimated horizontal error of solution
238IonoScintLevelDOUBLE-Ionospheric scintillation level on a scale from 0 (good) to 10 (bad). Special values include -1 to report that the value is not currently available, and -2 to indicate that the value was available previously, but the information that was used to calculate the number is too old or that the station being used is not IonoGuard capable, and the calculations are done on the rover. -1 may indicate that the mode is not enabled on the rover, the information is not yet calculated by the rover, or the information is not received from the base. For the special satus -2, any UI should render this as N/A (not available).
3120ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
512ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

SolutionMode: Mode in which the RTK engine is currently operating
ValueDescription
0RTK engine has no current solution
1Single baseline RTK solution via local base or CORS
2Network RTK solution
3CenterPoint RTX (std) solution
4CenterPoint RTX (fast) solution
5RangePoint RTX solution
6Reserved
7ViewPoint RTX Solution
8QZSS CLAS Solution
9Galileo HAS Solution

SolutionType: Type of solution being generated by RTK engine
ValueDescription
0RTK engine is not generating positions
1Float solution with sync data
2Float solution with propogated data
3Fixed solution with sync data
4Fixed solution with propogated data

0x89 0x50

Packet Response 0x89 0x50

RTK Aux Settings Rsp

Provides RTK Aux Settings

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1RTKIDID89
01RTKAuxSettingsIDSubID50
11ScintillationModeBYTEEnumRTK engine mode that mitigates the effects of atmospheric scintillation
21BackupSourceBYTEEnumRTK Backup Source currently enabled to be used
31RTKBaseDatumBYTEEnumDatum in which RTK base station was surveyed
48XFillSatFrequencyDOUBLEHzxFill L-band satellite frequency. Value 0 = unchanged
124XFillSatBitRateSINGLEbaudxFill L-band satellite bit rate (600, 1200, 2400)
162RTKBaseIDINTEGERnRTK Base Station ID Number
181SatConfigSourceBYTEEnumThe source for configuring Satellite tracking
193ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
222ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

ScintillationMode: RTK engine mode that mitigates the effects of atmospheric scintillation
ValueDescription
0Disable scintillation mode
1Enable scintillation mode
255Do not change this setting

BackupSource: RTK Backup Source currently enabled to be used
ValueDescription
0Do not use any RTK backup source
1Enable xFill
2Enable Fallback RTX (deprecated, no longer supported)
3Do not use this value
255Do not change this setting

RTKBaseDatum: Datum in which RTK base station was surveyed
ValueDescription
0ITRF2008 Datum
1NAD83 Datum
2ETRS89 Datum
3GDA94 Datum
4SIRGAS-CON 2016.0 Datum
5ITRF1988 Datum
6ITRF1989 Datum
7ITRF1990 Datum
8ITRF1991 Datum
9ITRF1992 Datum
10ITRF1993 Datum
11ITRF1994 Datum
12ITRF1996 Datum
13ITRF1997 Datum
14ITRF2000 Datum
15ITRF2005 Datum
16NAD83CSRS Datum
17NAD83-CORS96 Datum
18ETRF2000-R05 Datum
19SIRGAS2000 Datum
20SIRGAS95 Datum
21NAD83 2011 Datum
22NAD83 MA11 Datum
23NAD83 PA11 Datum
24RGF93v2 Datum
25CGCS2000 Datum
26NZGD2000 Datum
27JGD2000 Datum
28POSGAR07 Datum
29RGAF09 Datum
30RGFG95 Datum
31RGNC9193 Datum
32RGR92 Datum
33RGTAAF07 Datum
34MAGNASIRGAS Datum
35EST97 Datum
36ITRF2014 Datum
37GDA2020 Datum
38NAD83-CSRS 1997.0 Datum
39NAD83-CSRS 2002.0 Datum
40NAD83-CSRS 2010.0 Datum
246ITRF2014_2021 Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
247Legacy_SIRGASCON Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
248ITRF2014_CurrentEpoch Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
249Legacy_GDA94 Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
250Legacy_ITRF2000 Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
251Legacy_ETRF2000_R05 Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
252Legacy_ETRS89 Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
253Legacy_ETRS89 Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
254Automatic Datum (The picus receiver automatically determines the right datum to use depending on the receiver location.
255Do not change this setting

SatConfigSource: The source for configuring Satellite tracking
ValueDescription
0Manual: Receiver uses the Frequency and Bit Rate value from this packet to track the RTX satellite.
1Auto: Receiver uses its position to automatically configure frequency and bit rate to track the RTX Satellites
255Do not change this setting

0x89 0x51

RTK Aux Status Rsp

Provides RTK Aux Status

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1RTKIDID89
01RTKAuxStatusIDSubID51
11xFillStatusBYTEEnumStatus of xFill (extends RTK during correction outage)
22XFillTimeRemainingINTEGERsecondsTime remaining during which xFill can continue to extend RTK (-1 = n/a)
42XFillCorrectionAgeINTEGERsecondsCurrent age of xFill corrections (-1 = n/a)
622ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
282ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

xFillStatus: Status of xFill (extends RTK during correction outage)
ValueDescription
0xFill is disabled
1xFill is enabled but not yet ready to extend RTK
2xFill is ready to extend RTK
3xFill is actively extending RTK

0x89 0x60

Packet Response 0x89 0x60

RTK Base Info Rsp

Provides RTK Base Info
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 RTKID ID 89
0 1 RTKBaseInfoID SubID 60
1 2 BaseId INTEGER n Base station ID
3 9 BaseName STRING - Base station name
12 4 BaseFlags LONG n Bitfield that indicates various attributes of base. Least significant bit indicates whether CMR stream is active (1 = active, 0 = inactive)
16 8 DistToBase DOUBLE meters Distance from current position to base station
24 24 BaseVector BaseVector -
48 20 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
68 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

BaseVector: 
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
0 8 East DOUBLE meters East component of ENU vector to base station
8 8 North DOUBLE meters North component of ENU vector to base station
16 8 Up DOUBLE meters Up component of ENU vector to base station

0x89 0x61

Packet Response 0x89 0x61

CMR Info Rsp

Provides CMR Info
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 RTKID ID 89
0 1 CMRInfoID SubID 61
1 2 CMRId INTEGER n CMR ID
3 4 ConnectionTime LONG n Connection time with current CMR id
7 24 BasePosition BasePosition -
31 10 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
41 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

BasePosition: 
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
0 8 x DOUBLE meters x coordinate of the base station
8 8 y DOUBLE meters y coordinate of the base station
16 8 z DOUBLE meters z coordinate of the base station

0x89 0x62

Packet Response 0x89 0x62

Ionospheric Disturbance Response

Provides information on the overall disturbance level of the ionosphere

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1RTKIDID89
01IonoDisturbanceIDSubID62
11DisturbanceInfoSourceBYTEEnumSource of ionospheric disturbance information
21GeofenceActiveBYTEboolWhen set to true (1), indicates that a geofence is preventing the rover from computing amplitude and phase scintillation disturbance metrics. The rover will continue to estimate and correct for ionospheric gradients.
34AmplitudeDisturbanceSINGLE-Amplitude scintillation disturbance level for the station or network. Only valid when DisturbanceInfoSource is not NoState (0x00).
74PhaseDisturbanceSINGLE-Phase scintillation disturbance level for the station or network. Only valid when DisturbanceInfoSource is not NoState(0x00).
114GradientLevelSINGLE-Ionosphere Gradient level for the station or network. Only valid when DisturbanceInfoSource is not NoState(0x00)
151FeatureAllowedBYTEboolIf the IonoGuard feature is available for enabling the ionospheric disturbance mitigation, reports true (1). Otherwise reports false (0).
1665ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
812ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

DisturbanceInfoSource: Source of ionospheric disturbance information
ValueDescription
0No ionospheric disturbance information is being calculated.
1Ionospheric disturbance information computed at, and is being broadcast by the base station.
2Ionospheric disturbance information is computed at the rover from received base observations.
3Ionospheric disturbance information is computed using information from a local network of receivers, and is broadcast from a server.

Fallback RTX Offsets Rsp

For the designated source, retrieve the X, Y, and Z offset values in Fallback RTX. Request packet is 0x69 0x71 0x0. No longer supported, do not use. Packet will always return manual source, and an invalid offset with all zeros

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1RTKIDID89
01FallbackRTXSubID71
11FallbackRTXOffsetSourceValueSubID00
21RTXOffsetSourceBYTEEnumDesignated Fallback RTX Offset source
31OffsetValidBYTEnValidity of the offset values. (Valid = 1, Invalid = 0)
41RTXOffsetBaseIdBYTEnBase ID from which offsets are retrieved if they are set by the base station; For user-set offsets a value of 255 is reported in this field.
58XOffsetValueDOUBLEmetersX-Offset value (Range: +/- 100 Meters)
138YOffsetValueDOUBLEmetersY-Offset value (Range: +/- 100 Meters)
218ZOffsetValueDOUBLEmetersZ-Offset value (Range: +/- 100 Meters)
298ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
372ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

RTXOffsetSource: Designated Fallback RTX Offset source
ValueDescription
0Retrieve user-set offsets
1Retrieve base-set offsets

1 1 FallbackRTXOffsetSourceValue SubID 00

Fallback RTX Mode Configuration Response

Reports Fallback RTX mode. Request packet is 0x69 0x71 0x02. No longer supported, do not use. It will always return UnknownMode and manual status.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1RTKIDID89
01FallbackRTXSubID71
11FallbackRTXModeSubID01
21FallbackModeBYTEEnumFallback RTX Mode Setting
31RTXOffsetSourceBYTEEnumFallback RTX Offset Source
41ActivateFallbackStatusBYTEEnumIndicates if the receiver has enough information to activate Fallback mode
57ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
122ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

FallbackMode: Fallback RTX Mode Setting
ValueDescription
0Fallback RTX Correction Source is RTK
1Fallback RTX Correction Source is RTX
255Fallback RTX is not the configured correction source

RTXOffsetSource: Fallback RTX Offset Source
ValueDescription
0User-set offset values are considered.
1Base-set offset values are considered

ActivateFallbackStatus: Indicates if the receiver has enough information to activate Fallback mode
ValueDescription
0Fallback RTX cannot be activated. The receiver cannot transition from RTK to RTX mode.
1Fallback RTX can be activated. Receiver has enough information to transition from RTK to RTX mode.

- 1 RTKID ID 89
0 1 FallbackRTX SubID 71
1 1 FallbackRTXMode SubID 01
2 1 Result BYTE n Result of configuring Fallback RTX (Failure = 0, Success = 1)
