0x89 0x71 0x03

Packet Response 0x89 0x71 0x03

xFill Premium Status Response

Reports xFill Premium Status. Request packet is 0x69 0x71 0x03.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1RTKIDID89
01xFillPremiumIDSubID71
11xFillPremiumSourceSubID03
21xFillStatusBYTEEnumReports xFill Status.
32xFillTimeRemainingINTEGERsecondsTime remaining during which xFill can continue to extend RTK (-1 = n/a)
52xFillCorrectionAgeINTEGERsecondsCurrent age of xFill corrections (-1 = n/a)
71ScintillationSwitchRuleBYTEEnumReports the configured xFill Premium Scintillation Switch Rule. Note: xFill Premium is activated on radio outages regardless of the rule chosen and only if xFill Premium Status reports Available.
81ActivateHourBYTEnHour to force-activate xFill Premium. Receiver uses xFill Premium positions at this designated hour.
91ActivateMinuteBYTEnMinute to force-activate xFill Premium. Receiver uses xFill Premium positions at this designated minute.
101DeactivateHourBYTEnHour to unforce receiver from using xFill Premium generated positions. Receiver is no longer forced to use xFill Premium positions at this designated hour.
111DeactivateMinuteBYTEnMinute to unforce receiver from using xFill Premium generated positions. Receiver is no longer forced to use xFill Premium positions at this designated time.
121xFillPremiumStatusBYTEEnumReports xFill Premium Status.
131IsForceActivatedBYTEEnumReports if xFill Premium is force-activated in the receiver. Applicable for Manual or Time based switch rule.
141IsUserActivatedBYTEEnumFor Manual switch rule, reports if xFill Premium is activated.
151IsSubscrValidBYTEnIf a valid subscription is available for the receiver to use xFill Premium generated positions, report 1. Else report 0.
161IsBaseStnPosRecvBYTEnIn the current boot cycle, if the receiver has received the base station position atleast once, then report 1. 0 otherwise.
171AreOffsetsAvailableBYTEnIf the base station has transmitted valid offsets, then report 1. 0 otherwise.
181IsConvergedBYTEnIf xFill Premium generated positions have sufficiently converged or if the Horizontal error estimate for xFill Premium generated positions is below a pre-set threshold, then report 1. 0 otherwise.
194HorizErrorEstimateSINGLEmThe Horizontal error estimate for xFill Premium. (-1 if not supported)
234xFillPremiumProgressToReadySINGLE-xFill Premium progress to ready. The number will be decreasing (0 = Ready). NOT IMPLEMENTED YET. CURRENTLY ALWAYS 0.
271ManualScintSwitchStatusBYTEEnumEnumeration of states of a user interface button that controls manual switching from RTK to xFill Premium and back. This UI button should only be visible when the ScintillationSwitchRule is set to Manual i.e. has a value of 1. The UI button takes values corresponding to two actions: (1) switch to xFill Premium and (2) switch (back) to RTK.
282xFillCorrectionAgeNotActiveINTEGERsecondsCurrent age of xFill corrections when xFill is not active (-1 = n/a)
3014ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
442ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

xFillStatus: Reports xFill Status.
ValueDescription
0xFill has not been enabled by the user.
1xFill is still waiting on information to allow it to be useful.
2xFill has all the required information to generate positions. A radio or RTK correction outage will cause the receiver to use xFill positions.
3Receiver uses xFill generated positions.
255xFill is not enabled or available

ScintillationSwitchRule: Reports the configured xFill Premium Scintillation Switch Rule. Note: xFill Premium is activated on radio outages regardless of the rule chosen and only if xFill Premium Status reports Available.
ValueDescription
0Default rule. xFill Premium activates on radio outages only.
1User controls xFill Premium activation.
2xFill Premium will be force-activated at this user-configured time. Note that the receiver will use xFill Premium generated positions only if the xFill Premium Status reports Available. For Not Available or Not Ready Status, the receiver waits for xFill Premium to report Available status before force-activating and/or using xFill Premium positions. Note: The non-availability of RTK correction stream may lead the receiver to use xFill Premium generated positions beyond the deactivation time.
255Do not change this setting

xFillPremiumStatus: Reports xFill Premium Status.
ValueDescription
0Reported if the user has not enabled xFill.
1xFill Premium does not have enough information to generate valid positions in the receiver.
2xFill Premium has all the required information to generate positions. Receiver can use xFill Premium generated positions when desired or if there is a radio outage.
3Receiver uses xFill Premium generated positions.
255xFill Premium is not enabled

IsForceActivated: Reports if xFill Premium is force-activated in the receiver. Applicable for Manual or Time based switch rule.
ValueDescription
0Receiver has not been forced to use xFill Premium generated positions
1Receiver has been forced to use xFill Premium generated positions
255xFill Premium is not configured

IsUserActivated: For Manual switch rule, reports if xFill Premium is activated.
ValueDescription
0User has not forced receiver to use xFill Premium positions
1User has forced receiver to use xFill Premium positions
255xFill Premium is not configured

ManualScintSwitchStatus: Enumeration of states of a user interface button that controls manual switching from RTK to xFill Premium and back. This UI button should only be visible when the ScintillationSwitchRule is set to Manual i.e. has a value of 1. The UI button takes values corresponding to two actions: (1) switch to xFill Premium and (2) switch (back) to RTK.
ValueDescription
0xFill is not enabled, an xFill Premium subscription is not available, or the ScintillationSwitchRule is not set to Manual. Hide the switch to xFill Premium on the UI.
1The receiver does not have enough information to report xFill Premium positions. Grey out the switch to xFill Premium on the UI.
2The receiver has has all the required information to report xFill Premium positions. Set ActivateXFillPremium to 1 to report xFill Premium positions. Show the switch to xFill Premium on the UI.
3The receiver is reporting xFill Premium positions, because ActivateXFillPremium is set to 1. To start reporting RTK positions, set ActivateXFillPremium to 0. Show the switch (back) to RTK on the UI.
4The receiver is reporting xFill Premium positions, because ActivateXFillPremium is set to 1. Cannot start reporting RTK positions, because RTK corrections have not been received recently. Grey out the switch (back) to RTK on the UI.
5The receiver is automatically reporting xFill Premium positions, because because RTK corrections have not been received recently. To continue in xFill Premium mode after RTK corrections resume, set ActivateXFillPremium to 1. Show the switch to xFill Premium on the UI. Indicate that if the switch is used, the user will not be able to switch back to RTK until RTK corrections are received.

51DataValidBYTEEnumData validity flag
64RMSSINGLE-Position residual root mean square
104SigmaEastSINGLEmetersSigma east
144SigmaNorthSINGLEmetersSigma north
184CovENSINGLE-East-north covariance
224SigmaUpSINGLEmetersSigma Up
261AxesValidBYTEEnumValidity flag of the axes values
274SemiMajorAxisSINGLEmetersSemi-major axis of the error-ellipse in meters
314SemiMinorAxisSINGLEmetersSemi-minor axis of the error-ellipse in meters
354OrientationSINGLEradiansOrientation of the semi-major axis from true north
394UnitVarianceSINGLEvarianceResidual unit variance
432NumEpochsINTEGERcountUsed only for RTK
452DOFINTEGERnDegrees of freedom
476ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
532ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

DataValid: Data validity flag
ValueDescription
0Unavailable or invalid, the rest of the packet should be ignored.
1Data is valid

AxesValid: Validity flag of the axes values
ValueDescription
0Invalid. The semi-major axis, semi-minor axis and orientation values are not valid, and must be derived from the sigmas and covariance
1Axes are valid

0x8f 0x77

Packet Response 0x8f 0x77

FFT Report2

Sample data for the fft report

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01FFTAppIdSubID77
11PageIdBYTEnThe page number of the fft report
2END-2SampleARRAY of BYTE-Averaged power samples
2 + END-22ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

0x8f 0x77 0x0

Packet Response 0x8f 0x77 0x0

FFT Report

Report Packet 0x8F 0x77 is generated after Command Packet 0x8E 0x75 is acknowledged with Report Packet 0x8F 0x75. The receiver performs a 1024-point Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) by the number of times specified by the Number of Integrations parameter in Command Packet 0x8E 0x75. Once the FFT report is completed, the receiver begins the next FFT. The FFT reports are generated and sent continuously until the FFT Stop Command (Command Packet 0x8E 0x76) is issued. Because the amount of data contained in the FFT report exceeds 123 bytes, the report is divided into multiple packets (pages). Even if all data bytes are DLEs (which would transmit 2 TSIP bytes for each data byte), the message structure does not overflow the 255 byte TSIP buffer length.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01FFTAppIdSubID77
11Page0IdSubID0
28FrequencyDOUBLE-Center frequency of narow/wideband spectral plot (Hz)
108BinSizeDOUBLE-FFT bin size in Hz
181InputSquaredBYTEnIndicates whether input squaring was performed
191NumIntegrationsBYTEnNumber of post FFT power sums
202NumBinsINTEGERnNumber of FFT bins
224MaxLevelSINGLE-Maximum power level
2699SampleARRAY of BYTE-Averaged power samples
1252ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

0x8f 0x7b

Packet Response 0x8f 0x7b

DGPS Receiver RAM Configuration Block Report v3

The report contains current receiver configuration parameter settings and a software version report. The actual receiver configuration parameters are set using Command Packet 0x8E 0x7C.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 RamCfgId SubID 7b
1 1 PortNumber BYTE n The receiver port number
2 20 Name STRING - Software name
22 1 FirmwareMajVersion BYTE n Major firmware version
23 1 FirmwareMinVersion BYTE n Minor firmware version
24 1 FirmwareMonth BYTE n Firmware release month
25 1 FirmwareDay BYTE n Firmware release day
26 1 FirmwareYear BYTE n Firmware release year (from 1900)
27 187 ConfigBlockV3 ConfigBlockV3 -
214 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

ConfigBlockV3: 
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description

1 1 CfgBlkVersion BYTE 0x03
2 1 StartupCount BYTE n
3 120 FixedDef FixedDef -
123 60 UserDef UserDef -
183 2 Tail INTEGER 0xAA55
185 2 RxCfgChecksum INTEGER n This is an internal checksum and can be ignored by tsip users

Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description

61 32 RxDef RxDef -
93 27 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use

56 1 ManufactureMonth BYTE n Month of receiver manufacture
57 2 ManufactureYear INTEGER n Year of receiver manufacture
59 2 SuperPacketPP INTEGER n Super-packet post processing option

DGPS Receiver Configuration Block

sent to acknowledge Command Packet 0x8E 0x7C

0x8f 0x7f

Packet Response 0x8f 0x7f

DGPS Receiver ROM Configuration Block Report v3

The report contains current receiver configuration parameter settings and a software version report. The actual receiver configuration parameters are set using Command Packet 0x8E 0x7C.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 RomCfgId SubID 7f
1 1 PortNumber BYTE n The receiver port number
2 20 Name STRING - Software name
22 1 FirmwareMajVersion BYTE n Major firmware version
23 1 FirmwareMinVersion BYTE n Minor firmware version
24 1 FirmwareMonth BYTE n Firmware release month
25 1 FirmwareDay BYTE n Firmware release day
26 1 FirmwareYear BYTE n Firmware release year (from 1900)
27 187 ConfigBlockV3 ConfigBlockV3 -
214 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

ConfigBlockV3: 
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
0 1 Head BYTE 0x55
1 1 CfgBlkVersion BYTE 0x03
2 1 StartupCount BYTE n
3 120 FixedDef FixedDef -
123 60 UserDef UserDef -
183 2 Tail INTEGER 0xAA55
185 2 RxCfgChecksum INTEGER n This is an internal checksum and can be ignored by tsip users

FixedDef: 
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
0 61 BoilerPlate BoilerPlate -
61 32 RxDef RxDef -
93 27 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use

BoilerPlate: 
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
0 22 SerialNumber STRING - Serial Number
22 10 PartNumber STRING - Part Number
32 17 OverrideName STRING - Product name override
49 6 BriefOverrideName STRING - Brief product name override
55 1 ManufactureDay BYTE n Day of receiver manufacture
56 1 ManufactureMonth BYTE n Month of receiver manufacture
57 2 ManufactureYear INTEGER n Year of receiver manufacture
59 2 SuperPacketPP INTEGER n Super-packet post processing option

0x8f 0x80

Packet Response 0x8f 0x80

DGPS Service Provider System Information Report

This packet is used to obtain information about the current DGPS service provider. Due to operational differences among the service providers, the decoder state and access information is interpreted slightly differently for each service provider. Racal Service - At all times, the user access information accurately reflects the current access state, where “Access information available” indicates that no access information has been received yet. The initial confirmation of user access typically occurs after decoder initialization is complete. Omnistar Service - Once the initialization sequence is complete, the user access information is valid. Before initialization is completed, the access may not accurately reflect the final access state. To help determine whether the user access will become enabled when initialization is complete, the user may wish to look at the activation stop date provided by Report Packet 0x8F 0x8B. If the activation stop date is a future date, user access will become enabled when initialization is completed.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01DGPSServiceProviderSystemInfoIdSubID80
11UserAccessBYTEEnumReports current state of User Access information
24UserIDCodeLONGnDecoder User ID
64FirmwareVersionSINGLEn/aDecoder firmware version
101DecoderStateBYTEEnumReports current state of decoder
111ServiceProviderBYTEEnumIdentifies the satellite station DGPS corrections service provider
122ErrorsWarningsINTEGERBitsetAll current error or initialization conditions (OmniSTAR only)
141ReservedBYTEnReserved (set to zero)
152ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

UserAccess: Reports current state of User Access information
ValueDescription
0Access information unavailable
1User disabled
2User enabled

DecoderState: Reports current state of decoder
ValueDescription
0Initialization in progress
1Initialization in progress
2Initialization in progress
3Initialization in progress
4Initialization in progress
5Initialization complete
6User access confirmed
7RTCM data received
8Decoder reset detected
9Decoder unavailable
10No new RTCM data
11Need data update from master station
12No offshore operation permitted
13Invalid region of operation
14Invalid satellite link

ServiceProvider: Identifies the satellite station DGPS corrections service provider
ValueDescription
3Racal-Landstar
4OmniSTAR
255Unknown

ErrorsWarnings: All current error or initialization conditions (OmniSTAR only)
BitDescription
0=Valid; 1=Expiration is invalid
0=Valid; 1=Current position outside valid regions
0=Valid; 1=Current position is restricted
0=Valid; 1=Current satellite link is restricted
0=Valid; 1=Not available yet (still initializing)
0=Valid; 1=Not available yet (still initializing)
0=Valid; 1=Not available yet (still initializing)
0=Valid; 1=Not available yet (still initializing)
0=All Data Valid; 1=Need update from central hub

0x8f 0x84

Packet Ack 0x8f 0x84

Satellite FFT Control Ack

Acknowledges the start and stop of satellite FFT diagnostics.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 SpotFFTAppId SubID 84
1 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0x85

Packet Response 0x8f 0x85

DGPS Source Tracking Status Report

This packet is used to convey the DGPS tracking status for either beacon or satellite differential signals. Some fields have duplicate meanings depending on the Acquisition Mode (beacon or satellite). In addition, channels with Acquisition Mode 255 (Unused), should be ignored. For backwards compatibility, a minimum of two channels of data is always reported, but in a dual DSP receiver such as NH134, three channels of data may be reported. In this case, the channel block (bytes 3–32) will be repeated a third time for the third channel data. The value in byte 2 indicates how many blocks of channel data are provided in the packet with a minimum of 2 data blocks. If byte 2 is set to zero (backwards compatibility), 2 channels of data will be sent.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01DGPSSourceTrackingIdSubID85
11ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
21NumChannelsBYTEnNumber of channels of data reported
330 * NumChannelsChannelDataArrayARRAY of ChannelDataBlock-Array of channel data
3 + 30 * NumChannels2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

ChannelDataBlock: Channel data
Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
08FrequencyDOUBLE-Frequency
81AcquisitionModeBYTEEnumSignal acquisition mode
91StatusBYTEEnumChannel status
101RTCMUsedBYTEnRTCM corrections are being used
111SNRBYTEnSignal to noise ratio ranging from 0-25.5dB (in 0.1dB units)
121InputLevelBYTEnElectromagnetic field intensity level (ADC input voltage level for spot)
131BitRateBYTEEnumData transfer rate (bps)
141LockIndicatorBYTEnRatio of samples collected at peak and trough. Scaled by 16 to improve resolution at low SNR values
154CarrierOffsetLONGnFrequency difference between transmitter and receiver oscillators (in 0.1 Hz)
191TimeSinceSynchBYTEnTime since last synchronization (0.1 s)
201WordErrorRate_TimeSinceRTCMBYTEnNumber of TRCM word errors (Beacon) or time since last RTCM received (0.1s) (Satellite)
211HealthStatusBYTEEnumHealth status
221DGPSAutoSwitchingEnabledBYTEnDGPS source auto-switching
231NegativeSatelliteWordPolarityBYTEnWhether the satellite unique word polarity is negative
242SatelliteServiceIdINTEGERnSatellite service identifier
264ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use

AcquisitionMode: Signal acquisition mode
ValueDescription
0Manual acquisition mode
1Auto distance acquisition mode
2Auto power acquisition mode
4Dsabled acquisition mode
5Satellite acquisition mode
255Unused channel

Status: Channel status
ValueDescription
0Channel is powered down
1Performing wide-band fft
2Searching for channel
3Channel acquired
4Channel locked
5Channel disabled

BitRate: Data transfer rate (bps)
ValueDescription
025 bps
150 bps
2100 bps
3200 bps
4600 bps
51200 bps
62400 bps
74800 bps

HealthStatus: Health status
ValueDescription
6Reference station transmission not monitored
7Reference station not working

0x8f 0x89

Packet Response 0x8f 0x89

DGPS Source Control Report

Indicates current DGPS source and related settings.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8f
01DGPSSourceControlSubID89
11DGPSSrcModeBYTEEnumDGPS Source mode
21BeaconAcqModeBYTEEnumBeacon mode used to acquire DGPS signals:
32BeaconFreq0INTEGER2835-3250Channel 0 manual beacon frequency for manual beacon mode and DGPS source autoswitching mode (units of 0.1 kHz). Value 0 = unchanged
52BeaconFreq1INTEGER2835-3250Channel 1 manual beacon frequency for manual beacon mode and DGPS source autoswitching mode (units of 0.1 kHz). Value 0 = unchanged
72BeaconRTCMTimeoutINTEGERsecondsTime to wait for RTCM corrections before switching to an alternate source of DGPS in DGPS source auto-switching mode (default: XX sec). Value 0 = unchanged
98SatelliteFrequencyDOUBLEHzSatellite frequency for manual Satellite mode and DGPS source auto-switching mode. Value 0 = unchanged
174SatelliteBitRateSINGLEbaudSatellite bit rate (600, 1200, 2400)
212SatelliteRTCMTimeoutINTEGERsecondsTime to wait for RTCM corrections before switching to an alternate source of DGPS in DGPS source auto-switching mode (default: XX sec). Value 0 = unchanged
232WAASTimeoutINTEGERsecondsTime to wait for WAAS corrections before switching to an alternate source of DGPS in DGPS source auto-switching mode (default: XX sec). Value 0 = unchanged
251CorrectionOptionsBYTEBitsetCorrection options
261SatConfigSourceBYTEEnumThe source for configuring Satellite tracking
272ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

DGPSSrcMode: DGPS Source mode
ValueDescription
0Beacon differential only
1Satellite differential only
2Automatic source selection amongst all available DGPS sources (i.e. in an NH134, switching amongst beacon, satellite, external RTCM, and WAAS). This functionality is only available in the NH134 receiver (dual DSPs)
3WAAS differential only
4External RTCM/DGPS only
5Satellite high accuracy solution only (i.e. Omnistar HP)
6RTK only
7External Receiver Only
8CenterPoint RTX standard mode over network only
9CenterPoint RTX standard mode over satellite only
10CenterPoint RTX US fast mode over satellite only (Deprecated)
11CenterPoint RTX US fast mode over network only (Deprecated)
12RangePoint RTX over satellite
13Reserved
14CenterPoint RTX EU standard mode over network only
15CenterPoint RTX EU standard mode over satellite only
16RangePoint RTX over network
17CenterPoint RTX best subscription over network only
18CenterPoint RTX best subscription over satellite only
19Reserved for future use.
20Reserved for future use.
21ViewPoint RTX over network only
22ViewPoint RTX over satellite only
23CLAS (QZSS, Japan) over satellite only
130CenterPoint RTX Fast automatic delivery method selection
131CenterPoint RTX automatic delivery method selection
132RangePoint RTX automatic delivery method selection
133ViewPoint RTX automatic delivery method selection
134RTX Best automatic delivery method selection
167Galileo HAS automatic delivery method selection

BeaconAcqMode: Beacon mode used to acquire DGPS signals:
ValueDescription
0Manual mode
1Auto Distance mode
2Auto Power mode
3Reserved
4Channel disabled

CorrectionOptions: Correction options
BitDescription
Use WAAS corrections as a backup
Treat the test mode Type 2 WAAS corrections as a valid corrections source.
Use NCS decoder instead of the standard Racal/Omnistar decoders
Use DGPS solution before high accuracy solution has converged.

SatConfigSource: The source for configuring Satellite tracking
ValueDescription
0Manual: Receiver uses the Frequency and Bit Rate value from this packet to track the RTX satellite.
1Auto: Receiver uses its position to automatically configure frequency and bit rate to track the RTX Satellites
255Do not change this setting

0x8f 0x8c

Packet Ack 0x8f 0x8c

Remote Display Info Acknowledgment

Acknowledges when the remote display is enabled or disabled, or when a key press message is received.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 RDIId SubID 8c
1 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0x8c 0x01

Packet Response 0x8f 0x8c 0x01

Remote Display Screen Contents Report

Reports the data currently appearing on the display in ASCII format (except for a few special characters) and the cursor position and mode. This packet can be requested for a single output using Command Packet 0x8E 0x8C 0x01 or it can be configured to be output automatically whenever the screen contents changes using Command Packet 0x8E 0x8C 0x03.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01RDIIdSubID8c
11RDIScreenContentsIdSubID01
21CursorXBYTEnCurrent horizontal coordinate of cursor on display, i.e. character position on a line
31CursorYBYTEnCurrent vertical coordinate of cursor on display, i.e. line number Note that this line number may not match the line number in byte 5 if the cursor is on the other display line.
41CursorModeBYTEEnumCurrent cursor mode
51LineNumberBYTEnPacket contains data for this line number of the 2-line display
617LineContentsSTRING-16-display characters plus a termination character that may or may not be null. It is recommended that developers insert a null in byte 22.
235ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
281FlagsBYTEEnumRemote display flags
292ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

CursorMode: Current cursor mode
ValueDescription
0No cursor is displayed
1A blinking block cursor is used
2An underline cursor is used
3A blinking underline cursor is used

Flags: Remote display flags
ValueDescription
0Home screen state is unknown or unreported
1The screen is not on the home screen
2The home screen is displayed

0x8f 0x8c 0x02

Packet Response 0x8f 0x8c 0x02

Remote Output Line Control

Sent by the receiver to control the state of a remote output line, i.e. an alarm. It is sent automatically if the receiver has been configured to drive output lines. For example, the PSO interface allows the user to set criteria for tripping an audible alarm. To control the output line to this alarm remotely (i.e. the output line is not directly connected to the receiver), this packet is sent.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 RDIId SubID 8c
1 1 RDIOutputLineId SubID 02
2 4 SetHiMask LONG n Set Hi for 32 lines( 1 = Set Hi, 0 = No change )
6 4 SetLowMask LONG n Set Hi for 32 lines( 1 = Set Hi, 0 = No change )
10 3 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
13 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0x8c 0x03

Packet Response 0x8f 0x8c 0x03

Remote Display Configuration Report

Shows the receiver configuration to display data on its own display or a remote display (i.e. a PC) connected to a serial port. It is sent in response to a configuration request via Command Packet 0x8E 0x8C 0x03.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01RDIIdSubID8c
11RDIConfigIdSubID03
21DataLocationBYTEBitsetRemote display options
34ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
72ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

DataLocation: Remote display options
BitDescription
Display information to receiver front panel
Display information to remote display via serial port. screen content and cursor information packet

0x8f 0x8c 0x04

Packet Response 0x8f 0x8c 0x04

Extended Remote Display Configuration Report

Shows the extended receiver configuration to display data on its own display or a remote display (i.e. a PC) connected to a serial port. This packet includes a text description of what kind of receiver it is, i.e. Ag132, Ag124, etc. It is sent in response to a configuration request via Command Packet 0x8E 0x8C 0x03.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01RDIIdSubID8c
11RDIConfigExIdSubID04
21DataLocationBYTEBitsetRemote display options
36RemoteIdSTRING-5-character null-terminated ASCII string reporting a description of the type of unit connected, i.e. 'Ag132'
94ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
132ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

DataLocation: Remote display options
BitDescription
Display information to receiver front panel
Display information to remote display via serial port. screen content and cursor information packet

0x8f 0x8f

Packet Response 0x8f 0x8f

Receiver Identity Report

Report Packet 0x8F 0x8F is sent when the receiver is powered on and can be sent in response to Command Packet 0x8E 0x8F. The packet indicates the type of receiver and why the receiver restarted if an error caused the receiver to reset.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppID ID 8f
0 1 RcvrId SubID 8f
1 1 MachineID BYTE n Receiver Machine ID
2 1 ProductID BYTE n Receiver Product ID
3 4 RestartCode LONG n Reasons for receiver reset
7 7 Reserved BYTE 0 Reserved for future use
14 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

Packet Response 0x8f 0x91

8 4 SwathWidth SINGLE -

16 4 OutputRate LONG n

-1AppIDID8f

31HashIdSubID04
41ResultBYTEEnumResult of the Hash generation

11 + HashLengthEND-11 + HashLengthFilenameARRAY of CHAR-File to generate an HMAC
11 + HashLength + END-11 + HashLength2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

ValueDescription

1File was not found
2An internal error occurred while generating the Hash
255An unexpected Error

Algorithm: Algorithm to use for Hash generation

1SHA256 Secure Hash Algorithm (256-bit)

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8f
01DebugIdSubID93
11FileTransferIdSubID15
21FileDeleteIdSubID03
31StatusCodeBYTEEnumStatus code for delete request

ValueDescription
0File delete failed
1File delete succeeded

0x8f 0x9a

Packet Response 0x8f 0x9a

Differential Correction Information Report

Report Packet 0x8F 0x9A contains information about the last differential correction set that was received and used by the receiver.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8f
01DiffCorrInfoSubID9aDifferential correction information report
12DataSourceINTEGEREnumSource of corrections
32StationIDINTEGERnStationID. For SBAS, the SV PRN is reported.
58AgeDOUBLE-Age of last corrections received and used (-1 if no DGPS yet).
131PartialFlagBYTEEnumPartial flag
141Reserved1BYTEn
151Reserved2BYTEn
162RTKFlagsINTEGERBitsetRTK flags
185ReservedBYTE0Reserved for future use
232ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

DataSource: Source of corrections
ValueDescription
2External RTCM/DGPS
4Ommnistar VBS (when supported)
5SBAS
6Ommnistar HP (when supported)
7RTK
65535Source unknown

PartialFlag: Partial flag
ValueDescription
0Not partial correction set
1Partial correction set

RTKFlags: RTK flags
BitDescription
Single Baseline RTK mode
VRS mode
CenterPoint RTX (std) mode
deprecated CenterPoint RTX (fast RAIN) mode
RangePoint RTX mode
deprecated Tether RTK/RTX mode
CenterPoint RTX (fast RAM) mode
ViewPoint RTX mode
Corrections delivered via radio
Corrections delivered via network (cellular modem/WiFi)
Corrections delivered via L-band satellite
Automatic Delivery Method
xFill is active
QZSS CLAS mode
Galileo HAS mode

0x8f 0x9e

Packet Response 0x8f 0x9e

DGPS Priority Response

Reports the priorities of the DGPS sources that are in use by the receiver. Up to four different DGPS sources can be reported.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01DGPSPrioritiesIdSubID9e
11NumSourcesBYTEnNumber of DGPS sources used (1-4)
23ReservedBYTE0Reserved for future use
53 * NumSourcesSourceInfoArrayARRAY of 8ef9eDGPSSourceInfo-DGPS Source info array
5 + 3 * NumSources2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

8ef9eDGPSSourceInfo: DGPS Source info
Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
01DGPSSourceModeBYTEEnumDGPS Source mode
12ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use

DGPSSourceMode: DGPS Source mode
ValueDescription
0Beacon
1Omnistar VBS
3WAAS
4External RTCM/DGPS
5Omnistar HP
6RTK
7External Receiver Only
8CenterPoint RTX standard mode over network only
9CenterPoint RTX standard mode over satellite only
10CenterPoint RTX US fast mode over satellite only (Deprecated)
11CenterPoint RTX US fast mode over network only (Deprecated)
12RangePoint RTX
13
14CenterPoint RTX EU standard mode over network only
15CenterPoint RTX EU standard mode over satellite only
16RangePoint RTX over network only
17CenterPoint RTX best subscription over network only
18CenterPoint RTX best subscription over satellite only
19Stinger with RTX corrections over network only
20Stinger with RTX corrections over satellite only
21ViewPoint RTX over network only
22ViewPoint RTX over satellite only
23CLAS (QZSS, Japan) over satellite only
24Galileo HAS automatic delivery method selection

5 + 10 * NumChannels2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

CANChannelCfg: CAN Channel Config
Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
01ChannelBYTEnCAN Channel Id (Note: First channel = 0)
11BusSpeedBYTEEnumSpeed of the CAN bus
21J1939AddressBYTEnJ1939 Address for ECU on the bus

41J1939FunctionInstanceBYTEnJ1939 Function Instance used in the NAME field

0250 kbps
1125 kbps
2500 kbps
3850 kbps

Flags: Flags indicating special CAN properties
BitDescription
Inserts a delay between multi-packet writes
CAN channel is self configurable
The CAN bus is internally terminated (Note: Only relevant on hardware that supports internal termination)

0x8f 0xa2

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa2

Position Solution Status Report

Reports additional information about the current generated position solution. It is sent once in response to a query packet Command Packet 0x8E 0xA2, or automatically if configured using Command Packet 0x6A 0x01.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8f
01PosSolnStatusIdSubIDa2
11PositionQualityBYTEEnumA quality indicator of the current solution
21PositionTypeBYTEEnumThe type of position being output
32StationIdINTEGERnStation id of the correction source
51NumSvsBYTEnNumber of satellites in the fix
61SolutionFlagsBYTEBitset
72XFillTimeLeftINTEGERnTime left in the XFill mode in seconds
99ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
182ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

PositionQuality: A quality indicator of the current solution
ValueDescription
0Unknown quality
1Autonomous GPS quality
2DGPS quality
3High accuracy deci-meter network
4RTK fixed quality

PositionType: The type of position being output
ValueDescription
0Unknown position type
1Beacon DGPS position
2DGPS position from External RTCM corrections
3Thales DGPS position
4Omnistar VBS DGPS position
5WAAS DGPS position
6Omnistar HP position
7RTK position
8Omnistar XP position
9Omnistar HP-XP position
10Omnistar HP-G2 position
11Omnistar G2 position
12Omnistar MS position
13Omnistar L1 position
14Reserved for future use.

SolutionFlags: 
BitDescription
Indicates whether the solution is converged. For RTK, this means fixed integer solution.
Autoseed confidence threshold field valid
Reserved (see tsippackets.xml)
Reserved (see tsippackets.xml)
Reserved (see tsippackets.xml)
2-4GPS solution mode (2D3DMode)
Using automatic 2D-3D mode switching
Using automatic DGPS mode switching
Indicates whether the solution is an XFill solution.

2D3DMode: GPS solution mode
ValueDescription
0Unknown mode
10D mode
22D mode with clock hold
32D mode
43D mode
5Overdetermined clock mode
6Auto-DGPS mode
7Manual 2D-3D mode
8Manual-DGPS mode

64ExpirationTimeLONGsecondsSubscription expiration GPS time

Omnistar XP

GPS

0x8f 0xa3 0x02

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa3 0x02

Omnistar XP/HP Status Response

Status information on the current state of the Omnistar XP/HP engine

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8f
01OmnistarXPHPSubIdSubIDa3
11StatusXPHPSubID02
22EngineStatusINTEGERBitsetThe status of the XP/HP engine
41SolutionStatusBYTEEnumThe position solution status of the XP/HP engine
54CorrectionAgeSINGLEsThe age of the XP/HP corrections
94EstimatedErrorSINGLEmThe estimated 3D position error
131AutoseedStatusBYTEEnumThe status of the Autoseed functionality
141PositionTypeBYTEEnumThe type of position being output by the XP/HP engine
154GlobalStationIdLONGnThe station id used indicate the mode of operation
1910ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
291NumStationsBYTEnThe number of stations being used by the XP/HP engine
304 * NumStationsStationIdsARRAY of LONG-The base stations used by the XP/HP engine
30 + 4 * NumStations2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

EngineStatus: The status of the XP/HP engine
BitDescription
The users XP/HP subscription has expired
The current region is unsupported
The user is over the ocean
A problem with the satellite link occurred
No measurements have been received yet
No ephemeris information has been received yet
A position has not been received yet
The gps time has not been received yet
Users velocity is out of spec
No base station info has been received yet
No mapping info has been received yet
Static initialization is in operation
Waiting for status update

SolutionStatus: The position solution status of the XP/HP engine
ValueDescription
0The XP/HP engine is not generating position solutions
1The XP/HP engine is generating unconverged positions
2The XP/HP engine is generating converged positions

AutoseedStatus: The status of the Autoseed functionality
ValueDescription
0AutoSeed is not active for this startup
1The stored AutoSeed information from the last startup is being used
2The debug AutoSeed information is being used
16Initializing the stored AutoSeed information
17Initializing the debug AutoSeed information
128The AutoSeed info was not used due to low confidence
129The AutoSeed info was not used because the expected position, and generated position are significantly different
130The AutoSeed info was not used because user movement was detected
131The user rejected the AutoSeed info

PositionType: The type of position being output by the XP/HP engine
ValueDescription
0CenterPoint RTX RAIN (fast) positions are being generated (for Omnistar HP, this indicates no position). RAIN is now deprecated, this bit will not be set by RTX
1CenterPoint RTX (std) positions are being generated
2RangePoint RTX positions are being generated
4CenterPoint RTX Fast RAM positions are being generated
5ViewPoint RTX positions are being generated
60IonoFreeVBS positions are being generated
61HP positions are being generated
62XP positions are being generated
63Combination of HP and XP positions are being generated
64Combination of HP and G2 positions are being generated
65G2 positions are being generated
66Multiple Source OmniSTAR positions are being generated
67L1 only OmniSTAR positions are being generated
255No position currently generated by RTX engine

0x8f 0xa3 0x03

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa3 0x03

01OmnistarXPHPSubIdSubIDa3
11BaseStationXPHPSubID03
21NumStationsBYTEnThe number of base stations used
317 * NumStationsStationInfoArrayARRAY of StationInfo-Base station information
3 + 17 * NumStations16ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
19 + 17 * NumStations2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

StationInfo: Base station information
Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
04StationIdLONGnThe base station id
413StationNameSTRING-Null terminated base station name

0x8f 0xa3 0x04

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa3 0x04

Omnistar XP/HP Base Auto-Seed Response

Provides information about the current Auto-Seed information held by the receiver. If Auto-Seed functionality is on in the receiver, these values would be used at startup if the receiver was rebooted at this point in time.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppID ID 8f
0 1 OmnistarXPHPSubId SubID a3
1 1 PositionVarianceId SubID 04
2 4 Confidence SINGLE - The confidence in the position-variance pair
6 8 Latitude DOUBLE d The latitude of the position
14 8 Longitude DOUBLE d The longitude of the position
22 8 Height DOUBLE m The height of the position
30 4 LatitudeVariance SINGLE d2 The latitude variance of the position
34 4 LongitudeVariance SINGLE d2 The longitude variance of the position
38 4 HeightVariance SINGLE m2 The height variance of the position
42 16 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
58 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa3 0x05

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa3 0x05

Omnistar XP/HP Controls Response

Provides infomration about the control parameters of the Omnistar XP/HP processor

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8f
01OmnistarXPHPSubIdSubIDa3
11ControlsIdSubID05
22ValidFieldsINTEGERBitsetA bitset of which fields are being changed by the command
41SeedOnStartupBYTEEnumUse the AutoSeed functionality on startup
54ConfidenceThresholdSINGLE-The minimum confidence for AutoSeed to operate
94VelocityThresholdSINGLEm/sThe minimum velocity for AutoSeed to fail
134ConvergenceThresholdSINGLEmThe 3D sigma accuracy at which point the solution is regarded as converged
171StaticConvergenceBYTEEnumEnables or disables the static convergence functionality
181SourceSelectorBYTEEnumSelects source that will be controlled by this packet
1913ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
322ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

ValidFields: A bitset of which fields are being changed by the command
BitDescription
Autoseed enable field valid
Autoseed confidence threshold field valid
Autoseed velocity threshold field valid
Convergence threshold field valid
Static convergence field valid

SeedOnStartup: Use the AutoSeed functionality on startup
ValueDescription
0Disables the AutoSeed functionality on startup
1Enables the Autoseed functionality on startup

StaticConvergence: Enables or disables the static convergence functionality
ValueDescription
0Disables static initialization
1Enables static initialization

SourceSelector: Selects source that will be controlled by this packet
ValueDescription
0Omnistar HP/XP configuration
1GE configuration

0x8f 0xa3 0x06

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa3 0x06

01OmnistarXPHPSubIdSubIDa3
11DebugControlIdSubID06
21EnabledBYTEEnumDebug output is enabled
31PortBYTEEnumThe port to output the data on
418ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
222ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Enabled: Debug output is enabled
ValueDescription

1Enables the Autoseed functionality on startup

Port: The port to output the data on
ValueDescription
0Output debug info on Port A
1Output debug info on Port B
2Output debug info on Port C

0x8f 0xa5 0x00

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa5 0x00

SBAS Satellite Control Response

Reports the current SBAS settings. This packet can contain a variable number of SBAS SV entries. The request or command packet used to trigger the report will determine the number of entries. A 0x8EA500 request will generate a report for all SVs. A 0x8EA500 command will generate a report for those SVs listed in the command.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01SBASAppIdSubIDa5
11SBASInfoIdSubID00
21NumSvsBYTEnNumber of SBAS satellite settings
32 * NumSvsSBASInfoArrayARRAY of SBASInfo-Information for all the SBAS available satellites
3 + 2 * NumSvs2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

SBASInfo: Information for a particular SBAS satellite
Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
01PRNBYTEnPRN of the SBAS satellite
11FlagsBYTEEnumSBAS tracking states

Flags: SBAS tracking states
ValueDescription
0SBAS satellite is disabled
1SBAS satellite is enabled depending on the health
2SBAS satellite is enabled regardless of the health
3SBAS satellite is enabled if in the correct broadcast region and health is good
4SBAS satellite is enabled if in the correct broadcast region. Deprecated, please use EnabledOverrideHealth If commanded, will respond with EnabledOverrideHealth in future versions.

0x8f 0xa5 0x01

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa5 0x01

Use SBAS+ Control Response

Reports the current SBAS+ settings. Request packet is 0x8E 0xA5 0x01
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 SBASAppId SubID a5
1 1 SBASPlusSubId SubID 01
2 1 EnableSBASPlus BYTE bool 1 if 'Use SBAS+' is enabled, 0 otherwise
3 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

Manufacturing Information Response

Provides the manufacturing information of the unit.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 MfgAppId SubID a6
1 1 InfoId SubID 08
2 1 Result BYTE n Result of the command causing this response
3 16 SerialNumber STRING - Serial number of the unit
19 4 UniqueId LONG n Unique id of the unit, used as the base of the Omnistar id
23 1 MfgDay BYTE n Day of manufacture
24 1 MfgMonth BYTE n Month of manufacture
25 2 MfgYear INTEGER n Year of manufacture
27 1 VersionMajor BYTE n Firmware Major Version
28 1 VersionMinor BYTE n Firmware Minor Version
29 2 VersionBuild INTEGER n Firmware Build Number
31 1 VersionType BYTE n Firmware Build Type
32 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa6 0x17

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa6 0x17

Omnistar Id Response

Provides the Omnistar Id for the unit.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 MfgAppId SubID a6
1 1 OmniId SubID 17
2 1 Result BYTE n Result of the command causing this response
3 20 OmnistarId STRING - String holding the Omnistar Id of the unit
23 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa6 0x22

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa6 0x22

Mac Address Response

Provides MAC addresses (4 max) for the unit.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01MfgAppIdSubIDa6
11MacAddrIdSubID22
21MacCountBYTEnNumber of valid MAC addresses returned. Interfaces are platform specific
31Mac1TypeBYTEEnumAdapter type for MAC address 1
46Mac1AddrARRAY of BYTE-Array containing MAC address 1 in hex
101Mac2ypeBYTEEnumAdapter type for MAC address 2
116Mac2AddrARRAY of BYTE-Array containing MAC address 2 in hex
171Mac3TypeBYTEEnumAdapter type for MAC address 3
186Mac3AddrARRAY of BYTE-Array containing MAC address 3 in hex
241Mac4TypeBYTEEnumAdapter type for MAC address 4
256Mac4AddrARRAY of BYTE-Array containing MAC address 4 in hex
3116ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
472ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Mac1Type: Adapter type for MAC address 1
ValueDescription
0Wired Ethernet
1WiFi interface
2Bluetooth adapter
3BroadR-Reach adapter
255Unknown interface

Mac2ype: Adapter type for MAC address 2
ValueDescription
0Wired Ethernet
1WiFi interface
2Bluetooth adapter
3BroadR-Reach adapter
255Unknown interface

Mac3Type: Adapter type for MAC address 3
ValueDescription
0Wired Ethernet
1WiFi interface
2Bluetooth adapter
3BroadR-Reach adapter
255Unknown interface

Mac4Type: Adapter type for MAC address 4
ValueDescription
0Wired Ethernet
1WiFi interface
2Bluetooth adapter
3BroadR-Reach adapter
255Unknown interface

0x8f 0xa6 0x23

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa6 0x23

Alternate Unique ID Response

Provides the alternate unique ID for the unit.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01MfgAppIdSubIDa6
11AltUniqIdSubID23
21IdSizeBYTEnActual size of ID being sent (0 = no alternate ID)
320AltUniqueIdFieldARRAY of BYTE-Array containing hex value of an alternate unique ID
2320ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
432ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa6 0x24

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa6 0x24

Extended Manufacturing Information Response

Provides unit's extended manufacturing information.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 MfgAppId SubID a6
1 1 ExInfoId SubID 24
2 16 ModuleSN STRING - Serial number of embedded module
18 20 FactoryID STRING - Factory specific unit ID
38 60 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
98 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa6 0x25

Packet Ack 0x8f 0xa6 0x25

Extended Manufacturing Information Acknowledgment

Acks extended manufacturing information cmd.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 MfgAppId SubID a6
1 1 ExInfoId SubID 25
2 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa6 0x26

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa6 0x26

Product Information Response

Provides unit's product information.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 MfgAppId SubID a6
1 1 ProdInfoId SubID 26
2 20 PartNumber STRING - Product part number
22 18 Name STRING - Product name
40 6 AbbrevName STRING - Abbreviated product name (exactly 5 characters)
46 60 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
106 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa6 0x27

Packet Ack 0x8f 0xa6 0x27

Product Information Acknowledgment

Acks product information cmd.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 MfgAppId SubID a6
1 1 ProdInfoId SubID 27
2 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa6 0x28

Packet Ack 0x8f 0xa6 0x28

Manufacturing Unlock Ack

If received,receiver is now in manufacturing test mode.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 MfgAppId SubID a6
1 1 MFGUnlockId SubID 28
2 1 Result BYTE n Result of the set manufacturing mode cmd (0=success, non-zero=failure)
3 13 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
16 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa6 0x29

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa6 0x29

Firmware signature response

Responds with the signature at the given offset

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01MfgAppIdSubIDa6
11MfgFwSignatureSubID29
24offsetLONGnoffset into the firmware
632signatureARRAY of BYTE-Sha256 signature
388ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
462ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa6 0x30

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa6 0x30

[Manufacturing Test] GNSS Band RF Spectrum Analyzer Peak FFT Frequency

Response containing the peak FFT frequency and SNR from the RF spectrum analyzer for GNSS bands. Intended for factory test use only.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01MfgAppIdSubIDa6
11FFTTestIdSubID30
21ResultBYTEnResult of the FFT request (0=success, 1=Product Type mismatch 2=RF band not supported on product)
31RFBandBYTEEnumRF Band
48FilteredPeakFrequencyDOUBLE-Filtered Peak FFT Frequency (Hz)
124FilteredPeakSNRSINGLE-Filtered Peak FFT Frequency Signal-to-noise ratio. (dBHz)
168UnfilteredPeakFrequencyDOUBLE-Un-filtered Peak FFT Frequency (Hz)
244UnfilteredPeakSNRSINGLE-Un-filtered Peak FFT Frequency Signal-to-noise ratio. (dBHz)
2864ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
922ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

RFBand: RF Band
ValueDescription
0L1 Band (1575.42MHz / GLN = 1602 + 0.5625 * Chan)
1L2 Band (1227.6MHz / GLN = 1246 + 0.4375 * Chan)
4L5 Band (1176.45MHz / GAL 1191.795MHz)
5E6 Band (1278.75MHz / BDS 1268.52)
6B1 Band 1561.098MHz
11S1 Band 2492.028MHz

0x8f 0xa6 0x31

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa6 0x31

[Manufacturing Test] MSS Band Test

Response containing metadata about the condition of MSS tracking
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 MfgAppId SubID a6
1 1 FFTTestId SubID 31
2 1 Result BYTE n Result of the FFT request (0=success, 1=Product Type mismatch)
3 8 MSSPeakFrequency DOUBLE - Peak MSS FFT Frequency (Hz)
11 4 PeakADCSignalLevel SINGLE - MSS ADC Signal Level (microvolts)
15 2 MaxMSSADCSignalLevel INTEGER n Maximum ADC Signal Level (ADC Bits)
17 2 MinMSSADCSignalLevel INTEGER n Minimum ADC Signal Level (ADC Bits)
19 4 MSSAGCGainLevelPercent SINGLE - MSS ADC Gain Level (%)
23 4 MSSAGCGainLevelDb SINGLE - MSS ADC Gain Level (dB)
27 64 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
91 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa6 0x32

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa6 0x32

[Manufacturing Test] Polaris Register Read Response

Result of a register read operation to a specific polaris device
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 MfgAppId SubID a6
1 1 PolarisRegOpId SubID 32
2 1 Result BYTE n Result of the write operation (0=success, non-zero=failure)
3 1 AntennaId BYTE n Antenna ID
4 1 PolarisI2CAddr BYTE n Specific polaris ASIC I2C address
5 1 PolarisRegAddr BYTE n Specific polaris ASIC Regiseter
6 1 Value BYTE n If the result is success, contains the value read from the polaris ASIC
7 8 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
15 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa6 0x32

Packet Ack 0x8f 0xa6 0x32

[Manufacturing Test] Polaris Register Write Ack

Requests a register read operation to a specific polaris device
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 MfgAppId SubID a6
1 1 PolarisRegOpId SubID 32
2 1 Result BYTE n Result of the write operation (0=success, non-zero=failure)
3 1 AntennaId BYTE n Antenna ID
4 1 PolarisI2CAddr BYTE n Specific polaris ASIC I2C address
5 1 PolarisRegAddr BYTE n Specific polaris ASIC Regiseter
6 8 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
14 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa6 0x33

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa6 0x33

[Manufacturing Test] Polaris RSSI ADC Read Response

Result of a RSSI ADC value read operation to a specific polaris device

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01MfgAppIdSubIDa6
11PolarisRSSIADCReadOpIdSubID33
21ResultBYTEnResult of the write operation (0=success, non-zero=failure)
31AntennaIdBYTEnAntenna ID
41RFBandBYTEEnumRF Band
58RSSIADCValueDOUBLE-If the result is success, contains the ADC value read of RSSI from the selected polaris ASIC
138ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
212ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

RFBand: RF Band
ValueDescription
0L1 Band (1575.42MHz / GLN = 1602 + 0.5625 * Chan)
1L2 Band (1227.6MHz / GLN = 1246 + 0.4375 * Chan)
4L5 Band (1176.45MHz / GAL 1191.795MHz)
5E6 Band (1278.75MHz / BDS 1268.52)
6B1 Band 1561.098MHz
11S1 Band 2492.028MHz

0x8f 0xa6 0x34

Packet Ack 0x8f 0xa6 0x34

[Manufacturing Test] Polaris Full AGC Test Acknowledgement

Acknowledges the test request to perform the Polaris full AGC test. If accepted, the receiver will start the test, and then send the 0x8f 0xa6 0x34 response when the test completes. If there is a problem starting the test, the response will be returned in the result field.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 MfgAppId SubID a6
1 1 PolarisFullAGCTestId SubID 34
2 1 Result BYTE n Result of the FFT request (0=success and test started, 1=Product Type mismatch 2=Previous test reqest still in progress)
3 8 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
11 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa6 0x34

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa6 0x34

[Manufacturing Test] Polaris Full AGC Test Response

Response containing the results of the Polaris Full AGC test. Intended for factory test use only.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01MfgAppIdSubIDa6
11PolarisFullAGCTestIdSubID34
22ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
41BandsPresentBYTEnNumber of bands in this packet.
524 * BandsPresentBandResultArrayARRAY of BandResult-All the band results
5 + 24 * BandsPresent8FrequencyOffsetDOUBLE-Frequency offset (oscillator drift rate [m/s] divided by the speed of light). Multiply by 1e6 to get ppm.
13 + 24 * BandsPresent32ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
45 + 24 * BandsPresent2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

BandResult: Information for a single Antenna+RF Band+Histogram Bank
Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
01AntennaIdBYTEnId of the antenna
11BankIdBYTEEnumType of histogram bank (Internal or External)
21RFBandBYTEEnumRF Band
34DacReadIMPctSINGLE-Return I-Channel UnDecimated %1's Magnitude
74DacReadQMPctSINGLE-Return Q-Channel UnDecimated %1's Magnitude
114DacReadISPctSINGLE-Return I-Channel Sign Bit Symmetry Percentage
154DacReadQSPctSINGLE-Return Q-Channel Sign Bit Symmetry Percentage
195ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use

BankId: Type of histogram bank (Internal or External)
ValueDescription
0External Histogram Bank
1Internal Histogram Bank

RFBand: RF Band
ValueDescription
0L1 Band
1L2 Band
2L5E5A Band
3G1 Band
4G2 Band
5E5B/B2 Band
6E5 Band
7E6 Band
8B1 Band
9B3 Band
10S1 Band
11LGB1 Band
12LG1 Band
13LG2 Band
14LG1 Narrowband

0x8f 0xa6 0x40

Packet Ack 0x8f 0xa6 0x40

[Manufacturing Test] Enable PLT Mode Command Ack/Nack

Acknowledges the command to enable the PLT Mode for WiFi testing.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 MfgAppId SubID a6
1 1 PLTModeEnableId SubID 40
2 1 Result BYTE n Result of the operation (0=success, 1=Product Type mismatch, 2=Calibrator error, non-zero=failure). Consult the ErrorCode for the calibrator error.
3 1 ErrorCode BYTE n Calibrator error code (0=success, non-zero=failure)
4 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa6 0x41

Packet Ack 0x8f 0xa6 0x41

1 1 PLTModeDisableId SubID 41
2 1 Result BYTE n Result of the operation (0=success, 1=Product Type mismatch, 2=Calibrator error, non-zero=failure). Consult the ErrorCode for the calibrator error.
3 1 ErrorCode BYTE n Calibrator error code (0=success, non-zero=failure)
4 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

[Manufacturing Test] Collect RX Statistics Response

Response containing the RX statistics.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 MfgAppId SubID a6
1 1 RXStatsReqId SubID 47
2 1 Result BYTE n Result of the operation (0=success, 1=Product Type mismatch, 2=Calibrator error, non-zero=failure). Consult the ErrorCode for the calibrator error.
3 1 ErrorCode BYTE n Calibrator error code (0=success, non-zero=failure)
4 1 RXStatsStatus BYTE n Status of the RX statistics (0=success)
5 4 TotalPackets LONG n Total number of packets
9 4 FCSErrors LONG n Number of FCS errors
13 4 MACMismatch LONG n Number of MAC mismatches
17 4 GoodPackets LONG n Number of good packets
21 2 AvgRSSI_SOC INTEGER n Average RSSI (SOC)
23 2 AvgRSSI_ANT INTEGER n Average RSSI (ANT)
25 4 PER SINGLE - Total Bad / Total Received
29 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa6 0x48

Packet Ack 0x8f 0xa6 0x48

[Manufacturing Test] Stop RX Statistics Command Ack/Nack

- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 MfgAppId SubID a6
1 1 StopRXStatsId SubID 48
2 1 Result BYTE n Result of the operation (0=success, 1=Product Type mismatch, 2=Calibrator error, non-zero=failure). Consult the ErrorCode for the calibrator error.
3 1 ErrorCode BYTE n Calibrator error code (0=success, non-zero=failure)
4 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa7 0x00 0x00

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa7 0x00 0x00

GPS Position Auto-Report by Antenna Configuration Report

The auto-report settings for the current antenna, sent in response to the corresponding configuration command
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 PositioningId SubID a7
1 1 PositionAutoReportId SubID 00
2 1 FilterByAntennaId SubID 00
3 1 AntennaId BYTE n The id of the antenna to report on (255 means all antennas)
4 4 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
8 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa7 0x00 0x01

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa7 0x00 0x01

01PositioningIdSubIDa7
11PositionAutoReportIdSubID00
21FilterByPositionEngIdSubID01
31AntennaIdBYTEnThe id of the antenna to report on (255 means all antennas)
44ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
81NumEngsBYTEnThe number of position engines to enable/disable
9NumEngsPositionEngArrayARRAY of BYTE-An array of Position Engines to report on (not report on)

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa7 0x00 0x02

GPS Position Auto-Report by Position Type Configuration Report

The position auto-report settings by type, sent in response to the corresponding command

-1AppIdID8f
01PositioningIdSubIDa7
11PositionAutoReportIdSubID00
21FilterByPositionTypeIdSubID02
31AntennaIdBYTEnThe id of the antenna to report on (255 means all antennas)
44ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
81NumTypesBYTEnThe number of position types to enable/disable
9NumTypesPositionTypeArrayARRAY of BYTE-An array of Position types to report on (not report on)
9 + NumTypes2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa7 0x00 0x03

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa7 0x00 0x03

GPS Position Auto-Report by Flag Type Configuration Report

The position auto-report settings by flag, sent in response to the corresponding command

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PositioningIdSubIDa7
11PositionAutoReportIdSubID00
21FilterByFlagTypeIdSubID03
31EnableBYTEnIndicates whether to enable (1) or disable (0) the report
41AntennaIdBYTEnThe id of the antenna to report on (255 means all antennas)
54SetFlagsLONGBitsetEnables/Disables the report of positions with the given flags set.
94ClearedFlagsLONGBitsetEnables/Disables the report to positions with the given flags cleared.
134ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
172ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

SetFlags: Enables/Disables the report of positions with the given flags set.
BitDescription
Indicates this position was the one used internally for the antenna
Indicates that the position has gone through some form of filtering or adjustiment

ClearedFlags: Enables/Disables the report to positions with the given flags cleared.
BitDescription
Indicates this position was the one used internally for the antenna
Indicates that the position has gone through some form of filtering or adjustiment

0x8f 0xa7 0x01 0x00

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa7 0x01 0x00

GPS Position Packet

A GPS Position packet that provides a useful subset of positioning information, including an id for the antenna it came from.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PositioningIdSubIDa7
11GPSPositionIdSubID01
21NewPositionIdSubID00
38GPSSecondDOUBLE-GPS second (since Jan 5 1980)
111AntennaIdBYTEnAn identifier uniquly indicating the antenna the GPS position was generated from
121PositionEngineBYTEEnumIndicates the positioning engine the fix was generated from
131PositionTypeBYTEEnumThe type of position generated. This can also be used to determine the quality of the position.
144FlagsLONGBitsetFlags indicating special qualities of the position
188LatitudeDOUBLE-Position Latitude
268LongitudeDOUBLE-Position Longitude
348HeightDOUBLE-Position Altitude
424EVelocitySINGLE-East component of ENU velocity
464NVelocitySINGLE-North component of ENU velocity
504UVelocitySINGLE-Up component of ENU velocity
541NumSatellitesBYTEnNumber of satellites used to generate the position solution
554HDOPSINGLE-The HDOP of the satellites used to generate the position
594CorrectionAgeSINGLE-The age of corrections applied to the GPS solution
638ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
712ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

PositionEngine: Indicates the positioning engine the fix was generated from
ValueDescription
0The internal DGPS positioning engine
1The internal OmnistarHPXP positioning engine
2The internal RTK positioning engine
3An external positioning engine

PositionType: The type of position generated. This can also be used to determine the quality of the position.
ValueDescription
0An Autonomous GPS solution
1A DGPS solution generated using beacon corrections.
2A DGPS solution generated using SBAS (WAAS/EGNOS/MSAS etc) corrections.
3A DGPS solution generated using Omnistar VBS corrections.
4A DGPS solution generated using an External correction source (RTCM etc).
16An unconverged OmnistarHP position
17An unconverged OmnistarXP position
18A converged OmnistarHP position
19A converged OmnistarXP position
32A float RTK position
33A fixed ambiguity RTK position
48A position generated with a float RTK vector from a base position
49A position generated with a fixed RTK vector from a base position
128A position that came from an input external position source (NMEA, TSIP, etc).
129A 'fake' position generated from a demo or other internal mechanism
255The position type could not be resolved. This is an indication that the firmware needs to be updated to handle a new type.

Flags: Flags indicating special qualities of the position
BitDescription
Indicates this position was the one used internally for the antenna
Indicates that the position has gone through some form of filtering or adjustiment

0x8f 0xa7 0x01 0x01

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa7 0x01 0x01

No GPS Position Packet

Indicates that a GPS position wasn't generated for the given time period

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PositioningIdSubIDa7
11GPSPositionIdSubID01
21NoNewPositionIdSubID01
38GPSSecondDOUBLE-GPS second (since Jan 5 1980)
111AntennaIdBYTEnAn identifier uniquly indicating the antenna the position wasn't generated for
121PositionEngineBYTEEnumIndicates the positioning engine the fix was generated from
134FlagsLONGBitsetFlags indicating special qualities of the no position
171NumSatellitesBYTEnNumber of satellites tracked
188ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
262ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

PositionEngine: Indicates the positioning engine the fix was generated from
ValueDescription
0The internal DGPS positioning engine
1The internal OmnistarHPXP positioning engine
2The internal RTK positioning engine
3An external positioning engine

Flags: Flags indicating special qualities of the no position
BitDescription
Indicates this position was the one used internally for the antenna
Indicates that the position has gone through some form of filtering or adjustiment

0x8f 0xa7 0x01 0x02

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa7 0x01 0x02

Antenna Vector Packet

Provides the ENU vector between two GPS antennas
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 PositioningId SubID a7
1 1 GPSPositionId SubID 01
2 1 NewVectorId SubID 02
3 8 GPSSecond DOUBLE - GPS second (since Jan 5 1980)
11 1 BaseAntennaId BYTE n An identifier uniquly indicating the base antenna for the vector
12 1 RoverAntennaId BYTE n An identifier uniquly indicating the rover antenna for the vector
13 4 Magnitude SINGLE m Magnitude of the base-rover vector
17 4 Direction SINGLE rad Direction of the base-rover vector
21 8 X DOUBLE m ENU X Vector component
29 8 Y DOUBLE m ENU Y Vector component
37 8 Z DOUBLE m ENU Z Vector component
45 8 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
53 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa8 0x00

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa8 0x00

VRS Radio: Status

Response to the 8FA801 packet

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01VRSRadioSubIDa8
11StatusSubID00
21RSSIBYTEnRadio Signal Strength Indicator - in cell phone like bars 0 - 5. -1 = not known
31ConnectionStatusBYTEEnumIndicates the status of the connection
48ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
122ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

ConnectionStatus: Indicates the status of the connection
ValueDescription
0Status is Unknown
1Bluetooth is starting up
10Not Connected to Modem - Idle
11Not Connected to Modem - Trying to connect
15Connected to Modem - No GPRS or Data connection yet
20The network is idle, not trying to connect
21Configuring the Network
22Checking parameters
23Waiting ror GPRS signal
24Starting PPP connection
30Error: Incorrect mount point
31Error: Bad username or password
32Error: Server Error
33Error: Bad Gateway
34Error: Account already in use
35Error: Session lost
36Error: User disabled
40Logging in
45Logged in and ready to go

0x8f 0xa8 0x01

Packet Ack 0x8f 0xa8 0x01

VRS Radio: Ack to NTRIP Params set

Ack to NTRIP params set

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01VRSRadioSubIDa8
11NTRIPParamsSubID01
21ResultBYTEEnumIndicates the result of the data set
38ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
112ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Result: Indicates the result of the data set
ValueDescription
0Success
1Can't set, modem not connected

0x8f 0xa8 0x01

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa8 0x01

VRS Radio: NTRIP Params Report

Report of the current NTRIP params. All strings are not null terminated, and the stringLength value is the number of actual characters

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01VRSRadioSubIDa8
11NTRIPParamsSubID01
22IPPortINTEGERnIP Port (Unsigned 16 bit number)
41IPAddrLengthBYTEnString length of the IP Address
5IPAddrLengthIPAddressARRAY of CHAR-NTRIP IP Address (dotted quad, or canonical address)
5 + IPAddrLength1MountPointLengthBYTEnString length of the mount point
6 + IPAddrLengthMountPointLengthMountPointARRAY of CHAR-NTRIP mount point
6 + IPAddrLength + MountPointLength1UserNameLengthBYTEnString length of the userName
7 + IPAddrLength + MountPointLengthUserNameLengthUserNameARRAY of CHAR-NTRIP user name
7 + IPAddrLength + MountPointLength + UserNameLength1PasswordLengthBYTEnString length of the password
8 + IPAddrLength + MountPointLength + UserNameLengthPasswordLengthPasswordARRAY of CHAR-NTRIP password
8 + IPAddrLength + MountPointLength + UserNameLength + PasswordLength1UseForRTXBYTEnIf set to 1, NTRIP settings will be used for RTX modes, otherwise, hard-coded receiver defaults are used.
9 + IPAddrLength + MountPointLength + UserNameLength + PasswordLength7ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
16 + IPAddrLength + MountPointLength + UserNameLength + PasswordLength2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

-1AppIdID8f
01VRS_RadioSubIDa8
11VRS Radio Config rptSubID06
21VRSEnabledBYTEnIf set to 1, VRS radio is enabled, otherwise it is disabled
31AutoReportEnabledBYTEnIf set to 1, automatic VRS status change reports are enabled
41StreamTypeBYTEEnumIndicates stream type for VRS radio
56ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
112ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

StreamType: Indicates stream type for VRS radio
ValueDescription

1Use dedicated NTRIP client stream
2Use dedicated CLAAS RTK NET modem

0x8f 0xa9 0x00

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x00

Picus Version Response

Holds various firmware and hardware version information
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 PicusVersionId SubID 00
2 1 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
3 1 FirmwareMajorVersion BYTE n Major version of the firmware
4 1 FirmwareMinorVersion BYTE n Minor version of the firmware
5 2 FirmwareBuildNum INTEGER n Build number of the firmware
7 1 FirmwareBuildType BYTE n The type of firmware
8 2 FirmwarePentaVersion INTEGER n Penta-Version of the firmware
10 1 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
11 1 FirmwareDay BYTE n Day of year the firmware was created
12 1 FirmwareMonth BYTE n Month of year the firmware was created
13 2 FirmwareYear INTEGER n Year the firmware was created
15 1 FirmwareExpDay BYTE n Day of year the firmware expires (0 if no expiry)
16 1 FirmwareExpMonth BYTE n Minor version of the firmware (0 if no expiry)
17 2 FirmwareExpYear INTEGER n Build number of the firmware (0 if no expiry)
19 1 HardwareRevision BYTE n Revision of the hardware
20 1 HardwarePlatform BYTE n Hardware platform variant
21 2 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
23 32 AuxInfo STRING - Null terminated String containing freeform auxilliary version info. This should not be relied on for consistent formatting.
55 4 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
59 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x00

Packet Ack 0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x00

Picus Upgrade Ack

Acknowledges the upgrade command, providing information on how it proceeded.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PicusSLMPIdSubID01
21PicusBeginUpgradeIdSubID00
31UpgradeStatusBYTEEnumIndicates the current upgrade status
42ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

UpgradeStatus: Indicates the current upgrade status
ValueDescription
0The receiver is beginning the upgrade process.
1The receiver upgrade process was completed.
0x80The start upgrade request was rejected for some reason.
0x81The specified firmware was not found.
0x82The specified firmware failed for some reason.
0x83The version of the specified firmware is too low.

0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x00

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x00

Picus Upgrade Progress Response

Provides the progress of the upgrade as it proceeds

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8f
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PicusSLMPIdSubID01
21PicusBeginUpgradeIdSubID00
31UpgradeOperationBYTEEnumThe upgrade operation being carried out.
48ProgressDOUBLE-Progress of the operation (%)
122ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

UpgradeOperation: The upgrade operation being carried out.
ValueDescription
0Nothing going on
1Reporting the version information
2Uploading data to the reciever
3Flashing application into receiver
4Erasing application from receiver

0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x01

Packet Ack 0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x01

Picus Logging Control Ack

Acknowledges the logging control, providing information on how it proceeded.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PicusSLMPIdSubID01
21PicusLoggingControlIdSubID01
31LoggingStatusBYTEEnum
42ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

LoggingStatus: 
ValueDescription
0The Logging control command was successful.
80The logging control command failed for some reason.
81The logging control command failed as the directory was not found.

0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x01

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x01

Picus Logging Control Response

Reports the state of the logging control.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PicusSLMPIdSubID01
21PicusLoggingControlIdSubID01
31StatusBYTEEnumThe logging action to be performed.
41filenameLenBYTEnString length of current logfile name
5filenameLenfilenameARRAY of CHAR-The full path of the logfile.
5 + filenameLen2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Status: The logging action to be performed.
ValueDescription
0There is no logging session in progress
1A logging session is in progress

0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x02

Packet Ack 0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x02

Picus Log Dump Ack

Acknowledges the Log dump request providing information on how the operation proceeded.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PicusSLMPIdSubID01
21PicusLogDumpIdSubID02
31LogDumpStatusBYTEEnumIndicates the current log dump status
42ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

LogDumpStatus: Indicates the current log dump status
ValueDescription
0The logs were successfully dumped.
1The logs are in the process of being dumped.
128The log dump failed.
129The log dump failed due to a bad filename.

0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x03

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x03

Picus Ping Pong Rsp

A variable length data packet (pong), that is returned in response to a receiver request (ping). This allows a type of serial communication test to occur.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PicusSLMPIdSubID01
21PicusPingPongIdSubID03
31PongSizeBYTEnThe number of bytes being sent
4PongSizeDataARRAY of BYTE-Data load of Ping
4 + PongSize2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x04

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x04

Returns the IP Address and port number for the VRS Daemon.

- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 PicusSLMPId SubID 01
2 1 PicusVRSDeamonId SubID 04
3 20 IP STRING - The IP address for the VRS Deamon

25 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

Packet Ack 0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x05

Picus License File Ack

Acknowledges the license file has been processed.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PicusSLMPIdSubID01
21PicusLicenseFileAckIdSubID05
31StatusBYTEEnumIndicates the current upgrade status
42ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Status: Indicates the current upgrade status
ValueDescription
0License installation succeeded
1Failed to access license file
2License file not found
3Error reading the license file
4Error parsing the license entries
5License file was empty
6Error writing the licenses
7License file is currently installing

0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x06

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x06

Auto Reboot Frequency Response

Reports the current state of the receiver automatic reboot capability

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8f
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PicusSLMPIdSubID01
21PicusAutoRebootFrequencyIdSubID06
31FrequencyBYTEEnumThe frequency at which the receiver will automatically reboot
42ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Frequency: The frequency at which the receiver will automatically reboot
ValueDescription
0Do not automatically reboot on any interval
1Reboot every 5 minutes (5/10/15/20/etc)
2Reboot every 15 minutes (15/30/45/on hour)
3Reboot every 30 minutes (30/on hour)
4Reboot every hour (XX:00)

0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x07

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x07

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x30

Up/Downgrade Version Info Response

Holds the upgrade/downgrade version floor and related information

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PicusSLMPIdSubID01
21PicusDowngradeVersionFloorIdSubID30
32FirmwareVersionStatusINTEGERBitsetBitfield providing additional status values
51DowngradeMajorVersionBYTEnMajor version of the downgrade version floor
61DowngradeMinorVersionBYTEnMinor version of the downgrade version floor
72DowngradeBuildNumINTEGERnBuild number of the downgrade version floor
91DowngradeBuildTypeBYTEnThe type of the downgrade version floor
101DowngradeFeatureSpecificBYTEnWhether this is a feature specific build
1124ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
352ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

FirmwareVersionStatus: Bitfield providing additional status values
BitDescription
Indicates whether this firmware supports a downgrade protection floor
Indicates the presence of the Trimble support license, which allows you to bypass the downgrade protection

0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x31

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x01 0x31

Is Up/Downgrade Version Allowed Response

Returns whether the upgrade/downgrade is allowed

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PicusSLMPIdSubID01
21PicusDowngradeVersionCheckIdSubID31
31ResultBYTEEnumResult of the check. Up/Downgrade is allowed when result >= 0
48ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
122ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Result: Result of the check. Up/Downgrade is allowed when result >= 0
ValueDescription
0The provided version is allowed
1The provided version is allowed, because a support license is active
-1Version is not allowed due to the downgrade protection floor

0x8f 0xa9 0x02 0x00

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x02 0x00

Pollux Digital Out Mux Control Rsp

Returns the mux settings of the digital output for a particular port.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PolluxHardwareIdSubID02
21PolluxDigOutMuxIdSubID00
31PortIdBYTEEnumThe physical port the Digital output pin is on
41MuxTypeBYTEEnumThe current behaviour of the digital output
51OutputValueBYTEnThe Output value of the mux (0=lo,1=hi)
63ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
92ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

PortId: The physical port the Digital output pin is on
ValueDescription
0PortA (Castor)
1PortB (Castor)
2PortC (Pollux)
3PortD (Pollux)

MuxType: The current behaviour of the digital output
ValueDescription
0Pin acts as an input (Note: only PortC/D)
1Pin acts as an general output
2Pin acts as a PPS source
4Pin acts as a PWM output for radar and other applications
5Pin acts as an audio output (Note: only PortA/B)

0x8f 0xa9 0x02 0x01

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x02 0x01

Pollux Digital In Control Rsp

Returns the settings of the digital input for a particular port.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PolluxHardwareIdSubID02
21PolluxDigInIdSubID01
31PortIdBYTEEnumThe physical port the Digital output pin is on (Note: Currently only PortC/D are supported)
41ThresholdBYTEEnumThe input voltage threshold of the pin
51PullBYTEEnumThe pulling behaviour of the input pin
64ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
102ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

PortId: The physical port the Digital output pin is on (Note: Currently only PortC/D are supported)
ValueDescription
0PortA (Castor)
1PortB (Castor)
2PortC (Pollux)
3PortD (Pollux)

Threshold: The input voltage threshold of the pin
ValueDescription
0A 3.3V threshold is required for the input pin
1A 1.65 threshold is required for the input pin

Pull: The pulling behaviour of the input pin
ValueDescription
0The input does not pull the line
1The input pulls down the line
2The input pulls up the line

0x8f 0xa9 0x02 0x02

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x02 0x02

Pollux GPO Control Rsp

Returns the state of the Pollux FPGA GPO

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PolluxHardwareIdSubID02
21PolluxGPOIdSubID02
31GPOSettingBYTEBitsetThe new value for the Pollux FPGA GPO.
44ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
82ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

GPOSetting: The new value for the Pollux FPGA GPO.
BitDescription
Controls whether Antenna2 has 5V (0) or 7V (1) output to it.
Controls whether power is supplied to the antennas
Controls whether the radio gets 3.4V (0) or 3.6V (1)
Turns LED1 on/off
Turns LED2 on/off

0x8f 0xa9 0x03 0x00

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x03 0x00

Antenna Information Response

01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11AntennaIdSubID03
21AntennaInfoIdSubID00
31NumAntennasBYTEnNumber of Antennas being reported
413 * NumAntennasAntennaArrayARRAY of AntennaInfo-All the attached antenna states
4 + 13 * NumAntennas2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

AntennaInfo: Information for an antenna
Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
01AntennaIdBYTEnId of the antenna
14StateFlagsLONGBitsetBitset holding state information about the antenna
58ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use

StateFlags: Bitset holding state information about the antenna
BitDescription
Indicates whether power is applied to the antenna
Indicates whether an antenna is connected
Indicates whether an antenna is shorted

0x8f 0xa9 0x04 0x00

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x04 0x00

Radar Settings Response

Retrieves the Radar settings
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 RadarId SubID 04
2 1 RadarInfoId SubID 00
3 1 Enable BYTE n Enable the Radar out functionality (0=off, 1=on)
4 4 FreqSpeedRate SINGLE Hz/kph Defines the rate at which the radar frequency (HZ) changes with respect to speed (kph)
8 8 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
16 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum
