63ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
92ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

DigitalPin: The digital pin to be mux'd.
ValueDescription
0Digital Pin 1 on 14-Pin connector
1Digital Pin 2 on 14-Pin connector
2Digital Output on 12-Pin connector
3Digital Input on 12-Pin connector

MuxType: The type of mux to apply
ValueDescription
0Digital Pin should operate as an input
1Digital Pin should operate as an output

0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 ModuleAHardwareId SubID 07
2 1 ModuleAAutoShutdownId SubID 04
3 1 Delay BYTE minutes Auto shutdown timer delay.
4 1 TimeRemaining BYTE minutes Time remaining before shutdown (0 = not active).
5 3 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
8 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa9 0x07 0x05

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x07 0x05

Module-A Network Interface Rsp

Returns the Module A's network interface settings.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11ModuleAHardwareIdSubID07
21ModuleANetworkInterfaceIdSubID05
31NetIfaceSettingLifeBYTEEnumMake the iface settings temporary or permanent
41NetIfaceModeBYTEEnumThe network interface mode
54IPLONGnThe IP address of the interface.
94NetMaskLONGnThe netmask of the interface.
134GatewayLONGnThe interface's gateway address.
174BroadcastLONGnThe interface's broadcast address.
218ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
292ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

NetIfaceSettingLife: Make the iface settings temporary or permanent
ValueDescription
0Sets the iface settings in NV memory
1Sets the iface settings for one boot only

NetIfaceMode: The network interface mode
ValueDescription
0DHCP IP
1Static IP

0x8f 0xa9 0x07 0x06

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x07 0x06

Module-A hardware configuration response

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11ModuleAHardwareIdSubID07
21ModuleAHardwareConfigurationIdSubID06
34HardwareConfigLONGBitsetBitset providing information about the current hardware configuration
78ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
152ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

HardwareConfig: Bitset providing information about the current hardware configuration
BitDescription
Indicates whether Module-X hardware is attached to the Module-A.
Indicates whether the Module-A is powering a G12.

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x07 0x07

Module-A Port-D Socket Uart Server Rsp

The socket uart device configured for Port-D on the Module-A
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 ModuleAHardwareId SubID 07
2 1 ModuleAPortDDevice SubID 07
3 4 TBIPDeviceType LONG n TBIP device type used for Port-D, 0=Unknown, 1=PC, 2=G12, 3=Module-A 4=Freon
7 32 SerialNumber STRING - Serial number of the device used for Port-D
39 1 UartId BYTE n The uart on the device to use
40 8 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
48 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11CBIdSubID08
21CorrectionBroadcastIdSubID00
31OperationBYTEEnumOperational mode of the RTK correction broadcast
416ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
202ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Operation: Operational mode of the RTK correction broadcast
ValueDescription
0RTK correction broadcast disabled
1RTK correction broadcasting server

0x8f 0xa9 0x09

Packet Ack 0x8f 0xa9 0x09

Receiver LED Control Ack

Acknowledgement in response to TSIP Command packet: 0x8E 0xA9 0x08 0x00.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 RcvrHardwareId SubID 09
2 1 Result BYTE 00 Indicates result of command (0 = failed: LED state cannot be changed or receiver does not support this feature; 1 = success)
3 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa9 0x09

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x09

Receiver LED Control Rsp

Returns the state of the Receiver LED. This is in response to TSIP Request: 0x8E 0xA9 0x08 0x00.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11RcvrHardwareIdSubID09
21RcvrIdBYTEEnumReceiver ID for which the LED status is being reported. If LED control is not supported by this receiver, then 0xFF is reported
31LEDIdBYTEEnumThe LED ID whose state is being retrieved
41LEDStateBYTEEnumState of the LED being retrieved
58ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
132ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

RcvrId: Receiver ID for which the LED status is being reported. If LED control is not supported by this receiver, then 0xFF is reported
ValueDescription
17Receiver ID for Ag-372

LEDId: The LED ID whose state is being retrieved
ValueDescription
0LED 0
1LED 1

LEDState: State of the LED being retrieved
ValueDescription
0LED is OFF
1LED is ON
255Either the requested LED ID is unsupported or the Receiver was incorrectly specified.

0x8f 0xa9 0x0a 0x00

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x0a 0x00

8CenterPoint RTX fast over satellite

10CenterPoint VRS
11ViewPoint RTX

1Unlock installed

SubscriptionExpiryStatus: Whether the subscription is valid.
ValueDescription

1Not yet expired
2Already expired

81Reserved1BYTEnReserved for future use (set to zero)
91Reserved2BYTEnReserved for future use (set to zero)
101Reserved3BYTEnReserved for future use (set to zero)

CBC_Status: Flag determining utilized code bias calibration table

0Hard coded CBC table used
1Non-volatile memory CBC table used
2Null table used, special table to be used in lab to determine calibration

Picus System File Transfer Initiate Resp

Response to SysFileXferInitiate command.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11PicusSysFileOpIdSubID15
21PicusSysFileXferInitiateIdSubID00
31xferTypeBYTEEnumType of transfer (get or put)
41fileTypeBYTEEnumType of file to transfer
51resultCodeBYTEEnumSpecifies whether initiate succeeded and, if not, why.
64fileIdLONGnInternal identifier for the stream
104fileSizeLONGnSize of the file to be transferred
1416md5HashARRAY of BYTE-binary MD5 checksum of the source file
301clientFileIdSizeBYTEnSize of clientFileId
31clientFileIdSizeclientFileIdARRAY of BYTE-array of bytes that uniquely identifies file the client (usually pathname)
31 + clientFileIdSize2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

xferType: Type of transfer (get or put)
ValueDescription
0Get (export) a system file from the server
1Put (import) a system file to the server

fileType: Type of file to transfer
ValueDescription
0xFill Premium related files

resultCode: Specifies whether initiate succeeded and, if not, why.
ValueDescription
0Operation Succeeded
1Generic Failure
2File did not exist
3Cannot open the source file
4Cannot open the destination file
5Error occured while reading the file
6Error occured while writing the file
7Invalid transfer type specified
8Invalid file type specified
9Specified file ID is not valid
10Specified file ID is not valid
11File is busy and cannot be updated

0x8f 0xa9 0x15 0x01

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x15 0x01

Fusion IMU Settings Response

Reports the Nav's internal IMU orientation, offsets, and calibration.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 FusionOpId SubID 17
2 1 FusionIMUSettings SubID 00
3 4 AntLevX SINGLE m Antenna lever arm X offset from vehicle position reference point (PRP).
7 4 AntLevY SINGLE m Antenna lever arm Y offset from vehicle PRP.
11 4 AntLevZ SINGLE m Antenna lever arm Z offset from vehicle PRP.
15 4 ImuAngleRoll SINGLE radians IMU roll angle offset for the unit's installation orientation.
19 4 ImuAnglePitch SINGLE radians IMU pitch angle offset for the unit's installation orientation.
23 4 ImuAngleYaw SINGLE radians IMU yaw angle offset for the unit's installation orientation.
27 4 ImuLevX SINGLE m IMU lever arm X offset from vehicle PRP.
31 4 ImuLevY SINGLE m IMU lever arm Y offset from vehicle PRP.
35 4 ImuLevZ SINGLE m IMU lever arm Z offset from vehicle PRP.
39 4 ImuRollOffset SINGLE radians Calibration offset for IMU roll.
43 4 ImuPitchOffset SINGLE radians Calibration offset for IMU pitch.
47 12 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
59 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa9 0x17 0x01

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x17 0x01

IMU-Corrected Positions Enabled Response

Query if IMU-Corrected positions and streaming are enabled.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppId ID 8f
0 1 PicusAppId SubID a9
1 1 FusionOpId SubID 17
2 1 FusionIMUCorrPosEnableOp SubID 01
3 1 EnableIMUCorrection BYTE n When enabled, positions are corrected for roll/pitch/yaw
4 1 EnableIMUDetailStream BYTE n If this option is on but EnableIMUCorrection is off, only yaw, pitch, roll is valid in data stream and positions are zero. When enabled, a continuous stream of 0x8f 0xa9 0x17 0x02 packets are returned.
5 4 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
9 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xa9 0x17 0x02

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x17 0x02

Fusion IMU Position Detail Message

IMU corrected position including roll, pitch, yaw. Stream of messages enabled by 0x8e 0xa9 0x17 0x00 command.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11FusionOpIdSubID17
21FusionIMUPosDetailsSubID02
38GPSSecondsDOUBLEsecondsGPS seconds since Jan 5 1980.
111PositionEngineBYTEEnumPosition Engine used for Attitude correction.
121PositionTypeBYTEEnumPosition type
138LatitudeDOUBLEradiansPosition latitude with roll/tilt correction
218LongitudeDOUBLEradiansPosition longitude with roll/tilt correction
298HeightDOUBLEmPosition height with roll/tilt correction
378LatitudeOriginalDOUBLEradiansPosition latitude uncorrected
458LongitudeOriginalDOUBLEradiansPosition longitude uncorrected
538HeightOriginalDOUBLEmPosition height uncorrected
614EVelocitySINGLEm/sEast component of ENU velocity
654NVelocitySINGLEm/sNorth component of ENU velocity
694UVelocitySINGLEm/sUp component of ENU velocity
731DirectionBYTEEnumForward reverse direction
741ImuStatusBYTEEnumIMU compensation status
754YawSINGLEradiansYaw from north. Yaw is invalid until vehicle moves.
794PitchSINGLEradiansPitch from horizontal
834RollSINGLEradiansRoll from horizontal
874YawRateSINGLEradians/sYaw rate. Invalid until vehicle moves.
914PitchRateSINGLEradians/sPitch rate
954RollRateSINGLEradians/sRoll rate
991NumSatellitesBYTEnNumber of satellites used
1004HDOPSINGLE-HDOP of the satellites used
1044VDOPSINGLE-VDOP of the satellites used
1084ESigmaSINGLEmThe easting error estimate.
1124NSigmaSINGLEmThe northing error estimate.
1164USigmaSINGLEmThe vertical error estimate.
1204CorrectionAgeSINGLEsThe age of corrections applied
1241StationIDBYTEnID of base station
1258ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
1332ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

PositionEngine: Position Engine used for Attitude correction.
ValueDescription
0Engine in use is not known, or could not be determined by this firmware version.
1Engine in use is provided by the legacy NAVCONTROLLER algorithm. Nominally roll-correction only.
2ProPoint Aided INS Engine. Provides roll, pitch, and yaw attitude estimation.
3ProPoint GNSS-only Engine. GNSS position only, no attitude estimation.

PositionType: Position type
ValueDescription
0Autonomous
1DGPS
2Unconverged Omnistar
3Converged Omnistar
4Float RTK
5Fixed ambiguity RTK
6Demo manager
7Unknown correction

Direction: Forward reverse direction
ValueDescription
0Vehicle forward
1NotInitialized
2Vehicle reverse

ImuStatus: IMU compensation status
ValueDescription
0Position is IMU corrected, valid IMU data
1No position correction, valid IMU data
2No position correction, no IMU data
3No position, valid IMU data

0x8f 0xa9 0x17 0x03

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x17 0x03

Fusion IMU Settings Response Block 2

Reports the current IMU Settings covered by Block 2.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIdID8f
01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11FusionOpIdSubID17
21FusionIMUSettings2SubID03
31ProPointEngineModeBYTEEnumReports the configured mode of the ProPoint engine.
41StaticBenchModeEnableBYTEEnumReports the state of the static bench mode.
54StaticBenchHeadingSINGLEdegreesConfigured desired static bench mode heading.
91VehicleTypeBYTEEnumVehicle type for the ProPoint engine.
1080ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
902ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

ProPointEngineMode: Reports the configured mode of the ProPoint engine.
ValueDescription
0GNSS-Only Mode
1GNSS+INS (AINS) Mode

StaticBenchModeEnable: Reports the state of the static bench mode.
ValueDescription
0Static bench mode is disabled.
1Static bench mode is enabled.

VehicleType: Vehicle type for the ProPoint engine.
ValueDescription
0Unspecified vehicle type
1Land mapping vehicle
2Automotive vehicle
3Non-tracking vehicle
4Offroad vehicle
5Slow motion vehicle, such as most tractors.
255Do not change the existing setting.

0x8f 0xa9 0x18

Packet Response 0x8f 0xa9 0x18

01PicusAppIdSubIDa9
11CryptoChipIdSubID18
22crcINTEGERnCryptochip configuration CRC value
41statusBYTEEnumStatus of CRC value
52ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

status: Status of CRC value
ValueDescription
0Failed to retrieve CRC value
1Retrieved unexpected CRC value
2Retrieved expected CRC value

0x8f 0xa9 0xa5

1 1 PasscodeUpgradeID SubID a5
2 1 Success BYTE 0 Indicator of whether or not the password upgrade failed.
3 65 AckString STRING - Text message describing password upgrade operation

32AntennaTypeINTEGEREnumType of antenna connected to receiver
58ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
132ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

ValueDescription

0x8f 0xab 0x00

Packet Response 0x8f 0xab 0x00

RTX Subscription Info

Reports the status of the RTX subscription and shows which connections are available

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8f
01CenterpointRTXIDSubIDab
11SubscriptionInfoIDSubID00
24DaysUntilExpirationFastLONGdaysHow many days until the fast subscription expires
64DaysUntilExpirationStandardLONGdaysHow many days until the standard subscription expires
101AvailableConnectionsBYTEBitsetIndicates which RTX connections are available for use
114DaysUntilExpirationRangePointLONGdaysHow many days until the Rangepoint RTX subscription expires
154DaysUntilExpirationFallbackLONGdaysHow many days until the Fallback RTX subscription expires
194DaysUntilExpirationXFillPLONGdaysHow many days until the xFill Premium subscription expires
234DaysUntilExpirationViewPointLONGdaysHow many days until the Viewpoint RTX subscription expires
2716ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
432ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

AvailableConnections: Indicates which RTX connections are available for use
BitDescription
Viewpoint RTX over satellite is available
Centerpoint RTX fast over satellite is available
Centerpoint RTX fast over IP is available
Centerpoint RTX standard over satellite is available
Centerpoint RTX standard over IP is available
Rangepoint RTX over satellite is available
Fallback RTX is available. No longer supported, do not use (will always be false)
xFill Premium is available

0x8f 0xab 0x01

Packet Response 0x8f 0xab 0x01

Centerpoint RTX fast Network Info

Reports Centerpoint RTX fast Network Info

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8f
01CenterpointRTXIDSubIDab
11NetworkInfoIDSubID01
21StatusBYTEBitsetIndicates receiver RTX fast status
38DistToNetworkDOUBLEmetersCurrent distance to edge of RTX fast network (-1.0 = unknown, 0.0 = in network)
114TimestampLONGsecondsTimestamp of most recent distance report from RTX engine (-1 = unknown)
151ZoneBYTEEnumRTX fast zone in which receiver is currently operating
1620ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
362ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Status: Indicates receiver RTX fast status
BitDescription
Reserved
Centerpoint RTX fast is the selected fix source
Centerpoint RTX fast is actively producing positions

Zone: RTX fast zone in which receiver is currently operating
ValueDescription
0Current zone is unknown
1Inside RTX fast network, position accuracy as expected
2Yellow zone (up to 15km outside network), minor degradation in position accuracy
3Orange zone (15-45km outside network), significant degradation in position accuracy
4Red zone (more than 45km outside network), do not use RTX fast in this area

0x8f 0xab 0x02

Packet Ack 0x8f 0xab 0x02

Centerpoint RTX std Cancel FastRestart Acknowledgement

Acknowledgment that RTX std FastRestart has been cancelled
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppID ID 8f
0 1 CenterpointRTXID SubID ab
1 1 CancelFastRestartID SubID 02
2 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xab 0x03

Packet Response 0x8f 0xab 0x03

Centerpoint and RangePoint RTX Status Information

Reports Centerpoint and RangePoint RTX Status Information

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8f
01RTXIDSubIDab
11RTXStatusIDSubID03
21ConfiguredRTXSourceBYTEEnumThe Configured RTX correction source for the GNSS Receiver
31ActiveRTXSourceBYTEEnumThe correction/positioning source currently being used in the GNSSS Receiver
41DeliveryMethodBYTEEnumThe mechanism currently used by RTX to delivery corrections to the GNSS receiver
51ConvergenceStatusBYTEEnumIndicates receiver RTX Status
64HorizErrorEstimateSINGLEmThe Horizontal error estimate for CenterPoint RTX. RangePoint RTX does not report Horizontal error estimates. (-1 = Unknown or RangePoint RTX)
101WetDryStatusBYTEEnumIndicates the locaiton of the receiver is on wet or dry region
111Reserved1BYTEnReserved for future use.
121Reserved2BYTEnReserved for future use.
131Reserved3BYTEnReserved for future use.
144CorrectionAgeSINGLEsAge of the last received correction information. RTX or RTK Correction age is reported if the configured GNSS source is RTX or RTK resp. (-1 = unknown)
181SatTrckStatusBYTEEnumTracking Status of RTX satellite for receiving RTX Corrections
192RTXSatSNRINTEGERdBHzSNR of the RTX Satellite information
211FastCorrectionSatsBYTEnNumber of satellites with RTX Fast correction information (Diagnostic Use Only)
221FastRestartEnabledBYTEnHas Fast Restart been enabled by the user
231FastRestartStateBYTEEnumThe current Fast Restart state the receiver is currently operating in
241FastRestartFailureModesBYTEEnumFast Restart has failed in the receiver. We enumerate the possible fast restart failure modes. Please note that each of these failure conditions will lead the receiver to pursue regular RTX convergence.
251FastRestartReadyBYTEnDoes the receiver have a valid Fast Restart seed? If YES, and the receiver is shutdown now, then Fast Restart will likely succeed after the receiver restarts. If NO, and the receiver is shutdown now, Fast Restart will be skipped on the next reboot.(1 = YES, 0 = NO)
2619ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
452ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

ConfiguredRTXSource: The Configured RTX correction source for the GNSS Receiver
ValueDescription
0RangePoint RTX
1CenterPoint RTX Standard
2CenterPoint RTX Fast US
3CenterPoint RTX Fast EU
4
5Non RTX Source is configured in the GNSS receiver
6ViewPoint RTX

ActiveRTXSource: The correction/positioning source currently being used in the GNSSS Receiver
ValueDescription
0Correction source is RangePoint RTX
1Correction source is CenterPoint RTX Standard
2Correction source is CenterPoint RTX Fast US
3Correction source is CenterPoint RTX Fast EU
4Correction source is RTK
5Correction source is DGPS
6The Ag receiver is not using any RTX or DGPS corrections
0Correction source is ViewPoint RTX

DeliveryMethod: The mechanism currently used by RTX to delivery corrections to the GNSS receiver
ValueDescription
0Delivery through Radio
1Delivery through Network or Cellular operations
2Delivery through Satellite
255Error or Not applicable

ConvergenceStatus: Indicates receiver RTX Status
ValueDescription
0The receiver is not receiving any RTX correction information
1The receiver is receiving RTX correction information but not using it
2The receiver is Converging using RTX correction information
3The receiver has Converged to an RTX position
255Receiver is not configured to do RTX

WetDryStatus: Indicates the locaiton of the receiver is on wet or dry region
ValueDescription
0The receiver has not determined the region
1The receiver is in a dry region (allowed to operate)
2The receiver is in a wet region (is not allowed to operate)

SatTrckStatus: Tracking Status of RTX satellite for receiving RTX Corrections
ValueDescription
0Channel is powered down
1Performing wide-band FFT Search
2Searching for a Channel
3Channel acquired
4Channel locked
5Channel disabled

FastRestartState: The current Fast Restart state the receiver is currently operating in
ValueDescription
0Disabled: Fast Restart is disabled
1Waiting: Waiting for RTK engine to initialize Fast Restart
2In Progress: Fast convergence is in Progress.
3Success: RTX has converged successfully.
4Failure: Fast Restart failed. Continuing regular RTX standard convergence.

FastRestartFailureModes: Fast Restart has failed in the receiver. We enumerate the possible fast restart failure modes. Please note that each of these failure conditions will lead the receiver to pursue regular RTX convergence.
ValueDescription
0No Failure has been reported.
1CenterPoint RTX Standard is not configured.
2Initial position seed not available. Aborting Fast Restart.
3Vehicle position change detected. Aborted Fast Restart.
4Vehicle speed exceeds threshold. Aborted Fast Restart.
5Problems detected after Fast Restart reported success. Aborted Fast Restart and continuing regular RTX convergence.
6The RTK engine aborted Fast Restart

Packet Ack 0x8f 0xab 0x04

1 1 CenterPointRTXFastRestartID SubID 04
2 1 Result BYTE n Result of the command being acknowledged (0 = Command was successfully received, saved the status of vehicle moved confirmation, 1 = Error)
3 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

253Legacy_ETRS89 Datum, not found in ITRF library, legacy
254Automatic Datum (The picus receiver automatically determines the right datum to use depending on the receiver location.
255Do not change this setting

2Filter is set to pass 1555MHz to 1559MHz

0x8f 0xac 0x03

Packet Response 0x8f 0xac 0x03

Boom Height Actuator Calibration Status

Request for actuator calibration status

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8f
01Boom HeightSubIDac
11ActCalStatusSubID03Actuator calibration status response
21APIversionBYTE0API version
31CalTypeBYTEEnumCalibration Type
41CalStateBYTEEnumEActuatorCalState
51ZoneLocationBYTEEnumEHeightZones
64CalProgressSINGLE%Actuator calibration progress
104SensorHeightSINGLEmetersHeight of sensor. Invalid height is kNullDouble=1.0e+308
144ActuatorCmdSINGLE%Command being sent to the actuator
184DeadbandPosSINGLE%Deadand Positive result - only valid while CalState=eActuatorCalDone
224DeadbandNegSINGLE%Deadand Negative result - only valid while CalState=eActuatorCalDone
264SlopePosSINGLEcm/s/%Slope Positive result - only valid while CalState=eActuatorCalDone
304SlopeNegSINGLEcm/s/%Slope Negative result - only valid while CalState=eActuatorCalDone
344SensorArmLenSINGLEmeterscalSensorArmLength result - only valid while CalState=eActuatorCalDone
381isInvertedBYTEEnumReports if actuator data should be inverted
392ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

CalType: Calibration Type
ValueDescription
0perform deadband then slope calibration
1perform only deadband calibration - NOT SUPPORTED
2perform only slope calibration - NOT SUPPORTED

CalState: EActuatorCalState
ValueDescription
0eActuatorCalOff
1eActuatorCalDeadbandSTART

0x8f 0xad 0x00

Packet Response 0x8f 0xad 0x00

RTX Passcode Information

Reports Centerpoint/Rangepoint RTX Passcode if available. In case no passcodes are available, PasscodeType is set to 255. Request Packet: 0x8e 0xad 0x00

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AppIDID8f
01RTXPasscodeSubIDSubIDad
11RetrievePasscodeSubIDSubID00
21PasscodeTypeBYTEEnumPasscode Type sent by the receiver
31PasscodeLengthBYTEnLength of the Passcode, 0 if no passcode is found
4PasscodeLengthPasscodeARRAY of BYTE-Installed receiver passcode
4 + PasscodeLength4ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
8 + PasscodeLength2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

PasscodeType: Passcode Type sent by the receiver
ValueDescription
3Passcode for Std Cell Centerpoint RTX Subscription
4Passcode for Std Sat Centerpoint RTX Subscription
5Passcode for Fallback RTX Subscription
6Passcode for Fast Sat Centerpoint RTX Subscription
7Passcode for xFillX Subscription
8Passcode for Rangepoint RTX Subscription
255Passcode Type if no passcode is available in the receiver

0x8f 0xad 0x01

Packet Ack 0x8f 0xad 0x01

RTX Passcode Clear Ack

Acknowledges command to clear passcode. Command Packet is 0x8e 0xad 0x01
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppID ID 8f
0 1 ClearRTXPasscodeSubID SubID ad
1 1 ACKPasscodeSubID SubID 01
2 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0x8f 0xad 0x02

Packet Response 0x8f 0xad 0x02

RTX Position Allowed Status

Reports Centerpoint/Rangepoint RTX position allowed flag status. Request Packet: 0x8e 0xad 0x02
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AppID ID 8f
0 1 RTXBSMSubID SubID ad
1 1 PositionAllowedSubID SubID 02
2 1 PositionAllowed BYTE n Should RTX Positions be generated by the RTK Engine. A value of 0xFF implies that the current receiver does not support this feature
3 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

1 1 BluetoothPairingInfo SubID 00 Request the Bluetooth Pairing Status

5 1 PairedDevicesCount BYTE 00 The number of paired devices.
6 1 ConnectedDevicesCount BYTE 00 The number of connected devices.
7 4 Reserved BYTE 0 Reserved for future use
11 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0xb0 0x80

Packet Response 0xb0 0x80

PPS Signal Configuration Rsp

Reports the PPS configuration settings. This acknowleges the 0xB0 0x00 commands.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1PPSIdIDb0
01PPSConfigRspIdSubID80
11PPSNumberBYTEnCurrently supports the output of one PPS signal (PPSNumber=1)
21EnableFlagBYTEnEnabls or disables the specified PPS signal (0=Disabled, 1=Enabled)
31PPSTimebaseBYTEEnumSets the PPS timebase to use the GPS or UTC time of the week, or the receivers internal clock (user-defined)
41PPSPolarityBYTEEnumSets the polarity of the specified PPS signal.
51AutoReportBYTEnIdentifies whether or not the Report Packet 0xB0 0x82 is automatically generated for the PP signal and set to the remote device.
64ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
108PeriodDOUBLEHzCurrently only supports a 1Hz PPS (pulse per second) output rate
188OffsetDOUBLEsPPS output signal offset in seconds. The Polarity of the PPS signal is offset by the specified number of seconds. Negative polarity causes the signal to output sooner, and positive polarity causes the signal to output later.
264MaxUncThresholdSINGLEsMaximum allowable clock uncertainty threshold in seconds. If the Maximum Uncertainty Threshold is set to a large value (e.g. 1 second), the GPS receiver continues to produce a PPS signal even when a position fix is not available (satellites are not tracked). If the Maximum Uncertainty Threshold is set to a small value (e.g. 1ms), the PPS signal is disabled a few seconds after a position is no longer available (satellites are no longer tracked). The PPS signal drifts when a position fix is not available and snaps back to the correct position on ce a position fix becomes available again.
302ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

PPSTimebase: Sets the PPS timebase to use the GPS or UTC time of the week, or the receivers internal clock (user-defined)
ValueDescription
0GPS Time
1UTC (Universal Time Coordinated)
2Receiver's internal clock (user-defined)

PPSPolarity: Sets the polarity of the specified PPS signal.
ValueDescription
0Negative polarity
1Positive polarity

0xb0 0x81

Packet Response 0xb0 0x81

PPS Signal Enable/Disable Rsp

Reports whether the specified PPS signal is enabled or disabled. This acknowledges a 0xB0 0x01 command.
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 PPSId ID b0
0 1 PPSEnabledRspId SubID 81
1 1 PPSNumber BYTE n Currently supports the output of one PPS signal (PPSNumber=1)
2 1 EnableFlag BYTE n Enabls or disables the specified PPS signal (0=Disabled, 1=Enabled)
3 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0xbb 0x00

Packet Response 0xbb 0x00

Primary Receiver Configuration Parameters Report

Reports primary receiver configuration parameters in response to Command Packet 0xBB 0x00.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1RcvrCfgRepIdIDbbPrimary Receiver Configuration Parameters Report
01RcvrCfgBlockIdSubID00
11OperatingDimensionBYTEEnumOperating Dimension
21DGPSModeBYTEEnumDifferential GPS mode
31DynamicsCodeBYTEEnumVehicle dynamics code
41SolutionModeBYTEEnumSolution mode
54ElevetionMaskSINGLE0-PI/2Lowest satellite elevation for fixes in radians
94AMUMaskSINGLEAMUsMinimum signal level for fixes
134PDOPSINGLE-Maximum PDOP for fixes
174PDOPSwitchSINGLE-Selects 2D/3D transition mode when the receiver is operating in auto 2D/3D mode
211DGPSAgeLimitBYTEnMaximum time to use a DGPS correction, in seconds
221FoliageModeBYTEEnumFoliage mode usage
231LowPowerModeBYTEEnumLow power mode
241ClockHoldModeBYTEEnumClock hold mode
251MeasurementRateBYTEEnumMeasurement rate
261PosFixRateBYTEEnumPosition fix rate
2713ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use

OperatingDimension: Operating Dimension
ValueDescription
0Automatic
1Time Only (1 SV)
3Horizontal (2D)
4Full Position (3D)
5DGPS Reference
62D Clock Hold
7Overdetermined Clock

DGPSMode: Differential GPS mode
ValueDescription
0DGPS off
1DGPS only
3DGPS auto

DynamicsCode: Vehicle dynamics code
ValueDescription
0Land
2Sea
3Air
4Stationary

SolutionMode: Solution mode
ValueDescription
1Overdetermined fix
2Weighted overdetermined fix

FoliageMode: Foliage mode usage
ValueDescription
0Never use foliage mode
1Sometimes use foliage mode
2Always use foliage mode

LowPowerMode: Low power mode
ValueDescription
0Disabled
1Auto

ClockHoldMode: Clock hold mode
ValueDescription
0Off
1On

MeasurementRate: Measurement rate
ValueDescription
01 Hz
15 Hz
210 Hz

PosFixRate: Position fix rate
ValueDescription
01 Hz
15 Hz
210 Hz
3Position at measurement rate

0xbc

Packet Response 0xbc

Serial Port Configuration Parameters Response

Holds the configuration of a particular serial port. This includes the port settings and input and output protocols.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1PortConfigIdIDbc
01PortBYTEEnumSerial port for which params were requested
11InputBaudRateBYTEEnumInput baud rate
21OutputBaudRateBYTEEnumOutput baud rate
31DataBitsBYTEEnumNumber of Data Bits
41ParityBYTEEnumParity
51StopBitsBYTEEnumNumber of Stop Bits
61FlowControlBYTEEnumFlow control
71InputProtocolBYTEEnumInput protocol
81OutputProtocolBYTEEnumOutput protocol
91ProtocolOperationBYTEBitsetSpecial protocol operation modes

Port: Serial port for which params were requested
ValueDescription
0Serial Port A
1Serial Port B
2Serial Port C

InputBaudRate: Input baud rate
ValueDescription
0Don't configure baud rate
1110 bps
2300 bps
3600 bps
41200 bps
52400 bps
64800 bps
79600 bps
819200 bps
938400 bps
1057600 bps
11115200 bps

OutputBaudRate: Output baud rate
ValueDescription
0Don't configure baud rate
1110 bps
2300 bps
3600 bps
41200 bps
52400 bps
64800 bps
79600 bps
819200 bps
938400 bps
1057600 bps
11115200 bps

DataBits: Number of Data Bits
ValueDescription
05 data bits
16 data bits
27 data bits
38 data bits

Parity: Parity
ValueDescription
0No Parity
1Odd Parity
2Even Parity

StopBits: Number of Stop Bits
ValueDescription
01 bit for 6–8 data bits or 1.5 bits for 5 data bits
12 bits

FlowControl: Flow control
ValueDescription
0RTS/CTS enabled
1Transmit XON/XOFF
2Transmit XANY
3Receive XON/XOFF

InputProtocol: Input protocol
ValueDescription
0No Input Protocol
1TAIP Input Protocol
2TSIP Input Protocol
4NMEA Input Protocol
8RTCM Input Protocol
16DCOL (RTK Link) Input Protocol
32LBAR Input Protocol
64CMR Input Protocol
65NCS Input Protocol
80Omnistar Demodulated Data Input Protocol

OutputProtocol: Output protocol
ValueDescription

1TAIP Output Protocol
2TSIP Output Protocol
4NMEA Output Protocol
8RTCM Output Protocol
8RTCM Bent Pipe Output Protocol
16DCOL(RTK Link) Output Protocol
32LBAR Output Protocol
64CMR Output Protocol
70Output ASCII from Port A Protocol
71Output ASCII from Port B Protocol
81Omnistar HP Debug Output Protocol

ProtocolOperation: Special protocol operation modes
BitDescription
Reference station mode enabled

Autopilot Configuration packet response

Configuration packet response from the Autopilot Navigation Controller

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AutopilotRespIdIDbf
01ConfigSubIdSubID40
1END-1PacketDataARRAY of BYTE-Data bytes of the TSIP packet

0xbf 0x40 0x00 0x00

Packet Response 0xbf 0x40 0x00 0x00

Autopilot Configuration packet request app version config response

Requests App Version(?) configuration from the Autopilot Navigation Controller
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AutopilotCmdId ID bf
0 1 ConfigSubId SubID 40
1 1 AppVersionConfigPageId SubID 00
2 1 RequestConfigCommandId SubID 00
3 2 Version INTEGER n Application version, lsb = 0.01
5 2 fw_year INTEGER n Years since 1900, 118 => 2018
7 1 fw_month BYTE n 1 to 12
8 1 fw_day BYTE n 1 to 31
9 1 fw_config BYTE n Supposedly: 0 - single core system, 1 - dual core system, but I get 3 from my NAV900, so I dont know
10 1 fw_released BYTE n Whether firmware is released?
11 4 Entry LONG n Pointer to firmware entry point
15 4 ImageSize LONG n Firmware size(program+const)
19 4 Checksum LONG n Firmware checksum
23 8 Name STRING - Product Name
31 4 CompressedSize LONG n Compressed image size(firmware?)
35 4 CompressedChecksum LONG n Checksum of compressed image(firmware?)
39 4 beta_expiration LONG n When beta expires (based on GPS Week)
43 4 ErrorLogStart LONG n Where in flash the error log starts
47 4 ErrorLogEnd LONG n Where in flash the error log ends
51 4 FFSStart LONG n Where in flash the FFS starts
55 4 FFSEnd LONG n Where in flash the FFS ends
59 4 GuidanceStart LONG n Where in flash the Guidance starts
63 4 GuidanceEnd LONG n Where in flash the Guidance ends
67 4 CfgBlkSect1 LONG n Configuration block sector 1
71 4 CfgBlkSect2 LONG n Configuration block sector 2
75 4 AppStorageStart LONG n Where in flash the app storage starts
79 4 AppStorageEnd LONG n Where in flash the app storage ends
83 2 MinFailsafeMonVerson INTEGER n Minimum failsafe monitor version? lsb = 0.01
85 2 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
87 4 CompressedVersionNumber LONG n Compressed Version Number
91 4 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
95 4 CompressedVersionNumberHash LONG n security hash for Compressed Version Number
99 4 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
103 4 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
107 4 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
111 4 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
115 4 Reserved BYTE - Reserved for future use
119 4 FOOTER LONG n firmware config footer
123 END-123 - -
123 + END-123 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0xbf 0x40 0x01 0x00

Packet Response 0xbf 0x40 0x01 0x00

Autopilot Configuration packet request options config response

11FileIdBYTEEnumFile to perform action on

FileId: File to perform action on
ValueDescription
0
1
2
3
4
5Vehicle Configuration File
6Full Configuration File
8

CmdId: Action to perform on file
ValueDescription
0Prepare for file upload
1Send upload data
2Prepare for file download
3Retrieve download data
4Abort Download
5This reports if the given slot is valid and the total number of files

2 4 PosEast SINGLE - Position east

10 END-10 - -

Autopilot Field Computer Heartbeat packet response

Autopilot Field Computer Heartbeat packet response from the Autosteer Client

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AutosteerRespIdIDbf
01ExtDevSubIdSubID4c
11FieldComputerPageIdSubID01
21FieldComputerHeartbeatRespIdSubID01
31AutosteerVersionBYTEnAutosteer version (major*10 + minor)
41AutosteerStateBYTEEnumAutosteer state
51APIVersionBYTEnAPI version (major*10 + minor)
61SystemStateBYTEEnumSystem state
71WarningStateBYTEnID of active warning
81FaultStateBYTEnID of the active fault
91WarningLevelBYTEEnumSeverity of the active warning
101PositionFixQualityBYTEEnumReported position fix quality
112NoAutoAllowedINTEGERnAutomatic mode is not allowed reason
131RoadingStatusBitsBYTEBitsetBit field for roading allowed status
141RowGuidanceModeBYTEEnumRow guidance mode
151Reserved1BYTEnReserved byte 1
16END-16--
16 + END-162ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

AutosteerState: Autosteer state
ValueDescription
0Unknown state
1Reserved state
2Ready to receive swath data
3Swath data is loaded

SystemState: System state
ValueDescription
0Unknown state
1Initializing state
2Ready for automatic mode
3Automatic mode engaged
4A fault has occurred

WarningLevel: Severity of the active warning
ValueDescription
0Level 0 No warning
1Level 1 Don't inhibit autoguidance, post an alarm only if engaged
2Level 2 Don't inhibit autoguidance, post an alarm always
3Level 3 Inhibit autoguidance, post an alarm only if engaged
4Level 4 Inhibit autoguidance, post an alarm always

PositionFixQuality: Reported position fix quality
ValueDescription
0Unknown fix quality
1No position fix
2Autonomous
3Beacon (DGPS)
4RTK Float
5RTK Fixed
6WAAS (DGPS)
7OmniSTAR HP Converged
8OmniSTAR HP Unconverged
9OmniSTAR XP Converged
10OmniSTAR XP Unconverged
11OmniSTAR VBS (DGPS)
12External RTCM (DGPS)

RoadingStatusBits: Bit field for roading allowed status
BitDescription
Roading mode is enabled
reserved
Automatic mode is locked out

RowGuidanceMode: Row guidance mode
ValueDescription
0Unkown
1Rowguidance sensor and GPS guidance
2GPS guidance only
3Rowguidance sensor only

0Unknown state
1Reserved state
2Ready to receive swath data
3Swath data is loaded

SystemState: System state
ValueDescription
0Unknown state
1Initializing state
2Ready for automatic mode
3Automatic mode engaged
4A fault has occurred

WarningState: ID of active warning
ValueDescription
0None.
1Testing mode enabled.
2Sensor simulation enabled.
3GPS simulation enabled.
4Diagnostics mode enabled.
5End of Row Approaching.
6Non-critical fault detected.
7Logging Function failure.
8Manual Steering Override.
9Traveling too fast. Slow down!.
10Application command failed.
11Critical fault detected.
12Autopilot Disengaged. Maximum Speed Exceeded.
13Door Is Open.
14Outside Safety Boundary.
15Offline distance exceeded.
16Degraded GPS detected.
17No GPS data from receiver.
18Operator Alive Switch.
19Operator Alive Switch Override.
20GPS receiver auto-configuration failed.
21NVRAM configuration restored from FLASH.
22NVRAM configuration is invalid.
23By pressing ACCEPT.
24A trained and qualified AgGPS Autopilot system operator must always be present in the vehicle cab when the AgGPS Autopilot system is in AUTO or MANUAL mode.\nThe AgGPS Autopilot system cannot identify or avoid obstacles in the path of the vehicle.
25Cycle controller power.
26Fire Alarm.
27Reverse Operation Is Not Allowed.
28Reversing or too slow for auto. Go forward faster to avoid disengagement.
29None.
30None.
31None.
32Outside Pivot Guidance Zone.
33Outside Guidance Boundary.
34OPS Switch Open.
35OPS Switch Open Too Long.
36None.
37HP is still converging. Using VBS.
38HP is not available. Using VBS.
39HP is now converged. Now using HP.
40HP subscription is about to expire.
41HP subscription has expired.
42VBS subscription is about to expire.
43VBS subscription has expired.
44HP convergence process in progress.
45HP FastRestart has completed.
46HP FastRestart has failed.
47Direction Estimator not initialized. Change Speed.
48OmniSTAR XP is still converging using OmniSTAR VBS.
49OmniSTAR XP is not available using OmniSTAR VBS.
50OmniSTAR XP is now converged now using XP.
51OmniSTAR XP subscription is about to expire.
52OmniSTAR XP subscription has expired.
53Vehicle Roading Lockout Switch is On.
54Vehicle Steering Subsystem is not ready.
55Vehicle Steering Guidance Command Reset.
56No GPS comms.
57Vehicle Guidance Comm not OK.
58GPS Config in Progress.
59Unsafe turn rate detected.
60IMU orientation not correct.
61No steering movement detected - check hydraulics.
62Pitch Compensation Enabled.
63Swath section data starvation.
64No heartbeat received from Field Computer.
65TrueGuide No GPS or Degraded GPS - Autopilot Disengaged.
66No auto - No row sensor and no GPS.
67No or degraded GPS.
68No row sensor guidance and no swath loaded.
69ISO Guidance Status System not ready.
70Row guidance - Stuck Sensor detected.
71Manual Steering Override Needed.
72Steering mode not supported.
73Firmware Version and Hardware Options are incompatible.
74Steering actuator not available.
75Flashing IMU.
76IMU saturation.
77Maximum turn speed exceeded.
78The current vehicle profile does not support NextSwath - please check for an update.
79OnSwath needs to be enabled to use NextSwath.
80NextSwath not available for unknown reason.
81The current vehicle profile does not support TrueSwath - please check for an update.
82Selected implement is not compatible with TrueSwath.
83TrueSwath not available for unknown reason.
84OnSwath needs to be enabled to use TrueSwath.
85Traveled too far while below valve on speed.
86Currently selected swath is not compatible with NextSwath.
87Cannot reverse during turn.
88Insufficient GPS correction quality.
89Unable to use NextSwath NextSwath Connect.
90TrueSwath requires a Towed Implement and a Vehicle Tow Hitch Measurement.
91End of Row approaching - Auto Turn could not be generated.
92Cannot turn: NextSwath is disabled.
93Cannot turn: Autopilot not engaged and online.
94Cannot turn: No next swath selected.
95Cannot turn: Reversing.
96Cannot turn: Too fast.
97Cannot turn: Select Cancel or Accept.
98Currently selected swath is not compatible with TrueSwath.
99TrueSwath optimization unsuccessful.
100PVED CLS Steering Lockout.
101Autopilot not authorized for this region.
102Autopilot passcode not installed.
103Autopilot passcode expired.
104Vehicle width has to be set for NextSwath.
105Cannot turn: Enable Turn not checked.
106NextSwath was disabled because settings changed.
107NextSwath Turn Within Boundaries is enabled but no boundary is defined.
108Too many boundaries for NextSwath boundary enforcement.
109Field boundaries have changed. NextSwath needs to be restarted.
110Boundary not compatible with NextSwath.
111Auto Turn could not be generated within boundary.
112Implement length has to be set to use Stay Within Boundaries.
113Approaching End of Row Turn.
114NextSwath turn actions.
115Display software upgrade required.
116No NextSwath License Detected.
117Too Slow For Too Long. Auto Disengaged.
118EZ Pilot Pro is not available for the selected vehicle. Requires OnSwath.
119Steering Instability Detected.
120Nextswath turn canceled due to overspeed.
121CAN steering gyro disconnected or reporting bad data.
122Roll Compensation CAN IMU disconnected or reporting bad data.
123CAN IMU disconnected or reporting bad data.
124Speed too slow. Go faster.
125Speed too fast. Go slower.
126Turn vehicle around.
127Sonalert Fault: Shorted Out.
128Sonalert is disconnected.
129No usable IMU is present for the configured guidance mode.
130Incompatible CAN sensor types.
131Radar obstacle detected.
132Cannot maintain velocity.
133Follower ahead of leader.
134Follower too close to leader.
135Follower too far away from leader.
136Leader moving too fast.
137Heading difference too large to begin formation farming.
138No Vehicle to Vehicle Comms.
139Vehicle to Vehicle packets not in order.
140V2V comms latency limit exceeded.
141Follower behind leader.
142Follower at end of row.
143Following mode changed.
144Invalid Formation Farming Parameters - Using Defaults.
145Invalid Velocity Control Parameters - Using Defaults.
146Invalid velocity direction.
147Velocity limited by steering angle.
148Velocity Limited by Terrain.
149Velocity Control Emergency Stop.
150Multi-point Turn Completed.
151Follower out of bounds when starting formation.
152Turn is infeasible.
153Vehicle outside boundary.
154Swath endpoint outside boundary.
155Leader cannot drive in reverse during follow mode.
156Vehicle outside GeoFence.
157GeoFence enforcement enabled, but no GeoFence defined.
158The current planner doesn't support curved swaths. Please change the planner type.
159Bring the throttle back to neutral before engaging velocity control.
160The vehicle must come to a complete stop before engaging velocity control.
161Throttle communication error. Disengaging autopilot.
162Vehicle will move in 2.5 seconds. Press the autopilot disengage button to cancel.
163TIM client leaving automation active state.
164Object in vehicle slow zone, slowing down.
165Object in vehicle stop zone, emergency stop.
166Invalid Collision Envelope Parameters - Using Defaults.
167Vehicle Interface: Warning CAN Bus Errors.
168Vehicle Interface: Warning - Missing CAN Messages.
169Vehicle Interface: Warning - Irregular CAN Rate.
170Vehicle Interface: Warning - OEM Propel Joystick Undervolt.
171Vehicle Interface: Warning - OEM Propel Joystick Overvolt.
172Vehicle Interface: Warning - Measured Track Speed Too High.
173CAN Joystick Mapping Invalid.
174CNH Class III ISO Steering: Warning - Vehicle is sitting in stand still for too long.
175TIM Automation in Standstill.
176TIM Development Certificate Usage.
177Remote Control TAP parameters were set to an invalid value.
178The end of row turn planner failed. Please use Agremote to debug the issue.
179Please enable turn within boundary option from next swath setting to do headland following.
180End of segment. Change direction now.
181Velocity Calibration TAP parameters were set to an invalid value.
182Path or command results in invalid curvature.
183Four wheel steer has been detected as active (rear steering angle not 0) but the 4ws TAP is 0.
184Expecting the front and rear steering wheel angles to be equal, but they aren't.
185The front and rear steering angles are in an unsupported configuration.
186Perception system data communication error.
187The command curvature is currently being limited by the speed and roll angle of the vehicle.
188Vehicle close to geofence.
189Speed being slowed - close to geofence.
190Move the vehicle some distance to ensure state estimators have initialized.
191Heading error jumped greater than allowed limit.
192Auto Not Allowed: Press Remote Engage Switch.
193Auto Not Allowed Because: No Operator Sensed.
194Auto Not Allowed Because: Wheel Movement not Detected - Move Wheel.
195Auto Not Allowed Because: Wheel Motor does not have a valid license.
196Please Manually steer to enable steering center position to be determined.
197Look ahead and behind parameters are invalid. Setting values to defaults.
198Controller has Earthworks build and will not function without Earthworks.
199XTE is too high for velocity control.
200System has stopped vehicle - close to geofence.
201JD Cane Harvester Autocal Start.
202JD Cane Harvester Autocal Completed.
203Dismiss geofence warnings.
204Manual speed override.
205Speed too slow to engage.
206None.

FaultState: ID of the active fault
ValueDescription
0This means there are NO active faults.
1This is an operating system fault. It occurs when the inter-task message queue becomes filled for a specific task. This can happen primarily through excessive throughput loading on the processor, or through a firmware defect. Error Value 1: ID of stalled task.
2This is an operating system fault. It occurs when task initialization fails for a specific task. Error Value 1: ID of task which failed init.
3This is an operating system fault. It occurs when the operating system startup fails.
4This is a data sampling task failure. It occurs when the high precision ADC scan (used to sample IMU) fails to complete on time. Error Value 1: Computed delta time.
5This is a data sampling task failure. It occurs when the prior high precision ADC scan fails to complete before the next one begins. Error Value 1: ID of last channel scanned.
6This is a data sampling task failure. It occurs when some portion of the data acquisition sequence fails to complete on time. Error Value 1: DAQ state flag at time of failure.
7This is a data sampling task failure. It occurs when a high precision ADC interrupt occurs after all channels have been scanned. Error Value 1: DAQ state flag at time of failure.
8This error occurs when the internal flash programming voltage jumper is detected as being attached. This jumper should only be applied during the programming of the boot-monitor.
9This error occurs when the internal NVRAM backup battery status indicates a low voltage.
10Not currently implemented.
11This error occurs when a serial port initialization fails. Error Value 1: ID of the serial port reporting the failure.
12This is a math library failure. It occurs when a matrix inversion attempt fails.
13This is a math library failure. It occurs when a double precision floating point operation or function fails. Error Value 1: Type of failure Error Value 2: Address of calling function.
14This is a math library failure. It occurs when a single precision floating point operation or function fails. Error Value 1: Type of failure Error Value 2: Address of calling function.
15This error occurs when the controller suffers a fatal error and performs a reboot.
16This error occurs when an uninitialized CPU interrupt is detected.
17This error occurs when an uninitialized TPU module interrupt is detected.
18This error occurs when an uninitialized MIOS1 module interrupt is detected.
19This error occurs when an uninitialized QADC64 module interrupt is detected. Error Value 1: ID of queue associated with ISR.
20This error occurs when an undefined error is detected. This particular fault is used for debugging purposes, and should never appear during normal operation with a released build.
21This error occurs when an application assert is called with an unregistered error type. Error Value 1: ID of unregistered error.
22This error occurs when the voltage from the X-axis accelerometer is sampled at a value greater than 4.99924 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
23This error occurs when the voltage from the X-axis accelerometer is sampled at a value less than 0.00076 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
24This error occurs when the voltage from the Y-axis accelerometer is sampled at a value greater than 4.99924 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
25This error occurs when the voltage from the Y-axis accelerometer is sampled at a value less than 0.00076 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
26This error occurs when the voltage from the Z-axis accelerometer is sampled at a value greater than 4.99924 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
27This error occurs when the voltage from the Z-axis accelerometer is sampled at a value less than 0.00076 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
28This error occurs when the voltage from the X-axis gyro is sampled at a value greater than 4.99924 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
29This error occurs when the voltage from the X-axis gyro is sampled at a value less than 0.00076 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
30This error occurs when the voltage from the Y-axis gyro is sampled at a value greater than 4.99924 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
31This error occurs when the voltage from the Y-axis gyro is sampled at a value less than 0.00076 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
32This error occurs when the voltage from the Z-axis gyro is sampled at a value greater than 4.99924 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
33This error occurs when the voltage from the Z-axis gyro is sampled at a value less than 0.00076 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
34This error occurs when the voltage from the IMU thermistor is sampled at a value greater than 4.99924 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
35This error occurs when the voltage from the IMU thermistor is sampled at a value less than 0.00076 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
36This error occurs when the voltage from the IMU precision voltage reference is sampled at a value greater than 4.99924 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
37This error occurs when the voltage from the IMU precision voltage reference is sampled at a value less than 0.00076 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
38IMU Overheated
39This error occurs when the voltage from the steering angle sensor is sampled at a value greater than 4.85 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
40This error occurs when the voltage from the steering angle sensor is sampled at a value less than 0.15 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
41This error occurs when the voltage from the manual override sensor is sampled at a value greater than 4.5 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
42This error occurs when the voltage from the manual override sensor is sampled at a value less than 0.3 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
43This error occurs when the voltage from the analog gear lever sensor is sampled at a value greater than 4.952 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
44This error occurs when the voltage from the analog gear lever sensor is sampled at a value less than 0.15 volts. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
45RPM Freq Too Low
46This error occurs when hardware over-current detection indicates a fault.
47This error occurs when hardware over-current detection indicates a fault.
48This error occurs when the sum of the sampled current through the electro-hydraulic solenoids is greater than 3.0 Amps Error Value 1: Sum of sampled current (amps)
49This error occurs when the sum of the sampled current through the electro-hydraulic solenoids falls below the expected minimum for automatic mode operation. The minimum value for CCH vehicles is 0.12 Amps, 4WD vehicles 0.035 Amps Error Value 1: Sum of sampled current (amps)
50This error occurs when a sampled current through one of the electro-hydraulic solenoids greater than 5.0 Amps Error Value 1: ID of solenoid Error Value 2: Sampled current (amps)
51This error occurs when the measured pressure on one valve exceeds 400 psi. Error Value 1: ID of valve Error Value 2: Measured pressure for valve (psi)
52This error occurs when the sum of the measured pressures on both valves exceeds 600 psi. Error Value 1: Sum measured pressure for both valves (psi)
53This error occurs when the sum of the average pressures from the left and right valves is below the set threshold Error Value 1: Sum measured pressure for both valves (psi) ERR_SUM_PRESSURE_MIN
54SF Programming
55SF No Stack Space
56SF Msg Timeout Flag
57SF Angle Out Of Bound
58SF Steering Unsafe
59SF Velocity Unsafe
60No LB Packets
61No RDL Packets
62This error occurs when the 1PPS signal from the GPS receiver is not being detected by the controller. Error Value 0: current 1PPS count Error Value 1: prior 1PPS count Error Value 2: delta time b/w 1PPS interrupts
63No GPS Packets
64This error occurs when the output balance voltage from the ELC steering interface is outside the stated boundary. Error Value 1: Sampled output balance voltage
65This error occurs when the output balance voltage from the ELC steering interface is too close to the +5 V rail. Error Value 1: Sampled output balance voltage
66This error occurs when the output balance voltage from the ELC steering interface is too close to the 0 V rail. Error Value 1: Sampled output balance voltage
67This error occurs when the fault indication from the ELC steering interface indicates a fault has occurred.
68Steering Module Is Not Ready
69PWM Stuck Check
70This error occurs when the expiration date of the currently loaded test build has been reached. The fault will persist, preventing automatic mode.
71This error occurs when the sum of the sampled current through the electro-hydraulic solenoids is larger than the expected minimum for manual mode operation. The threshold for CCH vehicles is 0.12 Amps, and 4WD is 0.035 Amps. Error Value 1: Sum of sampled current (amps) Error Value 2: Sampled current from solenoid 1 Error Value 3: Sampled current from solenoid 2
72This error occurs when the voltage from the left pump pressure sensor is sampled at a value too close to the +5 V rail. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
73This error occurs when the voltage from the left pump pressure sensor is sampled at a value too close to the 0 V rail. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
74This error occurs when the voltage from the right pump pressure sensor is sampled at a value too close to the +5 V rail. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
75This error occurs when the voltage from the right pump pressure sensor is sampled at a value too close to the 0 V rail. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
76This is a firmware error. It occurs when a fault is detected in the VT object pool management section.
77This is a firmware error. It occurs when the object pool message buffer overflows. The object pool buffers message during working set upload, prior to first activation of working set, and while VT is changing data masks.
78This error occurs when a communication error is detected between the controller and the Virtual Terminal.
79This error occurs when the VT manager detects a timeout on a command sent to the VT or on a wait for a message from the VT. Error Value 1: VT manager state Error Value 2: VT manager sub-state Error Value 3: VT object ID
80This is a firmware error. It occurs when a fault is detected in the Path Planning task section. Error Value 1: Assert ID (See Path Planning Subcodes below.)
81This is a firmware error. It occurs when a fault is detected in the VT CAN layer section. Error Value 1: Assert ID
82This is a firmware error. It occurs when a fault is detected in the CAN task section.
83This error occurs when the voltage from the steering fault indicator on the fly-by-wire interface unit is sampled at a value too close to the 5 V rail. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
84This error occurs when the voltage from the steering fault indicator on the fly-by-wire interface unit is sampled at a value too close to the 0 V rail. Error Value 1: Sampled voltage of sensor.
85Occurs when the steering (as measured by the steering angle sensor) is seen to be moving incorrectly with respect to the commands being given the valve.
86It occurs when V Battery to controller is less than 10V, if you are in engaged mode, will disengage the system
87Occurs when Battery V GT 16 V, occurs when you are in engaged mode, will disengage the system
88TempGT85 degrees, occurs when you are in engaged mode, will disengage the system
89Temp LT-40 degrees, occurs when you are in engaged mode, will disengage the system
90FET Open or Coil open.
91Valve wiring problem, short somewhere
92High side coil short to low side coil
93High side short to battery
94PWM FET stuck closed
95Short Across coil
96Low side of coil shorted to Battery
97Either short across coil or Low side shorted to battery , high current drawn
98Short inside the controller one of the FETS is stuck please return for service
99Internal current measurement has a problem. Please return to service if problem Persists
100Task intialization
101Msg buffer overrun
102FE lock overrun
103Task stack overflow
104FE msg no space
105FE bad task ID
106Short inside the controller one of the FETS is stuck please return for service
107FE send msg too big
108CAN Operator Presence Switch Status message not received within specified interval.
109CAN Operator Presence Switch Status message indicates an error state for switch status.
110CAN Autoguidance message not received within specified interval.
111CAN Autoguidance message indicates an invalid or faulted state for the vehicle direction estimate. (state GT 2). Estimate of vehicle direction is unknown.
112CAN Autoguidance message indicates an invalid or faulted state for the IsolationValve Status. (state = 2). Valve cannot be controlled.
113CAN Autoguidance message indicates that the IsolationValve state is inconsistent with that being commanded by the Navigation controller. Either the Isolation Valve indicates Manual Mode when Automatic has been commanded, or Automatic Mode when Manual has been commanded by the Navigation controller.
114The main steering override quadrature encoder, used by John Deere Autotrac system, was not detected by the Navigation controller
115The orientation of the controller with respect to the vehicle local level has been determined to be inconsistent with the calibrated alignment angles. This check is performed only once per power cycle.
116The tractor is seen to be traveling above 2.0 m/s and no wheel speed sensor input has been detected. This check is performed only once per power cycle.
117The backup steering override quadrature encoder, used by John Deere Autotrac system, was not detected by the Navigation controller
118A guidance CAN packet has timed out. For example the ISO 11783 packet. Error values: Message type: See CAN message types in Appendix. Time since the message should have been received. Introduced in 5.30: 1 if at least one message has been received, otherwise 0.
119The ‘Steering Override’ Parameter sent by the CAT MT steering system has value 2 (error)
120The ‘Steering Override’ Parameter Parameter sent by the CAT MT steering system has value 3 (unavailable)
121The ‘Gear Lever’ Parameter sent by the CAT MT steering system has value 14 (error)
122The ‘Gear Lever’ Parameter sent by the CAT MT steering system has value 15 (unavailable)
123The ‘Gear Lever’ Parameter sent by the CAT MT steering system has value has value 4 – 13 (unknown state)
124The ‘Road Lockout Switch’ Parameter sent by the CAT MT steering system has value 2 (error)
125The ‘Road Lockout Switch’ Parameter sent by the CAT MT steering system has value 3 (unavailable)
126The ‘Steering Command Reset Required’ Parameter sent by the CAT MT steering system has value 2 (error)
127The ‘Steering Command Reset Required’ Parameter sent by the CAT MT steering system has value 3 (unavailable)
128The ‘Steering subsystem Ready’ Parameter sent by the CAT MT steering system has value 2 (error)
129The ‘Steering subsystem Ready’ Parameter sent by the CAT MT steering system has value 3 (unavailable)
130The ‘CAN Communication Status’ Parameter sent by the CAT MT steering system has value 2 (error)
131The ‘CAN Communication Status’ Parameter sent by the CAT MT steering system has value 3 (unavailable)
132The Manual_Override_SW Parameter sent by the CNH Flagship Combine in the Steering Info message has a value of 2 (=Error)
133The Manual_Override_SW Parameter sent by the CNH Flagship Combine in the Steering Info message has a value of 3 (=Not Available)
134The Vehicle_Direction Parameter sent by the CNH Flagship Combine in the Steering Info message has a value of 3 (=Not Available; Direction Not Known)
135The Vehicle_Ground_Speed Parameter sent by the CNH Flagship Combine in the Steering Info message has a value of 0xFC00 (=Not Calibrated)
136The Vehicle_Ground_Speed Parameter sent by the CNH Flagship Combine in the Steering Info message has a value of 0xFE00 (=Error)
137The Vehicle_Ground_Speed Parameter sent by the CNH Flagship Combine in the Steering Info message has a value of 0xFF00 (=Not Available)
138The Actual_Steering_Curvature Parameter sent by the CNH Flagship Combine in the Steering Info message has a value of 0xFC00 (=Not Calibrated)
139The Actual_Steering_Curvature Parameter sent by the CNH Flagship Combine in the Steering Info message has a value of 0xFE00 (=Error)
140The Actual_Steering_Curvature Parameter sent by the CNH Flagship Combine in the Steering Info message has a value of 0xFF00 (=Not Available)
141The Steer_Left_Valve_Status Parameter sent by the CNH Flagship Combine in the Steering Status message has a value of 2 (=Error Condition; Cannot Control Valve)
142The Steer_Left_Valve_Status Parameter sent by the CNH Flagship Combine in the Steering Status message has a value of 3 (=Not Available; Not Installed)
143The Steer_Right_Valve_Status Parameter sent by the CNH Flagship Combine in the Steering Status message has a value of 2 (=Error Condition; Cannot Control Valve)
144The Steer_Right_Valve_Status Parameter sent by the CNH Flagship Combine in the Steering Status message has a value of 3 (=Not Available; Not Installed)
145The Actual_System_Aggressiveness Parameter sent by the CNH Flagship Combine in the Steering Status message has a value of 0xFC (=Not Calibrated; Calibration Not Done Yet)
146The Actual_System_Aggressiveness Parameter sent by the CNH Flagship Combine in the Steering Status message has a value of 0xFE (=Error, eg. Value LT 50% or GT 150 %)
147The Actual_System_Aggressiveness Parameter sent by the CNH Flagship Combine in the Steering Status message has a value of 0xFF (=Not Available)
148The xCM3_Guidance_Status Parameter sent by the CNH Flagship Combine in the Steering Status message has a value of 0 (=Disabled) or 2 (=Calibrate)
149The xCM3_STEERING_INFO message has not been received by the Autopilot controller for more than 800 ms (3500 ms right after start-up)
150The xCM3_STEERING_STATUS message has not been received by the Autopilot controller for more than 800 ms (3500 ms right after start-up)
151The ‘Inching Pedal Depressed’ Parameter sent by the CAT MT steering system has value 2 (error)
152The ‘Inching Pedal Depressed’ Parameter sent by the CAT MT steering system has value 3 (unavailable)
153The Manual_Override_SW Parameter has an invalid value
154The Steer_Left_Valve_Status Parameter has an invalid value
155The Steer_Right_Valve_Status Parameter has an invalid value
156The controller receives too many 1pps interrupts.
157This error occurs when a TAP parameter fails a sanity check. Error Value 1: Which TAP is out of bounds. see - Tap Sanity Checks Error Value 2: The value of that TAP.
158ERR_JD_FBW_STATUS_WRONG = 158, /* system is engaged but status from new Interface box says it is not*/ The John Deere xx30T steering interface box indicates manual mode after the software application initiates the engage command. This fault applies only to John Deere xx30T vehicles.
159This error occurs when a status message from Maestro controller (used in CNH Cane Harvester) shows fault Error Value 1: Fault code
160Diag Assert
161This error occurs whenever Row Crop Sensor A (for CNH it would be Left Sensor) has an voltage below the minimum of 0.25V (indicating open)
162This error occurs whenever Row Crop Sensor A (for CNH it would be Left Sensor) has an voltage above the maximum 4.75V (indicating shorted to supply)
163This error occurs in GARU equipped vehicles when the XCM2NAVII packet shows Remote engage request has an error.
164Used to be an XCM CAN Autoguidance engage not available message
165This error occurs whenever Row Crop Sensor B (for CNH it would be Left Sensor) has a voltage below the minimum of 0.25V (indicating open).
166This error occurs whenever Row Crop Sensor B (for CNH it would be Left Sensor) has a voltage above the maximum 4.75V (indicating shorted to supply).
167Timeout in receiving Maestro status message (1 second timeout).
168Timeout in receiving CNH XCM Engine speed status packet (2 seconds).
169Quad encoder manual override sensor overvoltage – greater than 4.85V.
170Quad encoder manual override sensor undervoltage – less than 0.15V.
171Quad encoder manual override channels (1 and 2) are shorted to each other.
172CAN File system command error
173Memory backup restoring during initialization failed. Possible reflashing occurred. Error value 1 = 257 means that the checksum is erroneous..
174Profile has a setting for minimum firmware version with which it works. It that is not met with, this fault is thrown. Currently for AM only.
175Software reboot causes a fault to be thrown which will be logged along with data values showing reason for reboot.
176This error is thrown by NAVII when Sauer Danfoss Valve enable failure is indicated in the CAN message sent by Danfoss.
177JD CAN state machine change did not take place as expected within the timeout. (Note: Faults 182, 183, and 184 can be considered special cases of this fault.) Error values: 1 Autopilot state of secure authentication. See Appendix.
178JD CAN Guidance Status Limit indicates fault. Error values: 1 JD CAN exit code, 2 UI message code. See Appendix.
179ISO Guidance status message packet shows error status in one or more of the parameters.
180JD CAN Guidance Limit Status value coming from the vehicle indicating that Limit Status is not available. Error values: 1 JD CAN exit code, 2 UI message code. See Appendix.
181JD CAN Guidance Limit Status value coming from the vehicle indicating that Limit Status is reserved. Error values: 1 JD CAN exit code, 2 UI message code. See Appendix.
182CAN message “Guidance Status Message” was not received from controller. Error values: None.
183JD proprietary Secure Authentication failed (for reasons other than a missing activation code). Error values: 1 JD state of secure authentication. See Appendix.
184JD proprietary Secure Authentication failed because no activation code for external guidance was entered on the JD vehicle. Error values: None.
185A can message has timed out. Error values: Message type: See CAN message types in Appendix. Time since the message should have been received. Introduced in 5.30: 1 if at least one message has been received, otherwise 0.
186Line acquisition path planning failed.
187The display and receiver combination are not supported by the Nav. Error values: 1 Display type, 2 receiver type. See Appendix.
188The vehicle is using a steering mode that is not supported by Autopilot (such as 4 wheel steering or crab steering).
189The Steering Actuator is reporting an error. First Value is fault code from Cuda. See Cuda (SAM-200) Fault Codes
190Remote engage switch is inverted or pressed for too long.
191The IMU processor has not responded to any configuration commands or request for data.
192A failure occurred when attempting to upgrade the firmware on the IMU processor.
193The IMU is reporting an error.
194The data from the IMU is invalid.
195The selected vehicle profile cannot work with this version of Autopilot hardware.
196Error with NextSwath End of Row turn
197Bron Tile Plow reports error with valve coils Error value 1 indicates which coil is faulting Value Faulted Coil 1 Grade up Valve Coil Fault 2 Grade Down Valve Coil Fault 3 Pitch UP Valve Coil Fault 4 Pitch Down Valve Coil Fault 5 Left EDC Valve Coil Fault 6 Right EDC Valve Coil Fault
198ClaasAuthenticationFailed
199ClaasAliveMessageTimeout
2001: Reported Major Version2: Reported Minor Version3: Reported Build Number
201ValuesSuspectParamNum (SPN) - data item. See the spec sheet for the actuator. FailureModeIdentifier (FMI) - what happened. See the spec sheet for the actuator. LampStatus - 0x00 = no fault, 0x04 = Warning Info Error, 0x10 = Critical Error
202Maximum pwm period for this specific platform Current pwm period 0.0f
203Error Value 1: HFX Fault Index Error Value 2: Reserved Error Value 3: Reserved
2041: eBATSCommsErrors (see enum below) 2: Depends on the first value, usually the received value (speed, source, side, etc...) 3: 0.0f Errors defined in eBATSCommsErrors, in cn/bats.h: noError = 0, speedValueInvalid = 1, // Reported speed out of range speedSourceInvalid = 2, // Reported source out of range brakeApplySideInvalid = 3, // Reported brake apply side out of range sequenceNumberInvalid = 4, // Sequence number other than expected value received errorReportedByBatsECU = 5, // BATS ECU reported fault. See BATS CAN comms spec for fault values
205Velociraptor heartbeat failed to receive for too long
206Claas ICT Error (e.g. Authentication failed, synchronization failed, command failed, etc) Error Value 1: Function affected 1 == Cruise Control 3 == Rear PTO 5 == Hydraulics 7 == Synchronization 9 == Authentication Error Value 2: Reserved Error Value 3: Reserved
207Something is wrong with the received CAN heartbeat Error Value 1: 0 == Sequence Number Error 1 == Invalid State Reported or Requested 2 == System Fault reported by Vehicle ECU Error Value 2: Reserved Error Value 3: Reserved
208A fault during vehicle trajectory generation. Details are in the error values. Error Value 1: Reserved. Report to developers. Error Value 2: Reserved. Report to developers. Error Value 3: Reserved. Report to developers.
209VehicleInterfaceCanHandlerError
210VehicleInterfaceCanControllerNotFound
211VehicleInterfaceUnconfigured
212VehicleInterfaceCanHeartbeatError
213VehicleInterfaceCanHeartbeatTimeout
214VehicleInterfaceCanHorizontalCommandError
215VehicleInterfaceCanHorizontalCommandTimeout
216VehicleInterfaceOemSteeringJoystickUndervolt
217VehicleInterfaceOemSteeringJoystickOvervolt
218VehicleInterfaceUnused
219VehicleInterfaceMasterEnableDriverError
220VehicleInterfaceMasterEnableOvervolt
221VehicleInterfaceReferenceGroundError
222VehicleInterfaceReferenceGroundOvervolt
223VehicleInterfaceProportionalDriverError
224VehicleInterfaceProportionalDriverOvervolt
225VehicleInterfaceMemoryError
226VehicleInterfaceHardwareFailure
227VehicleInterfaceApplicationFailure
228VehicleInterfaceUndefinedErrorTypeDebuggingOnly
229VehicleInterfaceSensorPowerSupplyUndervolt
230VehicleInterfaceSensorPowerSupplyOvervolt
231VehicleInterfaceConfigurationParameterSanityCheckFailure
232VehicleInterfaceCalibrationParameterSanityCheckFailure
233VehicleInterfaceApplicationParameterSanityCheckFailure
234ErrSteeringOverCanEngagedWhenSystemDisengaged
235Attempted to engage autosteering but the SAM motor didn't respond with a state change to RUN
236The Steering Actuator is reporting an error. First Value is fault code from Turtle. See Turtle Fault Codes for list of faults

WarningLevel: Severity of the active warning
ValueDescription
0Level 0 No warning
1Level 1 Don't inhibit autoguidance, post an alarm only if engaged
2Level 2 Don't inhibit autoguidance, post an alarm always
3Level 3 Inhibit autoguidance, post an alarm only if engaged
4Level 4 Inhibit autoguidance, post an alarm always

PositionFixQuality: Reported position fix quality
ValueDescription
0Unknown fix quality
1No position fix
2Autonomous
3Beacon (DGPS)
4RTK Float
5RTK Fixed
6WAAS (DGPS)
7OmniSTAR HP Converged
8OmniSTAR HP Unconverged
9OmniSTAR XP Converged
10OmniSTAR XP Unconverged
11OmniSTAR VBS (DGPS)
12External RTCM (DGPS)

NoAutoAllowedReason: ID of the UI message that contains the reason autoguidance is not allowed.
