RoadingStatusBits: Bit field for roading allowed status
BitDescription
Roading mode is enabled
reserved
Automatic mode is locked out

RowGuidanceMode: Row guidance mode
ValueDescription
0Unkown
1Rowguidance sensor and GPS guidance
2GPS guidance only
3Rowguidance sensor only

VelocityControlState: Current state of velocity control
ValueDescription
0Unknown velocity control state
1Velocity control is in an error state
2Velocity control is in standby
3Velocity control is engaged
4Velocity control is disabled

0xbf 0x4c 0x01 0x20

Packet Response 0xbf 0x4c 0x01 0x20

Autopilot Full Field Automation Path for Field Computer

Path to be plotted on Field Computer map. Reference point is first point of path (only plot for first message). Included with all packets though.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AutopilotRespIdIDbf
01?? external deviceSubID4c
11?? page id field computerSubID01
21?? field computer pathSubID20
31PathTypeBYTEEnumPath of vehicle or implement? Turn or line acquisition.
41NumberOfPointsBYTEnNumber of points, not counting reference point.
51PacketNumberBYTEnPath might be split over multiple packets. Number of this packet.
61LastPacketBYTEnTrue if this is the last packet for this path.
78ReferenceLatitudeDOUBLE-Reference point: Latitude.
158ReferenceLongitudeDOUBLE-Reference point: Longitude.
238 * NumberOfPointsPointsARRAY of PointDiff-Points of path defined as Lat/Lon diff to the reference point
23 + 8 * NumberOfPointsEND-23 + 8 * NumberOfPoints--
23 + 8 * NumberOfPoints + END-23 + 8 * NumberOfPoints2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

PathType: Path of vehicle or implement? Turn or line acquisition.
ValueDescription
0Vehicle, line acquisition
1Implement, line acquisition
2Vehicle, end of row turn
3Implement, end of row turn

PointDiff: 
Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
04LatitudeDiffSINGLE-
44LongitudeDiffSINGLE-

0xbf 0x4c 0x05 0x00

Packet Response 0xbf 0x4c 0x05 0x00

Autopilot External Device Opis Firmware Update Progress

Sent by the NAV to the display when updating an opis device, like a SAM-300

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AutopilotRespIdIDbf
01ExtDevSubIdSubID4c
11OpisPageIdSubID05
21FirmwareUpdateCmdIdSubID00
31OperationStateBYTEEnumOpis firmware update operation state
41ProgressBYTE%Firmware update progress. 0% to 100%
51DeviceTypeBYTEnThe opis device ID
6END-6--
6 + END-62ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

OperationState: Opis firmware update operation state
ValueDescription
0Preparing
1Transferring
2Installing

0xbf 0x4c 0x20 0x00

Packet Response 0xbf 0x4c 0x20 0x00

Autopilot External Device guidance status enable response

External Device packet response to return the guidance status enable
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AutopilotRespId ID bf
0 1 ExtDevSubId SubID 4c
1 1 GuidanceStatusId SubID 20
2 1 GuidanceStatusEnableCmdId SubID 00
3 1 Enabled BYTE BOOL Automatic update enable
4 END-4 - -
4 + END-4 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0xbf 0x4c 0x20 0x01

Packet Response 0xbf 0x4c 0x20 0x01

Autopilot External Device guidance status response

External Device packet response to update the guidance status

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AutopilotRespIdIDbf
01ExtDevSubIdSubID4c
11GuidanceStatusIdSubID20
21GuidanceStatusCmdIdSubID01
34CanIdLONGnCAN Id of the sender of the guidance status message
71CanMsgLengthBYTEnLength of the CAN message
83ReservedBYTE0Reserved for future use
11CanMsgLengthCanMsgARRAY of BYTE-The CAN message
11 + CanMsgLengthEND-11 + CanMsgLength--
11 + CanMsgLength + END-11 + CanMsgLength2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

0xbf 0x4c 0x23 0x00

Packet Response 0xbf 0x4c 0x23 0x00

Clothoid New Path Response

Clothoid New Path Response

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AutopilotRespIdIDbf
01external deviceSubID4c
11Clothoid APISubID23
21New pathSubID00
31typeBYTEEnumDefine how to use points in clothoid AppendPath packet
4END-4--
4 + END-42ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

type: Define how to use points in clothoid AppendPath packet
ValueDescription
0Clothoid points have XYZ and distance, heading, curvature

0xbf 0x4c 0x23 0x01

Packet Response 0xbf 0x4c 0x23 0x01

Clothoid Append Path Response

Clothoid Append Path Response
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AutosteerCmdId ID bf
0 1 external device SubID 4c
1 1 Clothoid API SubID 23
2 1 Append path SubID 01
3 4 Sequence_Number LONG n starts at 0. Incremented for every point on path
7 END-7 - -
7 + END-7 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0xbf 0x4c 0x23 0x02

Packet Response 0xbf 0x4c 0x23 0x02

Clothoid Activate Clothoid Guidance Response

Clothoid Activate Clothoid Guidance Response
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 AutosteerCmdId ID bf
0 1 external device SubID 4c
1 1 Clothoid API SubID 23
2 1 Activate Clothoid Guidance SubID 02
3 1 Activate BYTE bools De/Activate using clothoids for guidance
4 END-4 - -
4 + END-4 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0xbf 0x4c 0x80 0x00

Packet Response 0xbf 0x4c 0x80 0x00

Autopilot External Device FFA HeartBeat Response

Updates the display with FFA status

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AutopilotCmdIdIDbf
01ExtDevSubIdSubID4c
11FfaIdSubID80
21FfaButtonPressIdSubID00
31VersionBYTEnVersion.
41SwathBYTEEnumWhat to do with the swath indicated below.
52RequestedSwathINTEGERnRequested swath.
71TurnProgressBYTEnPercentage of distance of turn that is completed, if currently turning.
81SignalBYTEEnumSignals that trigger certain actions on the display.
91SwathIndicatorsBYTEEnumStatus of the swath indicators.
101audibleAlertBYTEEnumAudible alert to be played.
111UIMessageBYTEEnumSpecial UI messages for FFA.
121ButtonNextSwathEnableBYTEBitsetStatus of the 'Enabled' button.
131ButtonTrueSwathEnableBYTEBitsetStatus of the 'True Swath' button.
141ButtonLeftBYTEBitsetStatus of the 'Left' button.
151ButtonRightBYTEBitsetStatus of the 'Right' button.
161ButtonSequenceEndOfRowBYTEBitsetStatus of the 'End of Row' sequence button.
171ButtonSequenceStartOfRowBYTEBitsetStatus of the 'Start of Row' sequence button.
181ButtonDismissBYTEBitsetStatus of the 'Dismiss' button.
191ButtonAutoTurnBYTEBitsetStatus of the 'Auto Turn at End of Row' button.
201ButtonFFAEngageBYTEn Bit 0 Whether the the button is visible, Bit 1 Whether the the button is enabled, Bit 2 Whether the button is checked, Bits 4 to 6: Enum, 0 gray, 1 red, 2 yellow, 3 green, 4 yellow-green
211ButtonAcceptLiabilityBYTEBitsetStatus of the 'AcceptLiability' button.
221ButtonDeclineLiabilityBYTEBitsetStatus of the 'Decline' button.
231ButtonTurnStartAutoBYTEBitset
241ButtonTurnStartManualBYTEBitsetStatus of the 'Decline' button.
251NumberOfEventsBYTEnNumber of events in this message. Length of the following field depends on the number and can be 0.
266 * NumberOfEventsSequenceEventsARRAY of SequenceEvent-List of upcoming events. This field might not exist if the number of events is 0, the size of the field depends on the number of events.
26 + 6 * NumberOfEventsEND-26 + 6 * NumberOfEvents--
26 + 6 * NumberOfEvents + END-26 + 6 * NumberOfEvents2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Swath: What to do with the swath indicated below.
ValueDescription
0No action.
1Highlight and send the swath.
2Switch/snap to the swath.
3Delete the selection, highlight nothing.
4Send the swath.
5Highlight the swath.

Signal: Signals that trigger certain actions on the display.
ValueDescription
0No beep.
1Stops coverage logging.
2Starts coverage logging.
3Marks the start of a manually initiated turn. Trigger reset of A/B extension distances on the display.

SwathIndicators: Status of the swath indicators.
ValueDescription
0-50 swaths left.
50Current swath.
10050 swath right.
0xFFNo indicator is active.

audibleAlert: Audible alert to be played.
ValueDescription
0No beep.
1Beep once to indicate that an event triggered.

UIMessage: Special UI messages for FFA.
ValueDescription
0No message.
1Generic Take control NOW! message.
2Warning that AP won't be able to start the turn because current speed is too high.
3Warning that the vehicle is going dangerously fast and AP might disengage soon.
4Cannot suggest auto turn because the user has not selected the next swath.

ButtonNextSwathEnable: Status of the 'Enabled' button.
BitDescription
Whether the the button is visible.
Whether the the button is enabled.
Whether the button is checked.

ButtonTrueSwathEnable: Status of the 'True Swath' button.
BitDescription
Whether the the button is visible.
Whether the the button is enabled.
Whether the button is checked.

ButtonLeft: Status of the 'Left' button.
BitDescription
Whether the the button is visible.
Whether the the button is enabled.
Whether the button is checked.

ButtonRight: Status of the 'Right' button.
BitDescription
Whether the the button is visible.
Whether the the button is enabled.
Whether the button is checked.

ButtonSequenceEndOfRow: Status of the 'End of Row' sequence button.
BitDescription
Whether the the button is visible.
Whether the the button is enabled.
Whether the button is checked.

ButtonSequenceStartOfRow: Status of the 'Start of Row' sequence button.
BitDescription
Whether the the button is visible.
Whether the the button is enabled.
Whether the button is checked.

ButtonDismiss: Status of the 'Dismiss' button.
BitDescription
Whether the the button is visible.
Whether the the button is enabled.
Whether the button is checked.

ButtonAutoTurn: Status of the 'Auto Turn at End of Row' button.
BitDescription
Whether the the button is visible.
Whether the the button is enabled.
Whether the button is checked.

ButtonAcceptLiability: Status of the 'AcceptLiability' button.
BitDescription
Whether the the button is visible.
Whether the the button is enabled.
Whether the button is checked.

ButtonDeclineLiability: Status of the 'Decline' button.
BitDescription
Whether the the button is visible.
Whether the the button is enabled.
Whether the button is checked.

ButtonTurnStartAuto: 
BitDescription
Whether the the button is visible.
Whether the the button is enabled.
Whether the button is checked.

ButtonTurnStartManual: Status of the 'Decline' button.
BitDescription
Whether the the button is visible.
Whether the the button is enabled.
Whether the button is checked.

SequenceEvent: An event.
Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
01Event1BYTEEnumType of event.
11CountdownSecondsBYTEnSeconds until event starts. @see EFFACountdownSecondsSpecialValues
24CountdownMetersSINGLE-Meters until events starts.

Event1: Type of event.
ValueDescription
0Nothing will happen.
1Start of turn (what exactly is the start?)
2End of turn (what exactly is the end?)
3Raise the implement.
4Lower the implement.
5Stop planting/spraying/seeding/...
6Start planting/spraying/seeding/...
7Change speed to turn speed.
8Change speed to turn speed.
9Trigger sequence A.
10Trigger sequence B.
11Autopilot commands the display to change swaths. Typically not shown to the user.
12Autopilot internal only. not shown to the user.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1AutosteerRespIdIDbf
01ExtDevSubIdSubID5c
11FieldComputerPageIdSubID01
21FieldComputerHeartbeatRespIdSubID01
31AutosteerVersionBYTEnAutosteer version (major*10 + minor)
41AutosteerStateBYTEEnumAutosteer state
51APIVersionBYTEnAPI version (major*10 + minor)
61SystemStateBYTEEnumSystem state
74WarningStateLONGnID of active warning
114NoAutoAllowedLONGnAutomatic mode is not allowed reason
151PositionFixQualityBYTEEnumReported position fix quality
161RoadingStatusBitsBYTEBitsetBit field for roading allowed status
171Reserved1BYTEnReserved byte 1
181Reserved2BYTEnReserved byte 2
19END-19--
19 + END-192ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

AutosteerState: Autosteer state
ValueDescription
0Unknown state
1Reserved state
2Ready to receive swath data
3Swath data is loaded

SystemState: System state
ValueDescription
0Unknown state
1Initializing state
2Ready for automatic mode
3Automatic mode engaged
4A fault has occurred

PositionFixQuality: Reported position fix quality
ValueDescription
0Unknown fix quality
1No position fix
2Autonomous
3Beacon (DGPS)
4RTK Float
5RTK Fixed
6WAAS (DGPS)
7OmniSTAR HP Converged
8OmniSTAR HP Unconverged
9OmniSTAR XP Converged
10OmniSTAR XP Unconverged
11OmniSTAR VBS (DGPS)
12External RTCM (DGPS)

RoadingStatusBits: Bit field for roading allowed status
BitDescription
Roading mode is enabled
reserved
Automatic mode is locked out

0xbf 0x68 0x00

Packet Response 0xbf 0x68 0x00

Inertial Nav Position/Attitude Packet

Roll/Pitch corrected Position and Attitude packet from the Inertial Nav feature.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1InertialNavRespIdIDbf
01PositioningIdSubID68
11NewPositionIdSubID00
28GPSSecondDOUBLE-GPS second (since Jan 5 1980)
101INSEngineIdBYTEnAn identifier uniquely indicating the INS engine used to generate the position/attitude data
111AntennaIdBYTEnAn identifier uniquely indicating the antenna the GPS position was generated from
121PositionEngineBYTEEnumIndicates the positioning engine the fix was generated from
131PositionTypeBYTEEnumThe type of position generated. This can also be used to determine the quality of the position.
144PositionFlagsLONGBitsetFlags indicating special qualities of the position
188LatitudeDOUBLE-Position Latitude
268LongitudeDOUBLE-Position Longitude
348HeightDOUBLE-Position Altitude
424EVelocitySINGLE-East component of ENU velocity
464NVelocitySINGLE-North component of ENU velocity
504UVelocitySINGLE-Up component of ENU velocity
541DirectionBYTEEnumIndicates the estimated direction of the vehicle
554IMUStatusLONGEnumIndicates the status of the IMU data used to calculate attitude
598YawDOUBLE-Attitude Yaw
678PitchDOUBLE-Attitude Pitch
758RollDOUBLE-Attitude Roll
838YawRateDOUBLE-Attitude Yaw Rate
918PitchRateDOUBLE-Attitude Pitch Rate
998RollRateDOUBLE-Attitude Roll Rate
1071NumSatellitesBYTEnNumber of satellites used to generate the position solution
1084HDOPSINGLE-The HDOP of the satellites used to generate the position
1124VDOPSINGLE-The VDOP of the satellites used to generate the position
1164ESigmaSINGLE-The easting error estimate of the satelites used to generate the position
1204NSigmaSINGLE-The northing error estimate of the satelites used to generate the position
1244USigmaSINGLE-The vertical error estimate of the satelites used to generate the position
1284SemiMajorSigmaSINGLE-Elipsoid error estimate semi-major axis
1324SemiMinorSigmaSINGLE-Elipsoid error estimate semi-minor axis
1364CorrectionAgeSINGLE-The age of corrections applied to the GPS solution
1401StationIDBYTEnID of base station
1418ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
1492ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

PositionEngine: Indicates the positioning engine the fix was generated from
ValueDescription
0The internal DGPS positioning engine
1The internal OmnistarHPXP positioning engine
2The internal RTK positioning engine
3An external positioning engine

PositionType: The type of position generated. This can also be used to determine the quality of the position.
ValueDescription
0An Autonomous GPS solution.
1A DGPS solution.
2An unconverged OmnistarHP/XP position.
3A converged OmnistarHP/XP position.
4A float RTK position.
5A fixed ambiguity RTK position.
6A position that was generated by the demo manager.
7A position that was generated by an unknown fix engine type.

PositionFlags: Flags indicating special qualities of the position
BitDescription
Indicates this position was the one used internally for the antenna
Indicates that the position has gone through some form of filtering or adjustiment

Direction: Indicates the estimated direction of the vehicle
ValueDescription
0Forward direction
1Direction estimate is not initialized
2Reverse direction

IMUStatus: Indicates the status of the IMU data used to calculate attitude
ValueDescription
0Valid data is being received from the IMU
256No data is being received from the IMU

0xbf 0x68 0x01

Packet Response 0xbf 0x68 0x01

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1InertialNavRespIdIDbf
01PositioningIdSubID68
11NoNewPositionIdSubID01
28GPSSecondDOUBLE-GPS second (since Jan 5 1980)

184IMUStatusLONGEnumIndicates the status of the IMU data used to calculate attitude

548PitchRateDOUBLE-Attitude Pitch Rate
628RollRateDOUBLE-Attitude Roll Rate
701NumSatellitesBYTEnNumber of satellites tracked
711StationIDBYTEnID of base station
728ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
802ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

PositionEngine: Indicates the positioning engine the fix was generated from
ValueDescription
0The internal DGPS positioning engine
1The internal OmnistarHPXP positioning engine
2The internal RTK positioning engine
3An external positioning engine

PositionFlags: Flags indicating special qualities of the no position
BitDescription
Indicates this position was the one used internally for the antenna
Indicates that the position has gone through some form of filtering or adjustiment

Direction: Indicates the estimated direction of the vehicle
ValueDescription
0Forward direction
1Direction estimate is not initialized
2Reverse direction

ValueDescription
0Valid data is being received from the IMU

34 1 AppCodeVersionDay BYTE n Application Code Version Day.

LineAcquisitionState: Line Acquisition State.
ValueDescription
0The XTE and/or Heading Error have been too large for too long to be 'online'
1The XTE and Heading Error have been less than the allowed value for at least 2 seconds

0xd5 0x00

Packet Response 0xd5 0x00

Satellite Constellation Report

Reports satellite constellation info. Note that multiple packets may be required to describe entire constellation.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1MultiSystemGNSSIdIDd5
01SatConstellationIdSubID00
11AntennaIdBYTEnID of antenna to which contellation report applies (0 = primary, 1 = secondary)
21NumPacketsBYTEnTotal number of packets being sent for this report
31SequenceIdBYTEnIndicates specific packet in report (1 - N)
42TotalNumSatsINTEGERnTotal number of satellites described in all packets of constellation report
62NumSatsUsedInFixINTEGERnNumber of satellites actually being used in current position fix (0 = no current fix)
82ConstellationFlagsINTEGERnBitfield that indicates various attributes of constellation [future]
101NumSatsInPacketBYTEnNumber of satellites described by this packet
1122 * NumSatsInPacketSatInfoArrayARRAY of SatInfo-Info for satellites in this packet
11 + 22 * NumSatsInPacket2ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

SatInfo: Info for a single satellite
Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
02SVIdINTEGERnID of satellite in GNSS system
21SystemBYTEEnumIndicates to which GNSS system this satellite belongs
34AzimuthSINGLEradiansAzimuth of satellite
74ElevationSINGLEradiansElevation of satellite
112SatFlagsINTEGERBitsetBitfield that indicates various attributes of satellite
139FreqBandArrayARRAY of FreqBandInfo-Type and SNR for up to three frequency bands

System: Indicates to which GNSS system this satellite belongs
ValueDescription
0GPS - Global Positioning System
1SBAS - Space Based Augmentation System (WAAS, EGNOS, etc.)
2GLONASS - Global Orbiting Navigational Satellite System
3Galileo Navigation Satellite System
4QZSS - Quasi-Zenith Satellite System
5BeiDou Navigation Satellite System
6Indian Regional Navigation Satellite System
7Mobile Satellite Services - RTX delivered via geo-stationary L-band satellites
8Total GNSS System Type

SatFlags: Bitfield that indicates various attributes of satellite
BitDescription
Receiver has acquired the signal from this satellite
Measurements from this satellite were used in current fix
Differential corrections are available for this satellite

FreqBandInfo: Type and SNR for a single frequency band
Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
01FreqBandBYTEEnumSpecific frequency band that is being tracked
12SNRINTEGER0.1 dBHzSignal to noise ratio in 1Hz bandwidth * 10 (divide by 10 to get value in dBHz)

FreqBand: Specific frequency band that is being tracked
ValueDescription
0GPS/GLONASS/Galileo/SBAS L1
1GPS/GLONASS L2
2GPS/SBAS L5/Galileo E5A
3Galileo E5B/BeiDou B2
4Galileo combined E5A/B - AltBOC
5Galileo E6
6BeiDou L1
7BeiDou E6
8BeiDou upper L1
9GLONASS G3 CDMA
10Terralite XPS
11L-band space from 1525 MHz to 1559 MHz
255Indicates that freq band slot is unused

0xd5 0x02

Packet Response 0xd5 0x02

Satellite System Selection Response

Report which satellite system is enabled/disabled

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1MultiSystemGNSSIdIDd5
01SatSystemSelectIDSubID02
12SatelliteSystemControlFlagINTEGERBitsetBitfield that indicates which satellite system is enabled/disabled, if bits are set then it means the satellite system is enabled, which bit to set corresponds to value in GNSSSystemType
32ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

SatelliteSystemControlFlag: Bitfield that indicates which satellite system is enabled/disabled, if bits are set then it means the satellite system is enabled, which bit to set corresponds to value in GNSSSystemType
BitDescription
0GPS - Global Positioning System
1SBAS - Space Based Augmentation System (WAAS, EGNOS, etc.)
2GLONASS - Global Orbiting Navigational Satellite System
3Galileo Navigation Satellite System
4QZSS - Quasi-Zenith Satellite System
5BeiDou Navigation Satellite System
6Indian Regional Navigation Satellite System
7Mobile Satellite Services - RTX delivered via geo-stationary L-band satellites

0xd6

Packet Ack 0xd6

TBIP Authenticated Command Ack/Nack

Acknowledges the success or failure of an authenticated command

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1TBIPAuthenticatedMessageIdIDd6
01DomainIdBYTEnThe authenticated message domain
11CmdIdBYTEnThe authenticated command in the domain
21ResultBYTEEnumThe result of the command being acknowledged
32ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Result: The result of the command being acknowledged
ValueDescription
0The command was successfully received
-1The command failed TBIP authentication

0xd7 0x00

Packet Ack 0xd7 0x00

Lock or Unlock Stinger Channels

Lock or Unlock all stinger channels. This packet is for diagnostic purposes only and may change without notice. Command Packet: 0xC7 0x00
Byte# Size Item Type Value/Units Description
- 1 StingerAppID ID d7
0 1 StingerSubId SubID 00
1 1 AcknowledgeChannelLockId BYTE n 0 if Unlock Channel Command was issued, else 1
2 2 Checksum INTEGER - Packet checksum

0xd7 0x01

Packet Ack 0xd7 0x01

Set RF Band and Filter Switches

Acknowledge RF Band and Filter Switches setting command.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1StingerIDd7
01StingerSubIdSubID01
11Band_SelectBYTEEnumSelect band filter to modify.
21Filter_SelectBYTEEnumSelect filter option.
32ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Band_Select: Select band filter to modify.
ValueDescription
0Select MSS Band filters
1Select L2 Band filters

Filter_Select: Select filter option.
ValueDescription
0Select automatic mode (not yet supported).
1Select full MSS / LG2 band.
2Select lower MSS or L2 (GPS only) band.

0xd7 0x01

Packet Response 0xd7 0x01

Return RF Band and Filter Switch setting for a signal.

Return the RF Band and Filter Switch setting for the signal requested.

Byte#SizeItemTypeValue/UnitsDescription
-1StingerIDd7
01StingerSubIdSubID01
11Band_SelectBYTEEnumSelect band filter to modify.
21Filter_SelectBYTEEnumSelect filter option.
310ReservedBYTE-Reserved for future use
132ChecksumINTEGER-Packet checksum

Band_Select: Select band filter to modify.
ValueDescription
0Select MSS Band filters
1Select L2 Band filters

Filter_Select: Select filter option.
ValueDescription
0Select automatic mode (not yet supported).
1Select full MSS / LG2 band.
2Select lower MSS or L2 (GPS only) band.
